}\\n320: \\n321: func (c *Controller) syncService(logger klog.Logger, key string) error {\\n322: \\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n323: \\tdefer func() {\\n324: \\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Finished syncing service endpoint slices\\\", \\\"key\\\", key, \\\"elapsedTime\\\", time.Since(startTime))\\n325: \\t}()\\n326: \\n327: \\tnamespace, name, err := cache.SplitMetaNamespaceKey(key)\\n328: \\tif err != nil {\\n329: \\t\\treturn err\\n330: \\t}\\n331: \\n332: \\tservice, err := c.serviceLister.Services(namespace).Get(name)\\n333: \\tif err != nil {\\n334: \\t\\tif !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {\\n335: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n336: \\t\\t}\\n337: \\n338: \\t\\tc.triggerTimeTracker.DeleteService(namespace, name)\\n339: \\t\\tc.reconciler.DeleteService(namespace, name)\\n340: \\t\\tc.endpointSliceTracker.DeleteService(namespace, name)\\n341: \\t\\t// The service has been deleted, return nil so that it won't be retried.\\n342: \\t\\treturn nil\\n343: \\t}\\n344: \\n345: \\tif service.Spec.Type == v1.ServiceTypeExternalName {\\n346: \\t\\t// services with Type ExternalName receive no endpoints from this controller;\\n347: \\t\\t// Ref: https://issues.k8s.io/105986\\n348: \\t\\treturn nil\\n349: \\t}\\n350: \\n351: \\tif service.Spec.Selector == nil {\\n352: \\t\\t// services without a selector receive no endpoint slices from this controller;\\n353: \\t\\t// these services will receive endpoint slices that are created out-of-band via the REST API.\\n354: \\t\\treturn nil\\n355: \\t}\\n356: \\n357: \\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"About to update endpoint slices for service\\\", \\\"key\\\", key)\\n358: \\n359: \\tpodLabelSelector := labels.Set(service.Spec.Selector).AsSelectorPreValidated()\\n360: \\tpods, err := c.podLister.Pods(service.Namespace).List(podLabelSelector)\\n361: \\tif err != nil {\\n362: \\t\\t// Since we're getting stuff from a local cache, it is basically\\n363: \\t\\t// impossible to get this error.\\n364: \\t\\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \\\"FailedToListPods\\\",\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Error listing Pods for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n366: \\t\\treturn err\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\n369: \\tesLabelSelector := labels.Set(map[string]string{\\n370: \\t\\tdiscovery.LabelServiceName: service.Name,\\n371: \\t\\tdiscovery.LabelManagedBy:   c.reconciler.GetControllerName(),\\n372: \\t}).AsSelectorPreValidated()\\n373: \\tendpointSlices, err := c.endpointSliceLister.EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).List(esLabelSelector)\\n374: \\n375: \\tif err != nil {\\n376: \\t\\t// Since we're getting stuff from a local cache, it is basically\\n377: \\t\\t// impossible to get this error.\\n378: \\t\\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \\\"FailedToListEndpointSlices\\\",\\n379: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Error listing Endpoint Slices for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n380: \\t\\treturn err\\n381: \\t}\\n382: \\n383: \\t// Drop EndpointSlices that have been marked for deletion to prevent the controller from getting stuck.\\n384: \\tendpointSlices = dropEndpointSlicesPendingDeletion(endpointSlices)\\n385: \\n386: \\tif c.endpointSliceTracker.StaleSlices(service, endpointSlices) {\\n387: \\t\\treturn endpointslicepkg.NewStaleInformerCache(\\\"EndpointSlice informer cache is out of date\\\")\\n388: \\t}\\n389: \\n390: \\t// We call ComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime here to make sure that the\\n391: \\t// state of the trigger time tracker gets updated even if the sync turns out\\n392: \\t// to be no-op and we don't update the EndpointSlice objects.\\n393: \\tlastChangeTriggerTime := c.triggerTimeTracker.\\n394: \\t\\tComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime(namespace, service, pods)\\n395: \\n396: \\terr = c.reconciler.Reconcile(logger, service, pods, endpointSlices, lastChangeTriggerTime)\\n397: \\tif err != nil {\\n398: \\t\\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \\\"FailedToUpdateEndpointSlices\\\",\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Error updating Endpoint Slices for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n400: \\t\\treturn err\\n401: \\t}\\n402: \\n403: \\treturn nil\\n404: }\\n405: \\n406: // onServiceUpdate updates the Service Selector in the cache and queues the Service for processing.\\n407: func (c *Controller) onServiceUpdate(obj interface{}) {\\n408: \\tkey, err := controller.KeyFunc(obj)\\n409: \\tif err != nil {\\n410: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Couldn't get key for object %+v: %v\\\", obj, err))\\n411: \\t\\treturn\\n412: \\t}\\n413: \\n414: \\tc.queue.Add(key)\\n415: }\\n416: \\n417: // onServiceDelete removes the Service Selector from the cache and queues the Service for processing.\\n418: func (c *Controller) onServiceDelete(obj interface{}) {\\n419: \\tkey, err := controller.KeyFunc(obj)\\n420: \\tif err != nil {\\n421: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Couldn't get key for object %+v: %v\\\", obj, err))\\n422: \\t\\treturn\\n423: \\t}\\n424: \\n425: \\tc.queue.Add(key)\\n426: }\\n427: \\n428: // onEndpointSliceAdd queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if the\\n429: // EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\\n430: // endpointSliceTracker.\\n431: func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceAdd(obj interface{}) {\\n432: \\tendpointSlice := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\\n433: \\tif endpointSlice == nil {\\n434: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Invalid EndpointSlice provided to onEndpointSliceAdd()\\\"))\\n435: \\t\\treturn\\n436: \\t}\\n437: \\tif c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.ShouldSync(endpointSlice) {\\n438: \\t\\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\\n439: \\t}\\n440: }\\n441: \\n442: // onEndpointSliceUpdate queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if\\n443: // the EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\\n444: // endpointSliceTracker or the managed-by value of the EndpointSlice has changed\\n445: // from or to this controller.\\n446: func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceUpdate(logger klog.Logger, prevObj, obj interface{}) {\\n447: \\tprevEndpointSlice := prevObj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\\n448: \\tendpointSlice := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\\n449: \\tif endpointSlice == nil || prevEndpointSlice == nil {\\n450: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Invalid EndpointSlice provided to onEndpointSliceUpdate()\\\"))\\n451: \", \"url\": !ok {\\\\n113: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\\\\n114: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n115: \\\\n116: \\\\t\\\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\\\\n117: \\\\t}\\\\n118: \\\\n119: \\\\t// reconcile for existing.\\\\n120: \\\\tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\\\\n121: \\\\t\\\\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\\\\n122: \\\\t\\\\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\\\\n123: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n124: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\terrs = append(errs, err)\\\\n125: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n126: \\\\t}\\\\n127: \\\\n128: \\\\t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\\\\n129: \\\\t// by the service\\\\n130: \\\\tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\\\\n131: \\\\t\\\\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\\\\n132: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n133: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\\\\\\\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\\\\n134: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n135: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\\\\n136: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\\\\\"delete\\\\\\\").Inc()\\\\n137: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n138: \\\\t}\\\\n139: \\\\n140: \\\\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\\\\n141: }\\\\n142: \\\\n143: // reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\\\n144: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\\\n145: // slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\\\n146: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\\\n147: func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time, addressType discovery.AddressType) error {\\\\n148: \\\\terrs := []error{}\\\\n149: \\\\n150: \\\\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\\\n151: \\\\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\\\n152: \\\\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\\\n153: \\\\tevents := []*topologycache.EventBuilder{}\\\\n154: \\\\n155: \\\\t// Build data structures for existing state.\\\\n156: \\\\texistingSlicesByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey][]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\\\n157: \\\\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\\\n158: \\\\t\\\\tif ownedBy(existingSlice, service) {\\\\n159: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(existingSlice.Ports)\\\\n160: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\texistingSlicesByPortMap[epHash] = append(existingSlicesByPortMap[epHash], existingSlice)\\\\n161: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n162: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\\\n163: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n164: \\\\t}\\\\n165: \\\\n166: \\\\t// Build data structures for desired state.\\\\n167: \\\\tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\\\\n168: \\\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\\\n169: \\\\n170: \\\\tfor _, pod := range pods {\\\\n171: \\\\t\\\\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\\\\n172: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n173: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n174: \\\\n175: \\\\t\\\\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\\\\n176: \\\\t\\\\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\\\\n177: \\\\t\\\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\\\n178: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\\\n179: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n180: \\\\n181: \\\\t\\\\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\\\n182: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = \\\\u0026endpointMeta{\\\\n183: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\taddressType: addressType,\\\\n184: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tports:       endpointPorts,\\\\n185: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n186: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n187: \\\\n188: \\\\t\\\\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\\\\n189: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n190: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\\\\n191: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\\\\n192: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\\\\n193: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn err\\\\n194: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n195: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\\\\n196: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\\\\n197: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\\\\n198: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\\\\n199: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// stuck forever.\\\\n200: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\\\\n201: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\\\\n202: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\\\\n203: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlogger.Info(\\\\\\\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"pod\\\\\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\\\\\"service\\\\\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\\\\\"node\\\\\\\", klog.KRef(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\\\\n204: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\\\\\\\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\\\\n205: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n206: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n207: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n208: \\\\t\\\\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\\\\n209: \\\\t\\\\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n210: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(\\\\u0026endpoint)\\\\n211: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n212: \\\\t}\\\\n213: \\\\n214: \\\\tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\\\\n215: \\\\ttotalAdded := 0\\\\n216: \\\\ttotalRemoved := 0\\\\n217: \\\\n218: \\\\t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\\\\n219: \\\\tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\\\\n220: \\\\t\\\\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\\\\n221: \\\\t\\\\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\\\\n222: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlogger, service, existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap], desiredEndpoints, desiredMetaByPortMap[portMap])\\\\n223: \\\\n224: \\\\t\\\\ttotalAdded += added\\\\n225: \\\\t\\\\ttotalRemoved += removed\\\\n226: \\\\n227: \\\\t\\\\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\\\\n228: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\\\\n229: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\\\\n230: \\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n231: \\\\n232: \\\\t\\\\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\\\\n233: \\\\t\\\\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToUpdate...)\\\\n234: \\\\t\\\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\\\\n235: \\\\t}\\\\n236: \\\\n237: \\\\t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\\\\n238: \\\\t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\\\\n239: \\\\tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\\\\n240: \\\\t\\\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\\\\n241: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\\\\n242: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n243: \\\\t}\\\\n244: \\\\n245: \\\\t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\\\\n246: \\\\tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 len(slicesToCreate) \\\\u003c 1 {\\\\n247: \\\\t\\\\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\\\\n248: \\\\t\\\\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, \\\\u0026endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\\\\n249: \\\\t\\\\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], topology cache\\\", \\\"existingSlice\\\", existingSlice, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n102: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n103: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(svcKey, existingSlice.AddressType)\\n104: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n105: \\t\\t\\t}\\n106: \\n107: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\n108: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n109: \\t\\t}\\n110: \\n111: \\t\\t// add list if it is not on our map\\n112: \\t\\tif _, ok := slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType]; !ok {\\n113: \\t\\t\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\\n114: \\t\\t}\\n115: \\n116: \\t\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\\n117: \\t}\\n118: \\n119: \\t// reconcile for existing.\\n120: \\tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\\n121: \\t\\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\\n122: \\t\\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\\n123: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n124: \\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, err)\\n125: \\t\\t}\\n126: \\t}\\n127: \\n128: \\t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\\n129: \\t// by the service\\n130: \\tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\\n131: \\t\\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\\n132: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n133: \\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\\\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\\n134: \\t\\t} else {\\n135: \\t\\t\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\\n136: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\"delete\\\").Inc()\\n137: \\t\\t}\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\n140: \\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\\n141: }\\n142: \\n143: // reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\n144: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\n145: // slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\n146: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\n147: func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time, addressType discovery.AddressType) error {\\n148: \\terrs := []error{}\\n149: \\n150: \\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n151: \\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n152: \\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n153: \\tevents := []*topologycache.EventBuilder{}\\n154: \\n155: \\t// Build data structures for existing state.\\n156: \\texistingSlicesByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey][]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n157: \\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\n158: \\t\\tif ownedBy(existingSlice, service) {\\n159: \\t\\t\\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(existingSlice.Ports)\\n160: \\t\\t\\texistingSlicesByPortMap[epHash] = append(existingSlicesByPortMap[epHash], existingSlice)\\n161: \\t\\t} else {\\n162: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\n163: \\t\\t}\\n164: \\t}\\n165: \\n166: \\t// Build data structures for desired state.\\n167: \\tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\\n168: \\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\n169: \\n170: \\tfor _, pod := range pods {\\n171: \\t\\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\\n172: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n173: \\t\\t}\\n174: \\n175: \\t\\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\\n176: \\t\\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\\n177: \\t\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\n178: \\t\\t\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\n179: \\t\\t}\\n180: \\n181: \\t\\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\n182: \\t\\t\\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\\n183: \\t\\t\\t\\taddressType: addressType,\\n184: \\t\\t\\t\\tports:       endpointPorts,\\n185: \\t\\t\\t}\\n186: \\t\\t}\\n187: \\n188: \\t\\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\\n189: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n190: \\t\\t\\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\\n191: \\t\\t\\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\\n192: \\t\\t\\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\\n193: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n194: \\t\\t\\t}\\n195: \\t\\t\\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\\n196: \\t\\t\\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\\n197: \\t\\t\\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\\n198: \\t\\t\\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\\n199: \\t\\t\\t// stuck forever.\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\\n201: \\t\\t\\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\\n202: \\t\\t\\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\\n203: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"node\\\", klog.KRef(\\\"\\\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\\\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\\n205: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n206: \\t\\t\\t}\\n207: \\t\\t}\\n208: \\t\\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\\n209: \\t\\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\\n210: \\t\\t\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\\n211: \\t\\t}\\n212: \\t}\\n213: \\n214: \\tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\\n215: \\ttotalAdded := 0\\n216: \\ttotalRemoved := 0\\n217: \\n218: \\t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\\n219: \\tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\\n220: \\t\\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\\n221: \\t\\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\\n222: \\t\\t\\tlogger, service, existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap], desiredEndpoints, desiredMetaByPortMap[portMap])\\n223: \\n224: \\t\\ttotalAdded += added\\n225: \\t\\ttotalRemoved += removed\\n226: \\n227: \\t\\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\\n228: \\t\\t\\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\\n229: \\t\\t\\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\\n230: \\t\\t})\\n231: \\n232: \\t\\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\\n233: \\t\\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToUpdate...)\\n234: \\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\n237: \\t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\\n238: \\t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\\n239: \\tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\\n240: \\t\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\\n241: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\\n242: \\t\\t}\\n243: \\t}\\n244: \\n245: \\t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\\n246: \\tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) && len(slicesToCreate) < 1 {\\n247: \\t\\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\\n248: \\t\\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, &endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\\n249: \\t\\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 && placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], placeholderSlice) {\\n250: \\t\\t\\t// We are about to unnecessarily delete/recreate the placeholder, remove it now.\\n251: \", \"url\": We need to make sure NoScheduleNoTraffic does NOT trigger eviction. Looking at the code, the eviction is triggered by NoExecute taints specifically, so we just need to make sure we don't add NoScheduleNoTraffic to any eviction logic.\n\nLet me look at the node lifecycle controller more carefully to understand how it handles NoExecute.\n\nActually, looking at the code:\n- The scheduler's `DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc` filters taints that prevent scheduling - we need to add `NoScheduleNoTraffic` here\n- The endpoint slice reconciler needs to check if a node has `NoScheduleNoTraffic` taint and skip pods on that node\n- The node lifecycle controller handles eviction for `NoExecute` - we should NOT add `NoScheduleNoTraffic` there\n\nFor the endpoint slice, the key is in `reconcileByAddressType` in `staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go`. After getting the node (line 188), we need to check if the node has a `NoScheduleNoTraffic` taint and skip the pod if so.\n\nLet me also check the `pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go` to see if there's a better place.\n\nActually, looking at the reconciler code more carefully:\n- Line 188: `node, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)`\n- Line 208: `endpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)`\n- Line 209-211: Add endpoint to desired set\n\nThe best place to add the NoScheduleNoTraffic check is after getting the node (line 188), before adding the endpoint. We check if the node has a NoScheduleNoTraffic taint and skip the pod.\n\nNow let me also check the `pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go` to see if there's a node-level check there too.\n\nLooking at the controller, it passes pods to the reconciler. The reconciler then gets the node for each pod. So the right place is in the reconciler.\n\nLet me now implement all the changes:\n\n1. Add constant to `staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go`\n2. Add constant to `pkg/apis/core/types.go`\n3. Update validation in `pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go`\n4. Update validation in `pkg/util/taints/taints.go`\n5. Update validation in `staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go`\n6. Update scheduler filter in `pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go`\n7. For example, the form `\\\\u003ckey\\\\u003e` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\\\\n91: func ParseTaints(spec []string) ([]v1.Taint, []v1.Taint, error) {\\\\n92: \\\\tvar taints, taintsToRemove []v1.Taint\\\\n93: \\\\tuniqueTaints := map[v1.TaintEffect]sets.String{}\\\\n94: \\\\n95: \\\\tfor _, taintSpec := range spec {\\\\n96: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/util/taints/taints.go%3FL75-95\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"75: \\ttaint.Value = value\\n76: \\ttaint.Effect = effect\\n77: \\n78: \\treturn taint, nil\\n79: }\\n80: \\n81: func validateTaintEffect(effect v1.TaintEffect) error {\\n82: \\tif effect != v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoExecute {\\n83: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\\\", effect)\\n84: \\t}\\n85: \\n86: \\treturn nil\\n87: }\\n88: \\n89: // ParseTaints takes a spec which is an array and creates slices for new taints to be added, taints to be deleted.\\n90: // It also validates the spec. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\\\\n3520: \\\\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\\\\n3521: \\\\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\\\\n3522: \\\\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\\\n3523: \\\\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\\\\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\\\\\"\\\\n3524: \\\\n3525: \\\\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\\\n3526: \\\\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\\\n3527: \\\\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\\\\\"NoExecute\\\\\\\"\\\\n3528: )\\\\n3529: \\\\n3530: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\\\\n3531: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3506-3530\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"3506: // +enum\\n3507: type TaintEffect string\\n3508: \\n3509: const (\\n3510: \\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\\n3511: \\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\\n3512: \\t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\\n3513: \\t// Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3514: \\tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"NoSchedule\\\"\\n3515: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\\n3516: \\t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\\n3517: \\t// onto the node entirely. {
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "/*\nCopyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage helper\n\nimport v1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\n// DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n// filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\nfunc DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule, NoScheduleNoTraffic and NoExecute taints.\n\t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic\n\t}\n}\n",
  "path": "/workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"File created successfully at: /workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go"

Input

{
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "/*\nCopyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage helper\n\nimport (\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n)\n\nfunc TestDoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\ttaint    *v1.Taint\n\t\texpected bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"should include the taints with NoSchedule effect\",\n\t\t\ttaint: &v1.Taint{\n\t\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpected: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"should include the taints with NoExecute effect\",\n\t\t\ttaint: &v1.Taint{\n\t\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoExecute,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpected: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"should not include the taints with PreferNoSchedule effect\",\n\t\t\ttaint: &v1.Taint{\n\t\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"should include the taints with NoScheduleNoTraffic effect\",\n\t\t\ttaint: &v1.Taint{\n\t\t\t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpected: true,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfilterPredicate := DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc()\n\n\tfor i := range tests {\n\t\ttest := tests[i]\n\t\tt.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tif got := filterPredicate(test.taint); got != test.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"unexpected result, expected %v but got %v\", test.expected, got)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n",
  "path": "/workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint_test.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"File created successfully at: /workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint_test.go"

unsupported taint effect\\\", effect)\\n84: \\t}\\n85: \\n86: \\treturn nil\\n87: }\\n88: \\n89: // ParseTaints takes a spec which is an array and creates slices for new taints to be added, taints to "{\"meta\": null, \"content\": [{\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"{\\\"content\\\":\\\"90: // It also validates the spec. For example, the form `\\\\u003ckey\\\\u003e` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\\\\n91: func ParseTaints(spec []string) ([]v1.Taint, []v1.Taint, error) {\\\\n92: \\\\tvar taints, taintsToRemove []v1.Taint\\\\n93: \\\\tuniqueTaints := map[v1.TaintEffect]sets.String{}\\\\n94: \\\\n95: \\\\tfor _, taintSpec := range spec {\\\\n96: \\\\t\\\\tif strings.HasSuffix(taintSpec, \\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\") {\\\\n97: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttaintToRemove, err := parseTaint(strings.TrimSuffix(taintSpec, \\\\\\\"-\\\\\\\"))\\\\n98: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n99: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn nil, nil, err\\\\n100: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n101: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, v1.Taint{Key: taintToRemove.Key, Effect: taintToRemove.Effect})\\\\n102: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n103: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewTaint, err := parseTaint(taintSpec)\\\\n104: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n105: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn nil, nil, err\\\\n106: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n107: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// validate that the taint has an effect, which is required to add the taint\\\\n108: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif len(newTaint.Effect) == 0 {\\\\n109: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"invalid taint spec: %v\\\\\\\", taintSpec)\\\\n110: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n111: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// validate if taint is unique by \\\\u003ckey, effect\\\\u003e\\\\n112: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) \\\\u003e 0 \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Has(newTaint.Key) {\\\\n113: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"duplicated taints with the same key and effect: %v\\\\\\\", newTaint)\\\\n114: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n115: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// add taint to existingTaints for uniqueness check\\\\n116: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) == 0 {\\\\n117: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect] = sets.String{}\\\\n118: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n119: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Insert(newTaint.Key)\\\\n120: \\\\n121: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttaints = append(taints, newTaint)\\\\n122: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n123: \\\\t}\\\\n124: \\\\treturn taints, taintsToRemove, nil\\\\n125: }\\\\n126: \\\\n127: // CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\\\\n128: func CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []v1.Taint, taints []v1.Taint) string {\\\\n129: \\\\tvar existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\\\\n130: \\\\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\\\n131: \\\\t\\\\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\\\\n132: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif taint.Key == oldTaint.Key \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\\\\n133: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\texistingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, taint.Key)\\\\n134: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n135: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n136: \\\\t}\\\\n137: \\\\treturn strings.Join(existingTaintList, \\\\\\\",\\\\\\\")\\\\n138: }\\\\n139: \\\\n140: // DeleteTaintsByKey removes all the taints that have the same key to given taintKey\\\\n141: func DeleteTaintsByKey(taints []v1.Taint, taintKey string) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\\\\n142: \\\\tnewTaints := []v1.Taint{}\\\\n143: \\\\tdeleted := false\\\\n144: \\\\tfor i := range taints {\\\\n145: \\\\t\\\\tif taintKey == taints[i].Key {\\\\n146: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdeleted = true\\\\n147: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n148: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n149: \\\\t\\\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\\\\n150: \\\\t}\\\\n151: \\\\treturn newTaints, deleted\\\\n152: }\\\\n153: \\\\n154: // DeleteTaint removes all the taints that have the same key and effect to given taintToDelete.\\\\n155: func DeleteTaint(taints []v1.Taint, taintToDelete *v1.Taint) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\\\\n156: \\\\tnewTaints := []v1.Taint{}\\\\n157: \\\\tdeleted := false\\\\n158: \\\\tfor i := range taints {\\\\n159: \\\\t\\\\tif taintToDelete.MatchTaint(\\\\u0026taints[i]) {\\\\n160: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdeleted = true\\\\n161: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n162: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n163: \\\\t\\\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\\\\n164: \\\\t}\\\\n165: \\\\treturn newTaints, deleted\\\\n166: }\\\\n167: \\\\n168: // RemoveTaint tries to remove a taint from annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\\\\n169: // false otherwise.\\\\n170: func RemoveTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\\\\n171: \\\\tnewNode := node.DeepCopy()\\\\n172: \\\\tnodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\\\\n173: \\\\tif len(nodeTaints) == 0 {\\\\n174: \\\\t\\\\treturn newNode, false, nil\\\\n175: \\\\t}\\\\n176: \\\\n177: \\\\tif !TaintExists(nodeTaints, taint) {\\\\n178: \\\\t\\\\treturn newNode, false, nil\\\\n179: \\\\t}\\\\n180: \\\\n181: \\\\tnewTaints, _ := DeleteTaint(nodeTaints, taint)\\\\n182: \\\\tnewNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\\\\n183: \\\\treturn newNode, true, nil\\\\n184: }\\\\n185: \\\\n186: // AddOrUpdateTaint tries to add a taint to annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\\\\n187: // false otherwise.\\\\n188: func AddOrUpdateTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\\\\n189: \\\\tnewNode := node.DeepCopy()\\\\n190: \\\\tnodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\\\\n191: \\\\n192: \\\\tvar newTaints []v1.Taint\\\\n193: \\\\tupdated := false\\\\n194: \\\\tfor i := range nodeTaints {\\\\n195: \\\\t\\\\tif taint.MatchTaint(\\\\u0026nodeTaints[i]) {\\\\n196: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif helper.Semantic.DeepEqual(*taint, nodeTaints[i]) {\\\\n197: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn newNode, false, nil\\\\n198: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n199: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\\\\n200: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tupdated = true\\\\n201: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n202: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n203: \\\\n204: \\\\t\\\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, nodeTaints[i])\\\\n205: \\\\t}\\\\n206: \\\\n207: \\\\tif !updated {\\\\n208: \\\\t\\\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\\\\n209: \\\\t}\\\\n210: \\\\n211: \\\\tnewNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\\\\n212: \\\\treturn newNode, true, nil\\\\n213: }\\\\n214: \\\\n215: // TaintExists checks if the given taint exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\\\\n216: func TaintExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintToFind *v1.Taint) bool {\\\\n217: \\\\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\\\n218: \\\\t\\\\tif taint.MatchTaint(taintToFind) {\\\\n219: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn true\\\\n220: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n221: \\\\t}\\\\n222: \\\\treturn false\\\\n223: }\\\\n224: \\\\n225: // TaintKeyExists checks if the given taint key exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\\\\n226: func TaintKeyExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintKeyToMatch string) bool {\\\\n227: \\\\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\\\n228: \\\\t\\\\tif taint.Key == taintKeyToMatch {\\\\n229: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn true\\\\n230: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n231: \\\\t}\\\\n232: \\\\treturn false\\\\n233: }\\\\n234: \\\\n235: // TaintSetDiff finds the difference between two taint slices and\\\\n236: // returns all new and removed elements of the new slice relative to the old slice.\\\\n237: // for example:\\\\n238: // input: taintsNew=[a b] taintsOld=[a c]\\\\n239: // output: taintsToAdd=[b] taintsToRemove=[c]\\\\n240: func TaintSetDiff(taintsNew, taintsOld []v1.Taint) (taintsToAdd []*v1.Taint, taintsToRemove []*v1.Taint) {\\\\n241: \\\\tfor _, taint := range taintsNew {\\\\n242: \\\\t\\\\tif !TaintExists(taintsOld, \\\\u0026taint) {\\\\n243: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tt := taint\\\\n244: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttaintsToAdd = append(taintsToAdd, \\\\u0026t)\\\\n245: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n246: \\\\t}\\\\n247: \\\\n248: \\\\tfor _, taint := range taintsOld {\\\\n249: \\\\t\\\\tif !TaintExists(taintsNew, \\\\u0026taint) {\\\\n250: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tt := taint\\\\n251: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, \\\\u0026t)\\\\n252: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n253: \\\\t}\\\\n254: \\\\n255: \\\\treturn\\\\n256: }\\\\n257: \\\\n258: // TaintSetFilter filters from the taint slice according to the passed fn function to get the filtered taint slice.\\\\n259: func TaintSetFilter(taints []v1.Taint, fn func(*v1.Taint) bool) []v1.Taint {\\\\n260: \\\\tres := []v1.Taint{}\\\\n261: \\\\n262: \\\\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\\\n263: \\\\t\\\\tif fn(\\\\u0026taint) {\\\\n264: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tres = append(res, taint)\\\\n265: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n266: \\\\t}\\\\n267: \\\\n268: \\\\treturn res\\\\n269: }\\\\n270: \\\\n271: // CheckTaintValidation checks if the given taint is valid.\\\\n272: // Returns error if the given taint is invalid.\\\\n273: func CheckTaintValidation(taint v1.Taint) error {\\\\n274: \\\\tif errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(taint.Key); len(errs) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n275: \\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"invalid taint key: %s\\\\\\\", strings.Join(errs, \\\\\\\"; \\\\\\\"))\\\\n276: \\\\t}\\\\n277: \\\\tif taint.Value != \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\" {\\\\n278: \\\\t\\\\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(taint.Value); len(errs) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n279: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"invalid taint value: %s\\\\\\\", strings.Join(errs, \\\\\\\"; \\\\\\\"))\\\\n280: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n281: \\\\t}\\\\n282: \\\\tif taint.Effect != \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\" {\\\\n283: \\\\t\\\\tif err := validateTaintEffect(taint.Effect); err != nil {\\\\n284: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn err\\\\n285: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n286: \\\\t}\\\\n287: \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"90: // It also validates the spec. For example, the form `<key>` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\\n91: func ParseTaints(spec []string) ([]v1.Taint, []v1.Taint, error) {\\n92: \\tvar taints, taintsToRemove []v1.Taint\\n93: \\tuniqueTaints := map[v1.TaintEffect]sets.String{}\\n94: \\n95: \\tfor _, taintSpec := range spec {\\n96: \\t\\tif strings.HasSuffix(taintSpec, \\\"-\\\") {\\n97: \\t\\t\\ttaintToRemove, err := parseTaint(strings.TrimSuffix(taintSpec, \\\"-\\\"))\\n98: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n99: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n100: \\t\\t\\t}\\n101: \\t\\t\\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, v1.Taint{Key: taintToRemove.Key, Effect: taintToRemove.Effect})\\n102: \\t\\t} else {\\n103: \\t\\t\\tnewTaint, err := parseTaint(taintSpec)\\n104: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n105: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n106: \\t\\t\\t}\\n107: \\t\\t\\t// validate that the taint has an effect, which is required to add the taint\\n108: \\t\\t\\tif len(newTaint.Effect) == 0 {\\n109: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid taint spec: %v\\\", taintSpec)\\n110: \\t\\t\\t}\\n111: \\t\\t\\t// validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\\n112: \\t\\t\\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Has(newTaint.Key) {\\n113: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"duplicated taints with the same key and effect: %v\\\", newTaint)\\n114: \\t\\t\\t}\\n115: \\t\\t\\t// add taint to existingTaints for uniqueness check\\n116: \\t\\t\\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) == 0 {\\n117: \\t\\t\\t\\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect] = sets.String{}\\n118: \\t\\t\\t}\\n119: \\t\\t\\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Insert(newTaint.Key)\\n120: \\n121: \\t\\t\\ttaints = append(taints, newTaint)\\n122: \\t\\t}\\n123: \\t}\\n124: \\treturn taints, taintsToRemove, nil\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: // CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\\n128: func CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []v1.Taint, taints []v1.Taint) string {\\n129: \\tvar existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\\n130: \\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\n131: \\t\\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\\n132: \\t\\t\\tif taint.Key == oldTaint.Key && taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\\n133: \\t\\t\\t\\texistingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, taint.Key)\\n134: \\t\\t\\t}\\n135: \\t\\t}\\n136: \\t}\\n137: \\treturn strings.Join(existingTaintList, \\\",\\\")\\n138: }\\n139: \\n140: // DeleteTaintsByKey removes all the taints that have the same key to given taintKey\\n141: func DeleteTaintsByKey(taints []v1.Taint, taintKey string) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\\n142: \\tnewTaints := []v1.Taint{}\\n143: \\tdeleted := false\\n144: \\tfor i := range taints {\\n145: \\t\\tif taintKey == taints[i].Key {\\n146: \\t\\t\\tdeleted = true\\n147: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n148: \\t\\t}\\n149: \\t\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\\n150: \\t}\\n151: \\treturn newTaints, deleted\\n152: }\\n153: \\n154: // DeleteTaint removes all the taints that have the same key and effect to given taintToDelete.\\n155: func DeleteTaint(taints []v1.Taint, taintToDelete *v1.Taint) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\\n156: \\tnewTaints := []v1.Taint{}\\n157: \\tdeleted := false\\n158: \\tfor i := range taints {\\n159: \\t\\tif taintToDelete.MatchTaint(&taints[i]) {\\n160: \\t\\t\\tdeleted = true\\n161: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n162: \\t\\t}\\n163: \\t\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\\n164: \\t}\\n165: \\treturn newTaints, deleted\\n166: }\\n167: \\n168: // RemoveTaint tries to remove a taint from annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\\n169: // false otherwise.\\n170: func RemoveTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\\n171: \\tnewNode := node.DeepCopy()\\n172: \\tnodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\\n173: \\tif len(nodeTaints) == 0 {\\n174: \\t\\treturn newNode, false, nil\\n175: \\t}\\n176: \\n177: \\tif !TaintExists(nodeTaints, taint) {\\n178: \\t\\treturn newNode, false, nil\\n179: \\t}\\n180: \\n181: \\tnewTaints, _ := DeleteTaint(nodeTaints, taint)\\n182: \\tnewNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\\n183: \\treturn newNode, true, nil\\n184: }\\n185: \\n186: // AddOrUpdateTaint tries to add a taint to annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\\n187: // false otherwise.\\n188: func AddOrUpdateTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\\n189: \\tnewNode := node.DeepCopy()\\n190: \\tnodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\\n191: \\n192: \\tvar newTaints []v1.Taint\\n193: \\tupdated := false\\n194: \\tfor i := range nodeTaints {\\n195: \\t\\tif taint.MatchTaint(&nodeTaints[i]) {\\n196: \\t\\t\\tif helper.Semantic.DeepEqual(*taint, nodeTaints[i]) {\\n197: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn newNode, false, nil\\n198: \\t\\t\\t}\\n199: \\t\\t\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\\n200: \\t\\t\\tupdated = true\\n201: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n202: \\t\\t}\\n203: \\n204: \\t\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, nodeTaints[i])\\n205: \\t}\\n206: \\n207: \\tif !updated {\\n208: \\t\\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\\n209: \\t}\\n210: \\n211: \\tnewNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\\n212: \\treturn newNode, true, nil\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // TaintExists checks if the given taint exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\\n216: func TaintExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintToFind *v1.Taint) bool {\\n217: \\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\n218: \\t\\tif taint.MatchTaint(taintToFind) {\\n219: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n220: \\t\\t}\\n221: \\t}\\n222: \\treturn false\\n223: }\\n224: \\n225: // TaintKeyExists checks if the given taint key exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\\n226: func TaintKeyExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintKeyToMatch string) bool {\\n227: \\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\n228: \\t\\tif taint.Key == taintKeyToMatch {\\n229: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n230: \\t\\t}\\n231: \\t}\\n232: \\treturn false\\n233: }\\n234: \\n235: // TaintSetDiff finds the difference between two taint slices and\\n236: // returns all new and removed elements of the new slice relative to the old slice.\\n237: // for example:\\n238: // input: taintsNew=[a b] taintsOld=[a c]\\n239: // output: taintsToAdd=[b] taintsToRemove=[c]\\n240: func TaintSetDiff(taintsNew, taintsOld []v1.Taint) (taintsToAdd []*v1.Taint, taintsToRemove []*v1.Taint) {\\n241: \\tfor _, taint := range taintsNew {\\n242: \\t\\tif !TaintExists(taintsOld, &taint) {\\n243: \\t\\t\\tt := taint\\n244: \\t\\t\\ttaintsToAdd = append(taintsToAdd, &t)\\n245: \\t\\t}\\n246: \\t}\\n247: \\n248: \\tfor _, taint := range taintsOld {\\n249: \\t\\tif !TaintExists(taintsNew, &taint) {\\n250: \\t\\t\\tt := taint\\n251: \\t\\t\\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, &t)\\n252: \\t\\t}\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\n255: \\treturn\\n256: }\\n257: \\n258: // TaintSetFilter filters from the taint slice according to the passed fn function to get the filtered taint slice.\\n259: func TaintSetFilter(taints []v1.Taint, fn func(*v1.Taint) bool) []v1.Taint {\\n260: \\tres := []v1.Taint{}\\n261: \\n262: \\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\n263: \\t\\tif fn(&taint) {\\n264: \\t\\t\\tres = append(res, taint)\\n265: \\t\\t}\\n266: \\t}\\n267: \\n268: \\treturn res\\n269: }\\n270: \\n271: // CheckTaintValidation checks if the given taint is valid.\\n272: // Returns error if the given taint is invalid.\\n273: func CheckTaintValidation(taint v1.Taint) error {\\n274: \\tif errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(taint.Key); len(errs) > 0 {\\n275: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid taint key: %s\\\", strings.Join(errs, \\\"; \\\"))\\n276: \\t}\\n277: \\tif taint.Value != \\\"\\\" {\\n278: \\t\\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(taint.Value); len(errs) > 0 {\\n279: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid taint value: %s\\\", strings.Join(errs, \\\"; \\\"))\\n280: \\t\\t}\\n281: \\t}\\n282: \\tif taint.Effect != \\\"\\\" {\\n283: \\t\\tif err := validateTaintEffect(taint.Effect); err != nil {\\n284: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n285: \\t\\t}\\n286: \\t}\\n287: \\n288: \\treturn nil\\n289: }\\n290: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/util/taints/taints.go%3FL90-300\"}, \"isError\": false}"

'\\\\u003ckey\\\\u003e=\\\\u003cvalue\\\\u003e:\\\\u003ceffect\\\\u003e', '\\\\u003ckey\\\\u003e:\\\\u003ceffect\\\\u003e', or '\\\\u003ckey\\\\u003e'.\\\\n77: func parseTaint(st string) \\\\n109: \\\\tif errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(key); len(errs) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n110: \\\\t\\\\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\\\\\\\", st, strings.Join(errs, \\\\\\\"; \\\\\\\"))\\\\n111: \\\\t}\\\\n112: \\\\n113: \\\\ttaint.Key = key\\\\n114: \\\\ttaint.Value = value\\\\n115: \\\\ttaint.Effect = effect\\\\n116: \\\\n117: \\\\treturn taint, nil\\\\n118: }\\\\n119: \\\\n120: func validateTaintEffect(effect corev1.TaintEffect) error {\\\\n121: \\\\tif effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoSchedule \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 effect != corev1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoExecute {\\\\n122: \\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\\\\\\\", effect)\\\\n123: \\\\t}\\\\n124: \\\\n125: \\\\treturn nil\\\\n126: }\\\\n127: \\\\n128: // reorganizeTaints returns the updated set of taints, taking into account old taints that were not updated,\\\\n129: // old taints that were For example, the form `<key>` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\\n39: func parseTaints(spec []string) ([]corev1.Taint, []corev1.Taint, error) {\\n40: \\tvar taints, taintsToRemove 42. str_replace_editor @ 2026-03-11T08:05:28.811324

Input

{
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\n// package taints implements utilities for working with taints\npackage taints\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/helper\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tMODIFIED  = \"modified\"\n\tTAINTED   = \"tainted\"\n\tUNTAINTED = \"untainted\"\n)\n\n// parseTaint parses a taint from a string, whose form must be either\n// '<key>=<value>:<effect>', '<key>:<effect>', or '<key>'.\nfunc parseTaint(st string) (v1.Taint, error) {\n\tvar taint v1.Taint\n\n\tvar key string\n\tvar value string\n\tvar effect v1.TaintEffect\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(st, \":\")\n\tswitch len(parts) {\n\tcase 1:\n\t\tkey = parts[0]\n\tcase 2:\n\t\teffect = v1.TaintEffect(parts[1])\n\t\tif err := validateTaintEffect(effect); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn taint, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpartsKV := strings.Split(parts[0], \"=\")\n\t\tif len(partsKV) > 2 {\n\t\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey = partsKV[0]\n\t\tif len(partsKV) == 2 {\n\t\t\tvalue = partsKV[1]\n\t\t\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(value); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n\t}\n\n\tif errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(key); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t}\n\n\ttaint.Key = key\n\ttaint.Value = value\n\ttaint.Effect = effect\n\n\treturn taint, nil\n}\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect v1.TaintEffect) error {\n\tif effect != v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoExecute && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ParseTaints takes a spec which is an array and creates slices for new taints to be added, taints to be deleted.\n// It also validates the spec. For example, the form `<key>` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\nfunc ParseTaints(spec []string) ([]v1.Taint, []v1.Taint, error) {\n\tvar taints, taintsToRemove []v1.Taint\n\tuniqueTaints := map[v1.TaintEffect]sets.String{}\n\n\tfor _, taintSpec := range spec {\n\t\tif strings.HasSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\") {\n\t\t\ttaintToRemove, err := parseTaint(strings.TrimSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\"))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, v1.Taint{Key: taintToRemove.Key, Effect: taintToRemove.Effect})\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnewTaint, err := parseTaint(taintSpec)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// validate that the taint has an effect, which is required to add the taint\n\t\t\tif len(newTaint.Effect) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", taintSpec)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\n\t\t\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Has(newTaint.Key) {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"duplicated taints with the same key and effect: %v\", newTaint)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// add taint to existingTaints for uniqueness check\n\t\t\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect] = sets.String{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Insert(newTaint.Key)\n\n\t\t\ttaints = append(taints, newTaint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn taints, taintsToRemove, nil\n}\n\n// CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\nfunc CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []v1.Taint, taints []v1.Taint) string {\n\tvar existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n\t\t\tif taint.Key == oldTaint.Key && taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\n\t\t\t\texistingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, taint.Key)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(existingTaintList, \",\")\n}\n\n// DeleteTaintsByKey removes all the taints that have the same key to given taintKey\nfunc DeleteTaintsByKey(taints []v1.Taint, taintKey string) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\n\tnewTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n\tdeleted := false\n\tfor i := range taints {\n\t\tif taintKey == taints[i].Key {\n\t\t\tdeleted = true\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n\t}\n\treturn newTaints, deleted\n}\n\n// DeleteTaint removes all the taints that have the same key and effect to given taintToDelete.\nfunc DeleteTaint(taints []v1.Taint, taintToDelete *v1.Taint) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\n\tnewTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n\tdeleted := false\n\tfor i := range taints {\n\t\tif taintToDelete.MatchTaint(&taints[i]) {\n\t\t\tdeleted = true\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n\t}\n\treturn newTaints, deleted\n}\n\n// RemoveTaint tries to remove a taint from annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\n// false otherwise.\nfunc RemoveTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\n\tnewNode := node.DeepCopy()\n\tnodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\n\tif len(nodeTaints) == 0 {\n\t\treturn newNode, false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif !TaintExists(nodeTaints, taint) {\n\t\treturn newNode, false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tnewTaints, _ := DeleteTaint(nodeTaints, taint)\n\tnewNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\n\treturn newNode, true, nil\n}\n\n// AddOrUpdateTaint tries to add a taint to annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\n// false otherwise.\nfunc AddOrUpdateTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\n\tnewNode := node.DeepCopy()\n\tnodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\n\n\tvar newTaints []v1.Taint\n\tupdated := false\n\tfor i := range nodeTaints {\n\t\tif taint.MatchTaint(&nodeTaints[i]) {\n\t\t\tif helper.Semantic.DeepEqual(*taint, nodeTaints[i]) {\n\t\t\t\treturn newNode, false, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\n\t\t\tupdated = true\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, nodeTaints[i])\n\t}\n\n\tif !updated {\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\n\t}\n\n\tnewNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\n\treturn newNode, true, nil\n}\n\n// TaintExists checks if the given taint exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\nfunc TaintExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintToFind *v1.Taint) bool {\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tif taint.MatchTaint(taintToFind) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// TaintKeyExists checks if the given taint key exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\nfunc TaintKeyExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintKeyToMatch string) bool {\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tif taint.Key == taintKeyToMatch {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// TaintSetDiff finds the difference between two taint slices and\n// returns all new and removed elements of the new slice relative to the old slice.\n// for example:\n// input: taintsNew=[a b] taintsOld=[a c]\n// output: taintsToAdd=[b] taintsToRemove=[c]\nfunc TaintSetDiff(taintsNew, taintsOld []v1.Taint) (taintsToAdd []*v1.Taint, taintsToRemove []*v1.Taint) {\n\tfor _, taint := range taintsNew {\n\t\tif !TaintExists(taintsOld, &taint) {\n\t\t\tt := taint\n\t\t\ttaintsToAdd = append(taintsToAdd, &t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, taint := range taintsOld {\n\t\tif !TaintExists(taintsNew, &taint) {\n\t\t\tt := taint\n\t\t\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, &t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn\n}\n\n// TaintSetFilter filters from the taint slice according to the passed fn function to get the filtered taint slice.\nfunc TaintSetFilter(taints []v1.Taint, fn func(*v1.Taint) bool) []v1.Taint {\n\tres := []v1.Taint{}\n\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tif fn(&taint) {\n\t\t\tres = append(res, taint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn res\n}\n\n// CheckTaintValidation checks if the given taint is valid.\n// Returns error if the given taint is invalid.\nfunc CheckTaintValidation(taint v1.Taint) error {\n\tif errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(taint.Key); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint key: %s\", strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t}\n\tif taint.Value != \"\" {\n\t\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(taint.Value); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint value: %s\", strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif taint.Effect != \"\" {\n\t\tif err := validateTaintEffect(taint.Effect); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n",
  "path": "/workspace/pkg/util/taints/taints.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

*[]corev1.Taint) bool {\\\\n164: \\\\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\\\\n165: \\\\t\\\\texistsInNew := false\\\\n166: \\\\t\\\\tfor _, taint := range *newTaints {\\\\n167: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !existsInNew {\\\\n173: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t*newTaints = append(*newTaints, oldTaint)\\\\n174: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n175: \\\\t}\\\\n176: \\\\treturn len(oldTaints) != len(*newTaints)\\\\n177: }\\\\n178: oldTaints {\\\\n184: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif taint.Key == oldTaint.Key \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\\\\n185: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\texistingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, != len(*newTaints)\\n177: }\\n178: \\n179: // checkIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\\n180: func checkIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []corev1.Taint, taints []corev1.Taint) string {\\n181: \\tvar existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\\n182: \\tfor _, taint := range taints {\\n183: \\t\\tfor _, {
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "/*\nCopyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\n// Package taints implements utilites for working with taints\npackage taint\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation\"\n)\n\n// Exported taint constant strings\nconst (\n\tMODIFIED  = \"modified\"\n\tTAINTED   = \"tainted\"\n\tUNTAINTED = \"untainted\"\n)\n\n// parseTaints takes a spec which is an array and creates slices for new taints to be added, taints to be deleted.\n// It also validates the spec. For example, the form `<key>` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\nfunc parseTaints(spec []string) ([]corev1.Taint, []corev1.Taint, error) {\n\tvar taints, taintsToRemove []corev1.Taint\n\tuniqueTaints := map[corev1.TaintEffect]sets.String{}\n\n\tfor _, taintSpec := range spec {\n\t\tif strings.HasSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\") {\n\t\t\ttaintToRemove, err := parseTaint(strings.TrimSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\"))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttaintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, corev1.Taint{Key: taintToRemove.Key, Effect: taintToRemove.Effect})\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnewTaint, err := parseTaint(taintSpec)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// validate that the taint has an effect, which is required to add the taint\n\t\t\tif len(newTaint.Effect) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", taintSpec)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\n\t\t\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Has(newTaint.Key) {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"duplicated taints with the same key and effect: %v\", newTaint)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// add taint to existingTaints for uniqueness check\n\t\t\tif len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect] = sets.String{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tuniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Insert(newTaint.Key)\n\n\t\t\ttaints = append(taints, newTaint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn taints, taintsToRemove, nil\n}\n\n// parseTaint parses a taint from a string, whose form must be either\n// '<key>=<value>:<effect>', '<key>:<effect>', or '<key>'.\nfunc parseTaint(st string) (corev1.Taint, error) {\n\tvar taint corev1.Taint\n\n\tvar key string\n\tvar value string\n\tvar effect corev1.TaintEffect\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(st, \":\")\n\tswitch len(parts) {\n\tcase 1:\n\t\tkey = parts[0]\n\tcase 2:\n\t\teffect = corev1.TaintEffect(parts[1])\n\t\tif err := validateTaintEffect(effect); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn taint, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpartsKV := strings.Split(parts[0], \"=\")\n\t\tif len(partsKV) > 2 {\n\t\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n\t\t}\n\t\tkey = partsKV[0]\n\t\tif len(partsKV) == 2 {\n\t\t\tvalue = partsKV[1]\n\t\t\tif errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(value); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n\t}\n\n\tif errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(key); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\treturn taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n\t}\n\n\ttaint.Key = key\n\ttaint.Value = value\n\ttaint.Effect = effect\n\n\treturn taint, nil\n}\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect corev1.TaintEffect) error {\n\tif effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoExecute && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// reorganizeTaints returns the updated set of taints, taking into account old taints that were not updated,\n// old taints that were updated, old taints that were deleted, and new taints.\nfunc reorganizeTaints(node *corev1.Node, overwrite bool, taintsToAdd []corev1.Taint, taintsToRemove []corev1.Taint) (string, []corev1.Taint, error) {\n\tnewTaints := append([]corev1.Taint{}, taintsToAdd...)\n\toldTaints := node.Spec.Taints\n\t// add taints that already existing but not updated to newTaints\n\tadded := addTaints(oldTaints, &newTaints)\n\tallErrs, deleted := deleteTaints(taintsToRemove, &newTaints)\n\tif (added && deleted) || overwrite {\n\t\treturn MODIFIED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n\t} else if added {\n\t\treturn TAINTED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n\t}\n\treturn UNTAINTED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n}\n\n// deleteTaints deletes the given taints from the node's taintlist.\nfunc deleteTaints(taintsToRemove []corev1.Taint, newTaints *[]corev1.Taint) ([]error, bool) {\n\tallErrs := []error{}\n\tvar removed bool\n\tfor _, taintToRemove := range taintsToRemove {\n\t\tif len(taintToRemove.Effect) > 0 {\n\t\t\t*newTaints, removed = deleteTaint(*newTaints, &taintToRemove)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t*newTaints, removed = deleteTaintsByKey(*newTaints, taintToRemove.Key)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !removed {\n\t\t\tallErrs = append(allErrs, fmt.Errorf(\"taint %q not found\", taintToRemove.ToString()))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn allErrs, removed\n}\n\n// addTaints adds the newTaints list to existing ones and updates the newTaints List.\n// TODO: This needs a rewrite to take only the new values instead of appended newTaints list to be consistent.\nfunc addTaints(oldTaints []corev1.Taint, newTaints *[]corev1.Taint) bool {\n\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n\t\texistsInNew := false\n\t\tfor _, taint := range *newTaints {\n\t\t\tif taint.MatchTaint(&oldTaint) {\n\t\t\t\texistsInNew = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !existsInNew {\n\t\t\t*newTaints = append(*newTaints, oldTaint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn len(oldTaints) != len(*newTaints)\n}\n\n// checkIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\nfunc checkIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []corev1.Taint, taints []corev1.Taint) string {\n\tvar existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tfor _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n\t\t\tif taint.Key == oldTaint.Key && taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\n\t\t\t\texistingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, taint.Key)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(existingTaintList, \",\")\n}\n\n// deleteTaintsByKey removes all the taints that have the same key to given taintKey\nfunc deleteTaintsByKey(taints []corev1.Taint, taintKey string) ([]corev1.Taint, bool) {\n\tnewTaints := []corev1.Taint{}\n\tfor i := range taints {\n\t\tif taintKey == taints[i].Key {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n\t}\n\treturn newTaints, len(taints) != len(newTaints)\n}\n\n// deleteTaint removes all the taints that have the same key and effect to given taintToDelete.\nfunc deleteTaint(taints []corev1.Taint, taintToDelete *corev1.Taint) ([]corev1.Taint, bool) {\n\tnewTaints := []corev1.Taint{}\n\tfor i := range taints {\n\t\tif taintToDelete.MatchTaint(&taints[i]) {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n\t}\n\treturn newTaints, len(taints) != len(newTaints)\n}\n",
  "path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Checks if the changes implement the required feature\n\nSo I need to create files in the workspace that represent the modified versions. For large files, I need to read them fully and create modified versions.\n\nLet me be strategic about which files I need to modify:\n1. `staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go` - LARGE file, need to add constant\n2. `pkg/apis/core/types.go` - LARGE file, need to add constant\n3. `pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go` - VERY LARGE file, need to update validateTaintEffect\n4. `pkg/util/taints/taints.go` - DONE (small file)\n5. `staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go` - DONE (small file)\n6. `pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go` - DONE (small file)\n7. `pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint_test.go` - DONE (small file)\n8. `staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go` - MEDIUM file, need to add taint check\n\nFor the large files, I need to read them in chunks. Let me do this efficiently.\n\nFor types.go files, I only need to add a few lines. Let me read just the relevant sections and create the full files.\n\nActually, I realize I should use a smarter approach. Let me check if the workspace has any existing files that I can use as a base.\n\nThe workspace is empty (just has config.toml and .git). So I need to create all files from scratch.\n\nFor large files, I'll read them in chunks and create the full modified versions. Let me start with the types.go files since they're critical.\n"
}

existingSlice)\\\\n161: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n162: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\\\n163: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n164: \\\\t}\\\\n165: \\\\n166: \\\\t// Build data structures for desired state.\\\\n167: \\\\tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\\\\n168: \\\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\\\n169: \\\\n170: \\\\tfor _, pod := range pods {\\\\n171: \\\\t\\\\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\\\\n172: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n173: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n174: \\\\n175: \\\\t\\\\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\\\\n176: \\\\t\\\\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\\\\n177: \\\\t\\\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\\\n178: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\\\n179: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n180: \\\\n181: \\\\t\\\\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\\\n182: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = \\\\u0026endpointMeta{\\\\n183: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\taddressType: addressType,\\\\n184: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tports:       endpointPorts,\\\\n185: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n186: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n187: \\\\n188: \\\\t\\\\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\\\\n189: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n190: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\\\\n191: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\\\\n192: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\\\\n193: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn err\\\\n194: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n195: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\\\\n196: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\\\\n197: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\\\\n198: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\\\\n199: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// stuck forever.\\\\n200: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\\\\n201: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\\\\n202: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\\\\n203: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlogger.Info(\\\\\\\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"pod\\\\\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\\\\\"service\\\\\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\\\\\"node\\\\\\\", klog.KRef(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\\\\n204: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\\\\\\\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\\\\n205: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n206: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n207: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n208: \\\\t\\\\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\\\\n209: \\\\t\\\\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) \\\\u003e 0 {\\\\n210: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(\\\\u0026endpoint)\\\\n211: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n212: \\\\t}\\\\n213: \\\\n214: \\\\tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\\\\n215: \\\\ttotalAdded := 0\\\\n216: \\\\ttotalRemoved := 0\\\\n217: \\\\n218: \\\\t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\\\\n219: \\\\tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\\\\n220: \\\\t\\\\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\\\\n221: \\\\t\\\\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\\\\n222: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlogger, service, existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap], desiredEndpoints, desiredMetaByPortMap[portMap])\\\\n223: \\\\n224: \\\\t\\\\ttotalAdded += added\\\\n225: \\\\t\\\\ttotalRemoved += removed\\\\n226: \\\\n227: \\\\t\\\\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\\\\n228: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\\\\n229: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\\\\n230: \\\\t\\\\t})\\\\n231: \\\\n232: \\\\t\\\\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\\\\n233: \\\\t\\\\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToUpdate...)\\\\n234: \\\\t\\\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\\\\n235: \\\\t}\\\\n236: \\\\n237: \\\\t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\\\\n238: \\\\t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\\\\n239: \\\\tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\\\\n240: \\\\t\\\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\\\\n241: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\\\\n242: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n243: \\\\t}\\\\n244: \\\\n245: \\\\t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\\\\n246: \\\\tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 len(slicesToCreate) \\\\u003c 1 {\\\\n247: \\\\t\\\\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\\\\n248: \\\\t\\\\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, \\\\u0026endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\\\\n249: \\\\t\\\\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], placeholderSlice) {\\\\n250: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// We are about to unnecessarily delete/recreate the placeholder, remove it now.\\\\n251: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go%3FL160-250\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"160: \\t\\t\\texistingSlicesByPortMap[epHash] = append(existingSlicesByPortMap[epHash], existingSlice)\\n161: \\t\\t} else {\\n162: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\n163: \\t\\t}\\n164: \\t}\\n165: \\n166: \\t// Build data structures for desired state.\\n167: \\tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\\n168: \\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\n169: \\n170: \\tfor _, pod := range pods {\\n171: \\t\\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\\n172: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n173: \\t\\t}\\n174: \\n175: \\t\\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\\n176: \\t\\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\\n177: \\t\\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\n178: \\t\\t\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\\n179: \\t\\t}\\n180: \\n181: \\t\\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\\n182: \\t\\t\\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\\n183: !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\\n193: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n194: \\t\\t\\t}\\n195: \\t\\t\\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\\n196: \\t\\t\\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\\n197: \\t\\t\\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\\n198: \\t\\t\\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\\n199: \\t\\t\\t// stuck forever.\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\\n201: \\t\\t\\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\\n202: \\t\\t\\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\\n203: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"node\\\", klog.KRef(\\\"\\\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\\\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\\n205: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n206: \\t\\t\\t}\\n207: \\t\\t}\\n208: \\t\\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\\n209: \\t\\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\\n210: \\t\\t\\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\\n211: \\t\\t}\\n212: \\t}\\n213: \\n214: \\tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\\n215: \\ttotalAdded := 0\\n216: \\ttotalRemoved := 0\\n217: \\n218: \\t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\\n219: \\tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\\n220: \\t\\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\\n221: \\t\\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\\n222:
