\\n227: \\t\\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\\n228: \\t\\t\\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\\n229: \\t\\t\\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\\n230: \\t\\t})\\n231: \\n232: \\t\\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\\n233: \\t\\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, placeholder.\\n246: \\tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) && len(slicesToCreate) < 1 {\\n247: \\t\\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\\n248: \\t\\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, &endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\\n249: \\t\\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 && "{\"meta\": null, \"content\": [{\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"{\\\"content\\\":\\\"50: \\\\tmetricsCache         *metrics.Cache\\\\n51: \\\\t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\\\\n52: \\\\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\\\\n53: \\\\ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\\\\n54: \\\\t// trafficDistributionEnabled determines if endpointDistribution field is to\\\\n55: \\\\t// be considered when reconciling EndpointSlice hints.\\\\n56: \\\\ttrafficDistributionEnabled bool\\\\n57: \\\\t// eventRecorder allows Reconciler to record and publish events.\\\\n58: \\\\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\\\\n59: \\\\tcontrollerName string\\\\n60: }\\\\n61: \\\\n62: type ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\\\\n63: \\\\n64: // WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\\\\n65: // `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\\\\n66: func WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\\\\n67: \\\\treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\\\\n68: \\\\t\\\\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\\\\n69: \\\\t}\\\\n70: }\\\\n71: \\\\n72: // endpointMeta includes the attributes we group slices on, this type helps with\\\\n73: // that logic in Reconciler\\\\n74: type endpointMeta struct {\\\\n75: \\\\tports       []discovery.EndpointPort\\\\n76: \\\\taddressType discovery.AddressType\\\\n77: }\\\\n78: \\\\n79: // Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\\\n80: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\\\n81: // slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\\\n82: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\\\n83: func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\\\\n84: \\\\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\\\\n85: \\\\terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\\\\n86: \\\\tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\\\\n87: \\\\n88: \\\\t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\\\\n89: \\\\tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\\\\n90: \\\\n91: \\\\t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\\\\n92: \\\\t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\\\\n93: \\\\t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\\\\n94: \\\\t// for further adjustment\\\\n95: \\\\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\\\n96: \\\\t\\\\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\\\\n97: \\\\t\\\\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\\\\n98: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\\\\n99: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\\\\n100: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n101: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tlogger.Info(\\\\\\\"Couldn't get key to remove EndpointSlice from topology cache\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"existingSlice\\\\\\\", existingSlice, \\\\\\\"err\\\\\\\", err)\\\\n102: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n103: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(svcKey, existingSlice.AddressType)\\\\n104: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n105: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n106: \\\\n107: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\\\\n108: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcontinue\\\\n109: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n110: \\\\n111: \\\\t\\\\t// add list if it is not on our map\\\\n112: \\\\t\\\\tif _, ok := slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType]; !ok {\\\\n113: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\\\\n114: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n115: \\\\n116: \\\\t\\\\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\\\\n117: \\\\t}\\\\n118: \\\\n119: \\\\t// reconcile for existing.\\\\n120: \\\\tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\\\\n121: \\\\t\\\\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\\\\n122: \\\\t\\\\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\\\\n123: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n124: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\terrs = append(errs, err)\\\\n125: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n126: \\\\t}\\\\n127: \\\\n128: \\\\t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\\\\n129: \\\\t// by the service\\\\n130: \\\\tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\\\\n131: \\\\t\\\\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\\\\n132: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n133: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\\\\\\\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\\\\n134: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n135: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\\\\n136: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\\\\\"delete\\\\\\\").Inc()\\\\n137: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n138: \\\\t}\\\\n139: \\\\n140: \\\\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\\\\n141: }\\\\n142: \\\\n143: // reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\\\\n144: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\\\n145: // slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\\\n146: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\\\n147: func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time, addressType discovery.AddressType) error {\\\\n148: \\\\terrs := []error{}\\\\n149: \\\\n150: \\\\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\\\n151: \\\\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\\\n152: \\\\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\\\n153: \\\\tevents := []*topologycache.EventBuilder{}\\\\n154: \\\\n155: \\\\t// Build data structures for existing state.\\\\n156: \\\\texistingSlicesByPortMap := "{\"meta\": null, \"content\": [{\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"{\\\"content\\\":\\\"400: \\\\t\\\\t// The AddressType field is immutable. Since Services also consider\\\\n401: \\\\t\\\\t// IPFamily immutable, the only case where this should matter will be\\\\n402: \\\\t\\\\t// the migration from IP to IPv4 and IPv6 AddressTypes, where there's a\\\\n403: \\\\t\\\\t// chance EndpointSlices with an IP AddressType would otherwise be\\\\n404: \\\\t\\\\t// updated to IPv4 or IPv6 without this check.\\\\n405: \\\\t\\\\tif sliceToDelete.AddressType == slice.AddressType \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 ownedBy(sliceToDelete, service) {\\\\n406: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslice.Name = sliceToDelete.Name\\\\n407: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToCreate = slicesToCreate[:len(slicesToCreate)-1]\\\\n408: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, slice)\\\\n409: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete[:i], slicesToDelete[i+1:]...)\\\\n410: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n411: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\ti++\\\\n412: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n413: \\\\t}\\\\n414: \\\\n415: \\\\t// Don't create new EndpointSlices if the Service is pending deletion. This\\\\n416: \\\\t// is to avoid a potential race condition with the garbage collector where\\\\n417: \\\\t// it tries to delete EndpointSlices as this controller replaces them.\\\\n418: \\\\tif service.DeletionTimestamp == nil {\\\\n419: \\\\t\\\\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToCreate {\\\\n420: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\taddTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice, triggerTime)\\\\n421: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tcreatedSlice, err := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Create(context.TODO(), endpointSlice, metav1.CreateOptions{})\\\\n422: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n423: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// If the namespace is terminating, creates will continue to fail. Simply drop the item.\\\\n424: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif errors.HasStatusCause(err, corev1.NamespaceTerminatingCause) {\\\\n425: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn nil\\\\n426: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n427: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"failed to create EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\\\\\\\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\\\n428: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n429: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.Update(createdSlice)\\\\n430: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\\\\\"create\\\\\\\").Inc()\\\\n431: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n432: \\\\t}\\\\n433: \\\\n434: \\\\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToUpdate {\\\\n435: \\\\t\\\\taddTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice, triggerTime)\\\\n436: \\\\t\\\\tupdatedSlice, err := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Update(context.TODO(), endpointSlice, metav1.UpdateOptions{})\\\\n437: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n438: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"failed to update %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\\\\\\\", endpointSlice.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\\\n439: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n440: \\\\t\\\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.Update(updatedSlice)\\\\n441: \\\\t\\\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\\\\\"update\\\\\\\").Inc()\\\\n442: \\\\t}\\\\n443: \\\\n444: \\\\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToDelete {\\\\n445: \\\\t\\\\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), endpointSlice.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\\\\n446: \\\\t\\\\tif err != nil {\\\\n447: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\\\\\"failed to delete %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\\\\\\\", endpointSlice.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\\\n448: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n449: \\\\t\\\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(endpointSlice)\\\\n450: \\\\t\\\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\\\\\"delete\\\\\\\").Inc()\\\\n451: \\\\t}\\\\n452: \\\\n453: \\\\ttopologyLabel := \\\\\\\"Disabled\\\\\\\"\\\\n454: \\\\tif r.topologyCache != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\\\\n455: \\\\t\\\\ttopologyLabel = \\\\\\\"Auto\\\\\\\"\\\\n456: \\\\t}\\\\n457: \\\\tvar trafficDistribution string\\\\n458: \\\\tif r.trafficDistributionEnabled \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 !hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\\\\n459: \\\\t\\\\tif service.Spec.TrafficDistribution != nil \\\\u0026\\\\u0026 *service.Spec.TrafficDistribution == corev1.ServiceTrafficDistributionPreferClose {\\\\n460: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\ttrafficDistribution = *service.Spec.TrafficDistribution\\\\n461: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n462: \\\\t}\\\\n463: \\\\n464: \\\\tnumSlicesChanged := len(slicesToCreate) + len(slicesToUpdate) + len(slicesToDelete)\\\\n465: \\\\tmetrics.EndpointSlicesChangedPerSync.WithLabelValues(topologyLabel, trafficDistribution).Observe(float64(numSlicesChanged))\\\\n466: \\\\n467: \\\\treturn nil\\\\n468: }\\\\n469: \\\\n470: // reconcileByPortMapping compares the endpoints found in existing slices with\\\\n471: // the list of desired endpoints and returns lists of slices to create, update,\\\\n472: // and delete. It also checks that the slices mirror the parent services labels.\\\\n473: // The logic is split up into several main steps:\\\\n474: //  1. Iterate through existing slices, delete endpoints that are no longer\\\\n475: //     desired and update matching endpoints that have changed. It also checks\\\\n476: //     if the slices have the labels of the parent services, and updates them if not.\\\\n477: //  2. Iterate through slices that have been modified in 1 and fill them up with\\\\n478: //     any remaining desired endpoints.\\\\n479: //  3. If there still desired endpoints left, try to fit them into a previously\\\\n480: //     unchanged slice and/or create new ones.\\\\n481: func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByPortMapping(\\\\n482: \\\\tlogger klog.Logger,\\\\n483: \\\\tservice *corev1.Service,\\\\n484: \\\\texistingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice,\\\\n485: \\\\tdesiredSet endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet,\\\\n486: \\\\tendpointMeta *endpointMeta,\\\\n487: ) ([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, []*discovery.EndpointSlice, []*discovery.EndpointSlice, int, int) {\\\\n488: \\\\tslicesByName := map[string]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\\\n489: \\\\tsliceNamesUnchanged := sets.New[string]()\\\\n490: \\\\tsliceNamesToUpdate := sets.New[string]()\\\\n491: \\\\tsliceNamesToDelete := sets.New[string]()\\\\n492: \\\\tnumRemoved := 0\\\\n493: \\\\n494: \\\\t// 1. Iterate through existing slices to delete endpoints no longer desired\\\\n495: \\\\t//    and update endpoints that have changed\\\\n496: \\\\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\\\n497: \\\\t\\\\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = existingSlice\\\\n498: \\\\t\\\\tnewEndpoints := []discovery.Endpoint{}\\\\n499: \\\\t\\\\tendpointUpdated := false\\\\n500: \\\\t\\\\tfor _, endpoint := range existingSlice.Endpoints {\\\\n501: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tgot := desiredSet.Get(\\\\u0026endpoint)\\\\n502: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// If endpoint is desired add it to list of endpoints to keep.\\\\n503: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif got != nil {\\\\n504: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnewEndpoints = append(newEndpoints, *got)\\\\n505: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// If existing version of endpoint doesn't match desired version\\\\n506: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// set endpointUpdated to ensure endpoint changes are persisted.\\\\n507: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif !endpointsliceutil.EndpointsEqualBeyondHash(got, \\\\u0026endpoint) {\\\\n508: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tendpointUpdated = true\\\\n509: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n510: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// once an endpoint has been placed/found in a slice, it no\\\\n511: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// longer needs to be handled\\\\n512: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tdesiredSet.Delete(\\\\u0026endpoint)\\\\n513: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n514: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n515: \\\\n516: \\\\t\\\\t// generate the slice labels and check if parent labels have changed\\\\n517: \\\\t\\\\tlabels, labelsChanged := setEndpointSliceLabels(logger, existingSlice, service, r.controllerName)\\\\n518: \\\\n519: \\\\t\\\\t// If an endpoint was updated or removed, mark for update or delete\\\\n520: \\\\t\\\\tif endpointUpdated || len(existingSlice.Endpoints) != len(newEndpoints) {\\\\n521: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif len(existingSlice.Endpoints) \\\\u003e len(newEndpoints) {\\\\n522: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tnumRemoved += len(existingSlice.Endpoints) - len(newEndpoints)\\\\n523: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n524: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tif len(newEndpoints) == 0 {\\\\n525: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// if no endpoints desired in this slice, mark for deletion\\\\n526: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tsliceNamesToDelete.Insert(existingSlice.Name)\\\\n527: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n528: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// otherwise, copy and mark for update\\\\n529: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tepSlice := existingSlice.DeepCopy()\\\\n530: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tepSlice.Endpoints = newEndpoints\\\\n531: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tepSlice.Labels = labels\\\\n532: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = epSlice\\\\n533: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(epSlice.Name)\\\\n534: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n535: \\\\t\\\\t} else if labelsChanged {\\\\n536: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// if labels have changed, copy and mark for update\\\\n537: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tepSlice := existingSlice.DeepCopy()\\\\n538: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tepSlice.Labels = labels\\\\n539: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = epSlice\\\\n540: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(epSlice.Name)\\\\n541: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n542: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\t// slices with no changes will be useful if there are leftover endpoints\\\\n543: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tsliceNamesUnchanged.Insert(existingSlice.Name)\\\\n544: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n545: \\\\t}\\\\n546: \\\\n547: \\\\tnumAdded := desiredSet.Len()\\\\n548: \\\\n549: \\\\t// 2. If we still have desired endpoints to add and slices marked for update,\\\\n550: \\\\t//    iterate through the slices and fill them up with the desired endpoints.\\\\n551: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go%3FL400-550\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"400: \\t\\t// The AddressType field is immutable. Since Services also consider\\n401: \\t\\t// IPFamily immutable, the only case where this should matter will be\\n402: \\t\\t// the migration from IP to IPv4 and IPv6 AddressTypes, where there's a\\n403: \\t\\t// chance EndpointSlices with an IP AddressType would otherwise be\\n404: \\t\\t// updated to IPv4 or IPv6 without this check.\\n405: \\t\\tif sliceToDelete.AddressType == slice.AddressType && ownedBy(sliceToDelete, service) {\\n406: \\t\\t\\tslice.Name = sliceToDelete.Name\\n407: \\t\\t\\tslicesToCreate = slicesToCreate[:len(slicesToCreate)-1]\\n408: \\t\\t\\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, slice)\\n409: \\t\\t\\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete[:i], slicesToDelete[i+1:]...)\\n410: \\t\\t} else {\\n411: \\t\\t\\ti++\\n412: \\t\\t}\\n413: \\t}\\n414: \\n415: \\t// Don't create new EndpointSlices if the Service is pending deletion. This\\n416: \\t// is to avoid a potential race condition with the garbage collector where\\n417: \\t// it tries to delete EndpointSlices as this controller replaces them.\\n418: \\tif service.DeletionTimestamp == nil {\\n419: \\t\\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToCreate {\\n420: \\t\\t\\taddTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice, triggerTime)\\n421: \\t\\t\\tcreatedSlice, err := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Create(context.TODO(), endpointSlice, metav1.CreateOptions{})\\n422: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n423: \\t\\t\\t\\t// If the namespace is terminating, creates will continue to fail. Simply drop the item.\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\tif errors.HasStatusCause(err, corev1.NamespaceTerminatingCause) {\\n425: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n426: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n427: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n428: \\t\\t\\t}\\n429: \\t\\t\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.Update(createdSlice)\\n430: \\t\\t\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\"create\\\").Inc()\\n431: \\t\\t}\\n432: \\t}\\n433: \\n434: \\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToUpdate {\\n435: \\t\\taddTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice, triggerTime)\\n436: \\t\\tupdatedSlice, err := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Update(context.TODO(), endpointSlice, metav1.UpdateOptions{})\\n437: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n438: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to update %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", endpointSlice.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n439: \\t\\t}\\n440: \\t\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.Update(updatedSlice)\\n441: \\t\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\"update\\\").Inc()\\n442: \\t}\\n443: \\n444: \\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToDelete {\\n445: \\t\\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), endpointSlice.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\\n446: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n447: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to delete %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", endpointSlice.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n448: \\t\\t}\\n449: \\t\\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(endpointSlice)\\n450: \\t\\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\\\"delete\\\").Inc()\\n451: \\t}\\n452: \\n453: \\ttopologyLabel := \\\"Disabled\\\"\\n454: \\tif r.topologyCache != nil && hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\\n455: \\t\\ttopologyLabel = \\\"Auto\\\"\\n456: \\t}\\n457: \\tvar trafficDistribution string\\n458: \\tif r.trafficDistributionEnabled && !hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\\n459: \\t\\tif service.Spec.TrafficDistribution != nil && *service.Spec.TrafficDistribution == corev1.ServiceTrafficDistributionPreferClose {\\n460: \\t\\t\\ttrafficDistribution = *service.Spec.TrafficDistribution\\n461: \\t\\t}\\n462: \\t}\\n463: \\n464: \\tnumSlicesChanged := len(slicesToCreate) + len(slicesToUpdate) + len(slicesToDelete)\\n465: \\tmetrics.EndpointSlicesChangedPerSync.WithLabelValues(topologyLabel, trafficDistribution).Observe(float64(numSlicesChanged))\\n466: \\n467: \\treturn nil\\n468: }\\n469: \\n470: // reconcileByPortMapping compares the endpoints found in existing slices with\\n471: // the list of desired endpoints and returns lists of slices to create, update,\\n472: // and delete. It also checks that the slices mirror the parent services labels.\\n473: // The logic is split up into several main steps:\\n474: //  1. Iterate through existing slices, delete endpoints that are no longer\\n475: //     desired and update matching endpoints that have changed. It also checks\\n476: //     if the slices have the labels of the parent services, and updates them if not.\\n477: //  2. Iterate through slices that have been modified in 1 and fill them up with\\n478: //     any remaining desired endpoints.\\n479: //  3. If there still desired endpoints left, try to fit them into a previously\\n480: //     unchanged slice and/or create new ones.\\n481: func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByPortMapping(\\n482: \\tlogger klog.Logger,\\n483: \\tservice *corev1.Service,\\n484: \\texistingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice,\\n485: \\tdesiredSet endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet,\\n486: \\tendpointMeta *endpointMeta,\\n487: ) ([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, []*discovery.EndpointSlice, []*discovery.EndpointSlice, int, int) {\\n488: \\tslicesByName := map[string]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n489: \\tsliceNamesUnchanged := sets.New[string]()\\n490: \\tsliceNamesToUpdate := sets.New[string]()\\n491: \\tsliceNamesToDelete := sets.New[string]()\\n492: \\tnumRemoved := 0\\n493: \\n494: \\t// 1. Iterate through existing slices to delete endpoints no longer desired\\n495: \\t//    and update endpoints that have changed\\n496: \\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\n497: \\t\\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = existingSlice\\n498: \\t\\tnewEndpoints := []discovery.Endpoint{}\\n499: \\t\\tendpointUpdated := false\\n500: \\t\\tfor _, endpoint := range existingSlice.Endpoints {\\n501: \\t\\t\\tgot := desiredSet.Get(&endpoint)\\n502: \\t\\t\\t// If endpoint is desired add it to list of endpoints to keep.\\n503: \\t\\t\\tif got != nil {\\n504: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewEndpoints = append(newEndpoints, *got)\\n505: \\t\\t\\t\\t// If existing version of endpoint doesn't match desired version\\n506: \\t\\t\\t\\t// set endpointUpdated to ensure endpoint changes are persisted.\\n507: \\t\\t\\t\\tif !endpointsliceutil.EndpointsEqualBeyondHash(got, &endpoint) {\\n508: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tendpointUpdated = true\\n509: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n510: \\t\\t\\t\\t// once an endpoint has been placed/found in a slice, it no\\n511: \\t\\t\\t\\t// longer needs to be handled\\n512: \\t\\t\\t\\tdesiredSet.Delete(&endpoint)\\n513: \\t\\t\\t}\\n514: \\t\\t}\\n515: \\n516: \\t\\t// generate the slice labels and check if parent labels have changed\\n517: \\t\\tlabels, labelsChanged := setEndpointSliceLabels(logger, existingSlice, service, r.controllerName)\\n518: \\n519: \\t\\t// If an endpoint was updated or removed, mark for update or delete\\n520: \\t\\tif endpointUpdated || len(existingSlice.Endpoints) != len(newEndpoints) {\\n521: \\t\\t\\tif len(existingSlice.Endpoints) > len(newEndpoints) {\\n522: \\t\\t\\t\\tnumRemoved += len(existingSlice.Endpoints) - len(newEndpoints)\\n523: \\t\\t\\t}\\n524: \\t\\t\\tif len(newEndpoints) == 0 {\\n525: \\t\\t\\t\\t// if no endpoints desired in this slice, mark for deletion\\n526: \\t\\t\\t\\tsliceNamesToDelete.Insert(existingSlice.Name)\\n527: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n528: \\t\\t\\t\\t// otherwise, copy and mark for update\\n529: \\t\\t\\t\\tepSlice := existingSlice.DeepCopy()\\n530: \\t\\t\\t\\tepSlice.Endpoints = newEndpoints\\n531: \\t\\t\\t\\tepSlice.Labels = labels\\n532: \\t\\t\\t\\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = epSlice\\n533: \\t\\t\\t\\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(epSlice.Name)\\n534: \\t\\t\\t}\\n535: \\t\\t} else if labelsChanged {\\n536: \\t\\t\\t// if labels have changed, copy and mark for update\\n537: \\t\\t\\tepSlice := existingSlice.DeepCopy()\\n538: \\t\\t\\tepSlice.Labels = labels\\n539: \\t\\t\\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = epSlice\\n540: \\t\\t\\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(epSlice.Name)\\n541: \\t\\t} else {\\n542: \\t\\t\\t// slices with no changes will be useful if there are leftover endpoints\\n543: \\t\\t\\tsliceNamesUnchanged.Insert(existingSlice.Name)\\n544: \\t\\t}\\n545: \\t}\\n546: \\n547: \\tnumAdded := desiredSet.Len()\\n548: \\n549: \\t// 2. If we still have desired endpoints to add and slices marked for update,\\n550: \\t//    iterate through the slices and fill them up with the desired endpoints.\\n551: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go%3FL400-550\"}, \"isError\": false}"

\\\\t\\\\t\\\\tsliceToFill = getSliceToFill(unchangedSlices, desiredSet.Len(), int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice))\\\\n586: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n587: \\\\n588: \\\\t\\\\t// If we didn't find a sliceToFill, \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tsliceNamesUnchanged.Delete(sliceToFill.Name)\\\\n608: \\\\t\\\\t} else {\\\\n609: \\\\t\\\\t\\\\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, sliceToFill)\\\\n610: \\\\t\\\\t}\\\\n611: \\tr.metricsCache.DeleteService(types.NamespacedName{Namespace: namespace, Name: name})\\n630: }\\n631: \\n632: func (r *Reconciler) GetControllerName() string {\\n633: \\treturn r.controllerName\\n634: }\\n635: \\n636: // ManagedByChanged returns true if one of the provided EndpointSlices is\\n637: // managed by the EndpointSlice controller while the other is not.\\n638: func (r *Reconciler) ManagedByChanged(endpointSlice1, endpointSlice2 *discovery.EndpointSlice) bool {\\n639: \\treturn r.ManagedByController(endpointSlice1) != r.ManagedByController(endpointSlice2)\\n640: }\\n641: \\n642: // ManagedByController returns true if the controller of the provided\\n643: // EndpointSlices is the EndpointSlice controller.\\n644: func (r *Reconciler) ManagedByController(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) bool {\\n645: \\tmanagedBy := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelManagedBy]\\n646: \\treturn managedBy == r.controllerName\\n647: }\\n648: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go%3FL550-700\"}, \"isError\": false}"

{
  "command": "create",
  "file_text": "/*\nCopyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage endpointslice\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\tclientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n\tcorelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\"\n\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\"\n\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/trafficdist\"\n\tendpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n)\n\n// Reconciler is responsible for transforming current EndpointSlice state into\n// desired state\ntype Reconciler struct {\n\tclient               clientset.Interface\n\tnodeLister           corelisters.NodeLister\n\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\n\tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\n\tmetricsCache         *metrics.Cache\n\t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\n\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\n\ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\n\t// trafficDistributionEnabled determines if endpointDistribution field is to\n\t// be considered when reconciling EndpointSlice hints.\n\ttrafficDistributionEnabled bool\n\t// eventRecorder allows Reconciler to record and publish events.\n\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n\tcontrollerName string\n}\n\ntype ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\n\n// WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\n// `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\nfunc WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\n\treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\n\t\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\n\t}\n}\n\n// endpointMeta includes the attributes we group slices on, this type helps with\n// that logic in Reconciler\ntype endpointMeta struct {\n\tports       []discovery.EndpointPort\n\taddressType discovery.AddressType\n}\n\n// Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n// compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n// slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n// to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\n\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\n\terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\n\tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\n\n\t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\n\tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\n\n\t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\n\t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\n\t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\n\t// for further adjustment\n\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n\t\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\n\t\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\n\t\t\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\n\t\t\t\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"Couldn't get key to remove EndpointSlice from topology cache\", \"existingSlice\", existingSlice, \"err\", err)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(svcKey, existingSlice.AddressType)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// add list if it is not on our map\n\t\tif _, ok := slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType]; !ok {\n\t\t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\n\t}\n\n\t// reconcile for existing.\n\tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\n\t\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\n\t\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\n\t// by the service\n\tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\n\t\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\n\t\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"delete\").Inc()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\n}\n\n// reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n// compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n// slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n// to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time, addressType discovery.AddressType) error {\n\terrs := []error{}\n\n\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tevents := []*topologycache.EventBuilder{}\n\n\t// Build data structures for existing state.\n\texistingSlicesByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey][]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n\t\tif ownedBy(existingSlice, service) {\n\t\t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(existingSlice.Ports)\n\t\t\texistingSlicesByPortMap[epHash] = append(existingSlicesByPortMap[epHash], existingSlice)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build data structures for desired state.\n\tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\n\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n\n\tfor _, pod := range pods {\n\t\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\n\t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n\t\t\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\n\t\t\t\taddressType: addressType,\n\t\t\t\tports:       endpointPorts,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\n\t\t\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\n\t\t\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n\t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n\t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n\t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n\t\t\t// stuck forever.\n\t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n\t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n\t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Skip pods on nodes tainted with NoScheduleNoTraffic: the node is being\n\t\t// drained from traffic routing while keeping existing pods running.\n\t\tif node != nil && nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(node) {\n\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node has NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n\t\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\n\ttotalAdded := 0\n\ttotalRemoved := 0\n\n\t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\n\tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\n\t\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\n\t\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\n\t\t\tlogger, service, existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap], desiredEndpoints, desiredMetaByPortMap[portMap])\n\n\t\ttotalAdded += added\n\t\ttotalRemoved += removed\n\n\t\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\n\t\t\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\n\t\t\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\n\t\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToUpdate...)\n\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\n\t}\n\n\t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\n\t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\n\tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\n\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\n\tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) && len(slicesToCreate) < 1 {\n\t\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\n\t\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, &endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\n\t\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 && placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], placeholderSlice) {\n\t\t\t// We are about to unnecessarily delete/recreate the placeholder, remove it now.\n\t\t\tslicesToDelete = slicesToDelete[:0]\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, placeholderSlice)\n\t\t}\n\t\tspMetrics.Set(endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(placeholderSlice.Ports), metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\n\t\t\tEndpoints: 0,\n\t\t\tSlices:    1,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics.EndpointsAddedPerSync.WithLabelValues().Observe(float64(totalAdded))\n\tmetrics.EndpointsRemovedPerSync.WithLabelValues().Observe(float64(totalRemoved))\n\n\tserviceNN := types.NamespacedName{Name: service.Name, Namespace: service.Namespace}\n\tr.metricsCache.UpdateServicePortCache(serviceNN, spMetrics)\n\n\t// Topology hints are assigned per address type. This means it is\n\t// theoretically possible for endpoints of one address type to be assigned\n\t// hints while another endpoints of another address type are not.\n\tsi := &topologycache.SliceInfo{\n\t\tServiceKey:  fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", service.Namespace, service.Name),\n\t\tAddressType: addressType,\n\t\tToCreate:    slicesToCreate,\n\t\tToUpdate:    slicesToUpdate,\n\t\tUnchanged:   unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete),\n\t}\n\n\tcanUseTrafficDistribution := r.trafficDistributionEnabled && !hintsEnabled(service.Annotations)\n\n\t// Check if we need to add/remove hints based on the topology annotation.\n\t//\n\t// This if/else clause can be removed once the annotation has been deprecated.\n\t// Ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-network/4444-service-routing-preference\n\tif r.topologyCache != nil && hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\n\t\t// Reaching this point means that we need to configure hints based on the\n\t\t// topology annotation.\n\t\tslicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, events = r.topologyCache.AddHints(logger, si)\n\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Reaching this point means that we will not be configuring hints based on\n\t\t// the topology annotation. We need to do 2 things:\n\t\t//  1. If hints were added previously based on the annotation, we need to\n\t\t//     clear up any locally cached hints from the topologyCache object.\n\t\t//  2. Optionally remove the actual hints from the EndpointSlice if we know\n\t\t//     that the `trafficDistribution` field is also NOT being used. In other\n\t\t//     words, if we know that the `trafficDistribution` field has been\n\t\t//     correctly configured by the customer, we DO NOT remove the hints and\n\t\t//     wait for the trafficDist handlers to correctly configure them. Always\n\t\t//     unconditionally removing hints here (and letting them get readded by\n\t\t//     the trafficDist) adds extra overhead in the form of DeepCopy (done\n\t\t//     within topologyCache.RemoveHints)\n\n\t\t// Check 1.\n\t\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\n\t\t\tif r.topologyCache.HasPopulatedHints(si.ServiceKey) {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"TopologyAwareHints annotation has changed, removing hints\", \"serviceKey\", si.ServiceKey, \"addressType\", si.AddressType)\n\t\t\t\tevents = append(events, &topologycache.EventBuilder{\n\t\t\t\t\tEventType: corev1.EventTypeWarning,\n\t\t\t\t\tReason:    \"TopologyAwareHintsDisabled\",\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   topologycache.FormatWithAddressType(topologycache.TopologyAwareHintsDisabled, si.AddressType),\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(si.ServiceKey, addressType)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check 2.\n\t\tif !canUseTrafficDistribution {\n\t\t\tslicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate = topologycache.RemoveHintsFromSlices(si)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif canUseTrafficDistribution {\n\t\tr.metricsCache.UpdateTrafficDistributionForService(serviceNN, service.Spec.TrafficDistribution)\n\t\tslicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, _ = trafficdist.ReconcileHints(service.Spec.TrafficDistribution, slicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete))\n\t} else {\n\t\tr.metricsCache.UpdateTrafficDistributionForService(serviceNN, nil)\n\t}\n\n\terr := r.finalize(service, slicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete, triggerTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n\t}\n\tfor _, event := range events {\n\t\tr.eventRecorder.Event(service, event.EventType, event.Reason, event.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\n\n}\n\nfunc NewReconciler(client clientset.Interface, nodeLister corelisters.NodeLister, maxEndpointsPerSlice int32, endpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker, topologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache, eventRecorder record.EventRecorder, controllerName string, options ...ReconcilerOption) *Reconciler {\n\tr := &Reconciler{\n\t\tclient:               client,\n\t\tnodeLister:           nodeLister,\n\t\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice: maxEndpointsPerSlice,\n\t\tendpointSliceTracker: endpointSliceTracker,\n\t\tmetricsCache:         metrics.NewCache(maxEndpointsPerSlice),\n\t\ttopologyCache:        topologyCache,\n\t\teventRecorder:        eventRecorder,\n\t\tcontrollerName:       controllerName,\n\t}\n\tfor _, option := range options {\n\t\toption(r)\n\t}\n\treturn r\n}\n\n// placeholderSliceCompare is a conversion func for comparing two placeholder endpoint slices.\n// It only compares the specific fields we care about.\nvar placeholderSliceCompare = conversion.EqualitiesOrDie(\n\tfunc(a, b metav1.OwnerReference) bool {\n\t\treturn a.String() == b.String()\n\t},\n\tfunc(a, b metav1.ObjectMeta) bool {\n\t\tif a.Namespace != b.Namespace {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor k, v := range a.Labels {\n\t\t\tif b.Labels[k] != v {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor k, v := range b.Labels {\n\t\t\tif a.Labels[k] != v {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn true\n\t},\n)\n\n// finalize creates, updates, and deletes slices as specified\nfunc (r *Reconciler) finalize(\n\tservice *corev1.Service,\n\tslicesToCreate,\n\tslicesToUpdate,\n\tslicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice,\n\ttriggerTime time.Time,\n) error {\n\t// If there are slices to create and delete, change the creates to updates\n\t// of the slices that would otherwise be deleted.\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(slicesToDelete); {\n\t\tif len(slicesToCreate) == 0 {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tsliceToDelete := slicesToDelete[i]\n\t\tslice := slicesToCreate[len(slicesToCreate)-1]\n\t\t// Only update EndpointSlices that are owned by this Service and have\n\t\t// the same AddressType. We need to avoid updating EndpointSlices that\n\t\t// are being garbage collected for an old Service with the same name.\n\t\t// The AddressType field is immutable. Since Services also consider\n\t\t// IPFamily immutable, the only case where this should matter will be\n\t\t// the migration from IP to IPv4 and IPv6 AddressTypes, where there's a\n\t\t// chance EndpointSlices with an IP AddressType would otherwise be\n\t\t// updated to IPv4 or IPv6 without this check.\n\t\tif sliceToDelete.AddressType == slice.AddressType && ownedBy(sliceToDelete, service) {\n\t\t\tslice.Name = sliceToDelete.Name\n\t\t\tslicesToCreate = slicesToCreate[:len(slicesToCreate)-1]\n\t\t\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, slice)\n\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete[:i], slicesToDelete[i+1:]...)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ti++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Don't create new EndpointSlices if the Service is pending deletion. This\n\t// is to avoid a potential race condition with the garbage collector where\n\t// it tries to delete EndpointSlices as this controller replaces them.\n\tif service.DeletionTimestamp == nil {\n\t\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToCreate {\n\t\t\taddTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice, triggerTime)\n\t\t\tcreatedSlice, err := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Create(context.TODO(), endpointSlice, metav1.CreateOptions{})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t// If the namespace is terminating, creates will continue to fail. Simply drop the item.\n\t\t\t\tif errors.HasStatusCause(err, corev1.NamespaceTerminatingCause) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.Update(createdSlice)\n\t\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"create\").Inc()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToUpdate {\n\t\taddTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice, triggerTime)\n\t\tupdatedSlice, err := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Update(context.TODO(), endpointSlice, metav1.UpdateOptions{})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to update %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\", endpointSlice.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.Update(updatedSlice)\n\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"update\").Inc()\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToDelete {\n\t\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), endpointSlice.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to delete %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\", endpointSlice.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(endpointSlice)\n\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"delete\").Inc()\n\t}\n\n\ttopologyLabel := \"Disabled\"\n\tif r.topologyCache != nil && hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\n\t\ttopologyLabel = \"Auto\"\n\t}\n\tvar trafficDistribution string\n\tif r.trafficDistributionEnabled && !hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\n\t\tif service.Spec.TrafficDistribution != nil && *service.Spec.TrafficDistribution == corev1.ServiceTrafficDistributionPreferClose {\n\t\t\ttrafficDistribution = *service.Spec.TrafficDistribution\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tnumSlicesChanged := len(slicesToCreate) + len(slicesToUpdate) + len(slicesToDelete)\n\tmetrics.EndpointSlicesChangedPerSync.WithLabelValues(topologyLabel, trafficDistribution).Observe(float64(numSlicesChanged))\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// reconcileByPortMapping compares the endpoints found in existing slices with\n// the list of desired endpoints and returns lists of slices to create, update,\n// and delete. It also checks that the slices mirror the parent services labels.\n// The logic is split up into several main steps:\n//  1. Iterate through existing slices, delete endpoints that are no longer\n//     desired and update matching endpoints that have changed. It also checks\n//     if the slices have the labels of the parent services, and updates them if not.\n//  2. Iterate through slices that have been modified in 1 and fill them up with\n//     any remaining desired endpoints.\n//  3. If there still desired endpoints left, try to fit them into a previously\n//     unchanged slice and/or create new ones.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) reconcileByPortMapping(\n\tlogger klog.Logger,\n\tservice *corev1.Service,\n\texistingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice,\n\tdesiredSet endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet,\n\tendpointMeta *endpointMeta,\n) ([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, []*discovery.EndpointSlice, []*discovery.EndpointSlice, int, int) {\n\tslicesByName := map[string]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tsliceNamesUnchanged := sets.New[string]()\n\tsliceNamesToUpdate := sets.New[string]()\n\tsliceNamesToDelete := sets.New[string]()\n\tnumRemoved := 0\n\n\t// 1. Iterate through existing slices to delete endpoints no longer desired\n\t//    and update endpoints that have changed\n\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n\t\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = existingSlice\n\t\tnewEndpoints := []discovery.Endpoint{}\n\t\tendpointUpdated := false\n\t\tfor _, endpoint := range existingSlice.Endpoints {\n\t\t\tgot := desiredSet.Get(&endpoint)\n\t\t\t// If endpoint is desired add it to list of endpoints to keep.\n\t\t\tif got != nil {\n\t\t\t\tnewEndpoints = append(newEndpoints, *got)\n\t\t\t\t// If existing version of endpoint doesn't match desired version\n\t\t\t\t// set endpointUpdated to ensure endpoint changes are persisted.\n\t\t\t\tif !endpointsliceutil.EndpointsEqualBeyondHash(got, &endpoint) {\n\t\t\t\t\tendpointUpdated = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// once an endpoint has been placed/found in a slice, it no\n\t\t\t\t// longer needs to be handled\n\t\t\t\tdesiredSet.Delete(&endpoint)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// generate the slice labels and check if parent labels have changed\n\t\tlabels, labelsChanged := setEndpointSliceLabels(logger, existingSlice, service, r.controllerName)\n\n\t\t// If an endpoint was updated or removed, mark for update or delete\n\t\tif endpointUpdated || len(existingSlice.Endpoints) != len(newEndpoints) {\n\t\t\tif len(existingSlice.Endpoints) > len(newEndpoints) {\n\t\t\t\tnumRemoved += len(existingSlice.Endpoints) - len(newEndpoints)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif len(newEndpoints) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t// if no endpoints desired in this slice, mark for deletion\n\t\t\t\tsliceNamesToDelete.Insert(existingSlice.Name)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// otherwise, copy and mark for update\n\t\t\t\tepSlice := existingSlice.DeepCopy()\n\t\t\t\tepSlice.Endpoints = newEndpoints\n\t\t\t\tepSlice.Labels = labels\n\t\t\t\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = epSlice\n\t\t\t\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(epSlice.Name)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if labelsChanged {\n\t\t\t// if labels have changed, copy and mark for update\n\t\t\tepSlice := existingSlice.DeepCopy()\n\t\t\tepSlice.Labels = labels\n\t\t\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = epSlice\n\t\t\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(epSlice.Name)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// slices with no changes will be useful if there are leftover endpoints\n\t\t\tsliceNamesUnchanged.Insert(existingSlice.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tnumAdded := desiredSet.Len()\n\n\t// 2. If we still have desired endpoints to add and slices marked for update,\n\t//    iterate through the slices and fill them up with the desired endpoints.\n\tif desiredSet.Len() > 0 && sliceNamesToUpdate.Len() > 0 {\n\t\tslices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\t\tfor _, sliceName := range sliceNamesToUpdate.UnsortedList() {\n\t\t\tslices = append(slices, slicesByName[sliceName])\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Sort endpoint slices by length so we're filling up the fullest ones\n\t\t// first.\n\t\tsort.Sort(endpointSliceEndpointLen(slices))\n\n\t\t// Iterate through slices and fill them up with desired endpoints.\n\t\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n\t\t\tfor desiredSet.Len() > 0 && len(slice.Endpoints) < int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice) {\n\t\t\t\tendpoint, _ := desiredSet.PopAny()\n\t\t\t\tslice.Endpoints = append(slice.Endpoints, *endpoint)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// 3. If there are still desired endpoints left at this point, we try to fit\n\t//    the endpoints in a single existing slice. If there are no slices with\n\t//    that capacity, we create new slices for the endpoints.\n\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\n\tfor desiredSet.Len() > 0 {\n\t\tvar sliceToFill *discovery.EndpointSlice\n\n\t\t// If the remaining amounts of endpoints is smaller than the max\n\t\t// endpoints per slice and we have slices that haven't already been\n\t\t// filled, try to fit them in one.\n\t\tif desiredSet.Len() < int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice) && sliceNamesUnchanged.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\t\t\tfor _, sliceName := range sliceNamesUnchanged.UnsortedList() {\n\t\t\t\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slicesByName[sliceName])\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsliceToFill = getSliceToFill(unchangedSlices, desiredSet.Len(), int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If we didn't find a sliceToFill, generate a new empty one.\n\t\tif sliceToFill == nil {\n\t\t\tsliceToFill = newEndpointSlice(logger, service, endpointMeta, r.controllerName)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// deep copy required to modify this slice.\n\t\t\tsliceToFill = sliceToFill.DeepCopy()\n\t\t\tslicesByName[sliceToFill.Name] = sliceToFill\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Fill the slice up with remaining endpoints.\n\t\tfor desiredSet.Len() > 0 && len(sliceToFill.Endpoints) < int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice) {\n\t\t\tendpoint, _ := desiredSet.PopAny()\n\t\t\tsliceToFill.Endpoints = append(sliceToFill.Endpoints, *endpoint)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// New slices will not have a Name set, use this to determine whether\n\t\t// this should be an update or create.\n\t\tif sliceToFill.Name != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(sliceToFill.Name)\n\t\t\tsliceNamesUnchanged.Delete(sliceToFill.Name)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, sliceToFill)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build slicesToUpdate from slice names.\n\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tfor _, sliceName := range sliceNamesToUpdate.UnsortedList() {\n\t\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, slicesByName[sliceName])\n\t}\n\n\t// Build slicesToDelete from slice names.\n\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tfor _, sliceName := range sliceNamesToDelete.UnsortedList() {\n\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, slicesByName[sliceName])\n\t}\n\n\treturn slicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete, numAdded, numRemoved\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) DeleteService(namespace, name string) {\n\tr.metricsCache.DeleteService(types.NamespacedName{Namespace: namespace, Name: name})\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) GetControllerName() string {\n\treturn r.controllerName\n}\n\n// ManagedByChanged returns true if one of the provided EndpointSlices is\n// managed by the EndpointSlice controller while the other is not.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) ManagedByChanged(endpointSlice1, endpointSlice2 *discovery.EndpointSlice) bool {\n\treturn r.ManagedByController(endpointSlice1) != r.ManagedByController(endpointSlice2)\n}\n\n// ManagedByController returns true if the controller of the provided\n// EndpointSlices is the EndpointSlice controller.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) ManagedByController(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) bool {\n\tmanagedBy := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelManagedBy]\n\treturn managedBy == r.controllerName\n}\n\n// nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint returns true if the node has a taint with\n// effect NoScheduleNoTraffic, indicating that the node should be removed from\n// service EndpointSlices while keeping existing pods running.\nfunc nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(node *corev1.Node) bool {\n\tfor _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n\t\tif taint.Effect == corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n",
  "path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}
