protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,28,opt,name=csi\\\\\\\"`\\\\n157: \\\\t// ephemeral represents a volume that is handled by a cluster storage driver.\\\\n158: \\\\t// The volume's lifecycle is tied to the pod that defines it - it will be created before the pod starts,\\\\n159: \\\\t// and deleted when the pod is removed.\\\\n160: \\\\t//\\\\n161: \\\\t// Use this if:\\\\n162: \\\\t// a) the volume is only needed while the pod runs,\\\\n163: \\\\t// b) features of normal volumes like restoring from snapshot or capacity\\\\n164: \\\\t//    tracking are needed,\\\\n165: \\\\t// c) the storage driver is specified through a storage class, and\\\\n166: \\\\t// d) the storage driver supports dynamic volume provisioning through\\\\n167: \\\\t//    a PersistentVolumeClaim (see EphemeralVolumeSource for more\\\\n168: \\\\t//    information on the connection between this volume type\\\\n169: \\\\t//    and PersistentVolumeClaim).\\\\n170: \\\\t//\\\\n171: \\\\t// Use PersistentVolumeClaim or one of the vendor-specific\\\\n172: \\\\t// APIs for volumes that persist for longer than the lifecycle\\\\n173: \\\\t// of an individual pod.\\\\n174: \\\\t//\\\\n175: \\\\t// Use CSI for light-weight local ephemeral volumes if the CSI driver is meant to\\\\n176: \\\\t// be used that way - see the documentation of the driver for\\\\n177: \\\\t// more information.\\\\n178: \\\\t//\\\\n179: \\\\t// A pod can use both types of ephemeral volumes and\\\\n180: \\\\t// persistent volumes at the same time.\\\\n181: \\\\t//\\\\n182: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n183: \\\\tEphemeral *EphemeralVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"ephemeral,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,29,opt,name=ephemeral\\\\\\\"`\\\\n184: }\\\\n185: \\\\n186: // PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource references the user's PVC in the same namespace.\\\\n187: // This volume finds the bound PV and mounts that volume for the pod. A\\\\n188: // PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource is, essentially, a wrapper around another\\\\n189: // type of volume that is owned by someone else (the system).\\\\n190: type PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource struct {\\\\n191: \\\\t// claimName is the name of a PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace as the pod using this volume.\\\\n192: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims\\\\n193: \\\\tClaimName string `json:\\\\\\\"claimName\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=claimName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n194: \\\\t// readOnly Will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\\\\n195: \\\\t// Default false.\\\\n196: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n197: \\\\tReadOnly bool `json:\\\\\\\"readOnly,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"varint,2,opt,name=readOnly\\\\\\\"`\\\\n198: }\\\\n199: \\\\n200: // PersistentVolumeSource is similar to VolumeSource but meant for the\\\\n201: // administrator who creates PVs. Exactly one of its members must be set.\\\\n202: type PersistentVolumeSource struct {\\\\n203: \\\\t// gcePersistentDisk represents a GCE Disk resource that is attached to a\\\\n204: \\\\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod. Provisioned by an admin.\\\\n205: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk\\\\n206: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n207: \\\\tGCEPersistentDisk *GCEPersistentDiskVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"gcePersistentDisk,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=gcePersistentDisk\\\\\\\"`\\\\n208: \\\\t// awsElasticBlockStore represents an AWS Disk resource that is attached to a\\\\n209: \\\\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\\\n210: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore\\\\n211: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n212: \\\\tAWSElasticBlockStore *AWSElasticBlockStoreVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"awsElasticBlockStore,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=awsElasticBlockStore\\\\\\\"`\\\\n213: \\\\t// hostPath represents a directory on the host.\\\\n214: \\\\t// Provisioned by a developer or tester.\\\\n215: \\\\t// This is useful for single-node development and testing only!\\\\n216: \\\\t// On-host storage is not supported in any way and WILL NOT WORK in a multi-node cluster.\\\\n217: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath\\\\n218: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n219: \\\\tHostPath *HostPathVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"hostPath,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=hostPath\\\\\\\"`\\\\n220: \\\\t// glusterfs represents a Glusterfs volume that is attached to a host and\\\\n221: \\\\t// exposed to the pod. Provisioned by an admin.\\\\n222: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md\\\\n223: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n224: \\\\tGlusterfs *GlusterfsPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"glusterfs,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=glusterfs\\\\\\\"`\\\\n225: \\\\t// nfs represents an NFS mount on the host. Provisioned by an admin.\\\\n226: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs\\\\n227: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n228: \\\\tNFS *NFSVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"nfs,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=nfs\\\\\\\"`\\\\n229: \\\\t// rbd represents a Rados Block Device mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime.\\\\n230: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md\\\\n231: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n232: \\\\tRBD *RBDPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"rbd,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=rbd\\\\\\\"`\\\\n233: \\\\t// iscsi represents an ISCSI Disk resource that is attached to a\\\\n234: \\\\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod. Provisioned by an admin.\\\\n235: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n236: \\\\tISCSI *ISCSIPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"iscsi,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=iscsi\\\\\\\"`\\\\n237: \\\\t// cinder represents a cinder volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.\\\\n238: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md\\\\n239: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n240: \\\\tCinder *CinderPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"cinder,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=cinder\\\\\\\"`\\\\n241: \\\\t// cephFS represents a Ceph FS mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime\\\\n242: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n243: \\\\tCephFS *CephFSPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"cephfs,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=cephfs\\\\\\\"`\\\\n244: \\\\t// fc represents a Fibre Channel resource that is attached to a kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\\\n245: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n246: \\\\tFC *FCVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"fc,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,10,opt,name=fc\\\\\\\"`\\\\n247: \\\\t// flocker represents a Flocker volume attached to a kubelet's host machine and exposed to the pod for its usage. This depends on the Flocker control service being running\\\\n248: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n249: \\\\tFlocker *FlockerVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"flocker,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,11,opt,name=flocker\\\\\\\"`\\\\n250: \\\\t// flexVolume represents a generic volume resource that is\\\\n251: \\\\t// provisioned/attached using an exec based plugin.\\\\n252: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n253: \\\\tFlexVolume *FlexPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"flexVolume,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,12,opt,name=flexVolume\\\\\\\"`\\\\n254: \\\\t// azureFile represents an Azure File Service mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.\\\\n255: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n256: \\\\tAzureFile *AzureFilePersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"azureFile,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,13,opt,name=azureFile\\\\\\\"`\\\\n257: \\\\t// vsphereVolume represents a vSphere volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine\\\\n258: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n259: \\\\tVsphereVolume *VsphereVirtualDiskVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"vsphereVolume,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,14,opt,name=vsphereVolume\\\\\\\"`\\\\n260: \\\\t// quobyte represents a Quobyte mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime\\\\n261: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n262: \\\\tQuobyte *QuobyteVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"quobyte,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,15,opt,name=quobyte\\\\\\\"`\\\\n263: \\\\t// azureDisk represents an Azure Data Disk mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.\\\\n264: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n265: \\\\tAzureDisk *AzureDiskVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"azureDisk,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,16,opt,name=azureDisk\\\\\\\"`\\\\n266: \\\\t// photonPersistentDisk represents a PhotonController persistent disk attached and mounted on kubelets host machine\\\\n267: \\\\tPhotonPersistentDisk *PhotonPersistentDiskVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"photonPersistentDisk,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,17,opt,name=photonPersistentDisk\\\\\\\"`\\\\n268: \\\\t// portworxVolume represents a portworx volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine\\\\n269: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n270: \\\\tPortworxVolume *PortworxVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"portworxVolume,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,18,opt,name=portworxVolume\\\\\\\"`\\\\n271: \\\\t// scaleIO represents a ScaleIO persistent volume attached and mounted on Kubernetes nodes.\\\\n272: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n273: \\\\tScaleIO *ScaleIOPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"scaleIO,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,19,opt,name=scaleIO\\\\\\\"`\\\\n274: \\\\t// local represents directly-attached storage with node affinity\\\\n275: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n276: \\\\tLocal *LocalVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"local,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,20,opt,name=local\\\\\\\"`\\\\n277: \\\\t// storageOS represents a StorageOS volume that is attached to the kubelet's host machine and mounted into the pod\\\\n278: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/storageos/README.md\\\\n279: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n280: \\\\tStorageOS *StorageOSPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"storageos,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,21,opt,name=storageos\\\\\\\"`\\\\n281: \\\\t// csi represents storage that is handled by an external CSI driver (Beta feature).\\\\n282: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n283: \\\\tCSI *CSIPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\"csi,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,22,opt,name=csi\\\\\\\"`\\\\n284: }\\\\n285: \\\\n286: const (\\\\n287: \\\\t// BetaStorageClassAnnotation represents the beta/previous StorageClass annotation.\\\\n288: \\\\t// It's currently still used and will be held for backwards compatibility\\\\n289: \\\\tBetaStorageClassAnnotation = \\\\\\\"volume.beta.kubernetes.io/storage-class\\\\\\\"\\\\n290: \\\\n291: \\\\t// MountOptionAnnotation defines mount option annotation used in PVs\\\\n292: \\\\tMountOptionAnnotation = \\\\\\\"volume.beta.kubernetes.io/mount-options\\\\\\\"\\\\n293: )\\\\n294: \\\\n295: // +genclient\\\\n296: // +genclient:nonNamespaced\\\\n297: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\\\n298: \\\\n299: // PersistentVolume (PV) is a storage resource provisioned by an administrator.\\\\n300: // It is analogous to a node.\\\\n301: // More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes\\\\n302: type PersistentVolume struct {\\\\n303: \\\\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\\\\\\\",inline\\\\\\\"`\\\\n304: \\\\t// Standard object's metadata.\\\\n305: \\\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\\\\n306: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n307: \\\\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\\\\\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\\\\\"`\\\\n308: \\\\n309: \\\\t// spec defines a specification of a persistent volume owned by the cluster.\\\\n310: \\\\t// Provisioned by an administrator.\\\\n311: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistent-volumes\\\\n312: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n313: \\\\tSpec PersistentVolumeSpec `json:\\\\\\\"spec,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=spec\\\\\\\"`\\\\n314: \\\\n315: \\\\t// status represents the current information/status for the persistent volume.\\\\n316: \\\\t// Populated by the system.\\\\n317: \\\\t// Read-only.\\\\n318: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistent-volumes\\\\n319: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n320: \\\\tStatus PersistentVolumeStatus `json:\\\\\\\"status,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=status\\\\\\\"`\\\\n321: }\\\\n322: \\\\n323: // PersistentVolumeSpec is the specification of a persistent volume.\\\\n324: type PersistentVolumeSpec struct {\\\\n325: \\\\t// capacity is the description of the persistent volume's resources and capacity.\\\\n326: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#capacity\\\\n327: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n328: \\\\tCapacity ResourceList `json:\\\\\\\"capacity,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=capacity,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n329: \\\\t// persistentVolumeSource is the actual volume backing the persistent volume.\\\\n330: \\\\tPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\\\\\",inline\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=persistentVolumeSource\\\\\\\"`\\\\n331: \\\\t// accessModes contains all ways the volume can be mounted.\\\\n332: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#access-modes\\\\n333: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n334: \\\\t// +listType=atomic\\\\n335: \\\\tAccessModes []PersistentVolumeAccessMode `json:\\\\\\\"accessModes,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,rep,name=accessModes,casttype=PersistentVolumeAccessMode\\\\\\\"`\\\\n336: \\\\t// claimRef is part of a bi-directional binding between PersistentVolume and PersistentVolumeClaim.\\\\n337: \\\\t// Expected to be non-nil when bound.\\\\n338: \\\\t// claim.VolumeName is the authoritative bind between PV and PVC.\\\\n339: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#binding\\\\n340: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n341: \\\\t// +structType=granular\\\\n342: \\\\tClaimRef *ObjectReference `json:\\\\\\\"claimRef,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=claimRef\\\\\\\"`\\\\n343: \\\\t// persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy defines what happens to a persistent volume when released from its claim.\\\\n344: \\\\t// Valid options are Retain (default for manually created PersistentVolumes), Delete (default\\\\n345: \\\\t// for dynamically provisioned PersistentVolumes), and Recycle (deprecated).\\\\n346: \\\\t// Recycle must be supported by the volume plugin underlying this PersistentVolume.\\\\n347: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#reclaiming\\\\n348: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n349: \\\\tPersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy `json:\\\\\\\"persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy,casttype=PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy\\\\\\\"`\\\\n350: \\\\t// storageClassName is the name of StorageClass to which this persistent volume belongs. Empty value\\\\n351: \\\\t// means that this volume does not belong to any StorageClass.\\\\n352: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n353: \\\\tStorageClassName string `json:\\\\\\\"storageClassName,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=storageClassName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n354: \\\\t// mountOptions is the list of mount options, e.g. [\\\\\\\"ro\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"soft\\\\\\\"]. Not validated - mount will\\\\n355: \\\\t// simply fail if one is invalid.\\\\n356: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/#mount-options\\\\n357: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n358: \\\\t// +listType=atomic\\\\n359: \\\\tMountOptions []string `json:\\\\\\\"mountOptions,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=mountOptions\\\\\\\"`\\\\n360: \\\\t// volumeMode defines if a volume is intended to be used with a formatted filesystem\\\\n361: \\\\t// or to remain in raw block state. Value of Filesystem is implied when not included in spec.\\\\n362: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n363: \\\\tVolumeMode *PersistentVolumeMode `json:\\\\\\\"volumeMode,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=volumeMode,casttype=PersistentVolumeMode\\\\\\\"`\\\\n364: \\\\t// nodeAffinity defines constraints that limit what nodes this volume can be accessed from.\\\\n365: \\\\t// This field influences the scheduling of pods that use this volume.\\\\n366: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n367: \\\\tNodeAffinity *VolumeNodeAffinity `json:\\\\\\\"nodeAffinity,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=nodeAffinity\\\\\\\"`\\\\n368: \\\\t// Name of VolumeAttributesClass to which this persistent volume belongs. Empty value\\\\n369: \\\\t// is not allowed. When this field is not set, it indicates that this volume does not belong to any\\\\n370: \\\\t// VolumeAttributesClass. This field is mutable and can be changed by the CSI driver\\\\n371: \\\\t// after a volume has been updated successfully to a new class.\\\\n372: \\\\t// For an unbound PersistentVolume, the volumeAttributesClassName will be matched with unbound\\\\n373: \\\\t// PersistentVolumeClaims during the binding process.\\\\n374: \\\\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling VolumeAttributesClass feature.\\\\n375: \\\\t// +featureGate=VolumeAttributesClass\\\\n376: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n377: \\\\tVolumeAttributesClassName *string `json:\\\\\\\"volumeAttributesClassName,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,10,opt,name=volumeAttributesClassName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n378: }\\\\n379: \\\\n380: // VolumeNodeAffinity defines constraints that limit what nodes this volume can be accessed from.\\\\n381: type VolumeNodeAffinity struct {\\\\n382: \\\\t// required specifies hard node constraints that must be met.\\\\n383: \\\\tRequired *NodeSelector `json:\\\\\\\"required,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=required\\\\\\\"`\\\\n384: }\\\\n385: \\\\n386: // PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy describes a policy for end-of-life maintenance of persistent volumes.\\\\n387: // +enum\\\\n388: type PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy string\\\\n389: \\\\n390: const (\\\\n391: \\\\t// PersistentVolumeReclaimRecycle means the volume will be recycled back into the pool of unbound persistent volumes on release from its claim.\\\\n392: \\\\t// The volume plugin must support Recycling.\\\\n393: \\\\tPersistentVolumeReclaimRecycle PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy = \\\\\\\"Recycle\\\\\\\"\\\\n394: \\\\t// PersistentVolumeReclaimDelete means the volume will be deleted from Kubernetes on release from its claim.\\\\n395: \\\\t// The volume plugin must support Deletion.\\\\n396: \\\\tPersistentVolumeReclaimDelete PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy = \\\\\\\"Delete\\\\\\\"\\\\n397: \\\\t// PersistentVolumeReclaimRetain means the volume will be left in its current phase (Released) for manual reclamation by the administrator.\\\\n398: \\\\t// The default policy is Retain.\\\\n399: \\\\tPersistentVolumeReclaimRetain PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy = \\\\\\\"Retain\\\\\\\"\\\\n400: )\\\\n401: \\\\n402: // PersistentVolumeMode describes how a volume is intended to be consumed, either Block or Filesystem.\\\\n403: // +enum\\\\n404: type PersistentVolumeMode string\\\\n405: \\\\n406: const (\\\\n407: \\\\t// PersistentVolumeBlock means the volume will not be formatted with a filesystem and will remain a raw block device.\\\\n408: \\\\tPersistentVolumeBlock PersistentVolumeMode = \\\\\\\"Block\\\\\\\"\\\\n409: \\\\t// PersistentVolumeFilesystem means the volume will be or is formatted with a filesystem.\\\\n410: \\\\tPersistentVolumeFilesystem PersistentVolumeMode = \\\\\\\"Filesystem\\\\\\\"\\\\n411: )\\\\n412: \\\\n413: // PersistentVolumeStatus is the current status of a persistent volume.\\\\n414: type PersistentVolumeStatus struct {\\\\n415: \\\\t// phase indicates if a volume is available, bound to a claim, or released by a claim.\\\\n416: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#phase\\\\n417: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n418: \\\\tPhase PersistentVolumePhase `json:\\\\\\\"phase,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=phase,casttype=PersistentVolumePhase\\\\\\\"`\\\\n419: \\\\t// message is a human-readable message indicating details about why the volume is in this state.\\\\n420: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n421: \\\\tMessage string `json:\\\\\\\"message,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=message\\\\\\\"`\\\\n422: \\\\t// reason is a brief CamelCase string that describes any failure and is meant\\\\n423: \\\\t// for machine parsing and tidy display in the CLI.\\\\n424: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n425: \\\\tReason string `json:\\\\\\\"reason,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=reason\\\\\\\"`\\\\n426: \\\\t// lastPhaseTransitionTime is the time the phase transitioned from one to another\\\\n427: \\\\t// and automatically resets to current time everytime a volume phase transitions.\\\\n428: \\\\t// This is a beta field and requires the PersistentVolumeLastPhaseTransitionTime feature to be enabled (enabled by default).\\\\n429: \\\\t// +featureGate=PersistentVolumeLastPhaseTransitionTime\\\\n430: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n431: \\\\tLastPhaseTransitionTime *metav1.Time `json:\\\\\\\"lastPhaseTransitionTime,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=lastPhaseTransitionTime\\\\\\\"`\\\\n432: }\\\\n433: \\\\n434: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\\\n435: \\\\n436: // PersistentVolumeList is a list of PersistentVolume items.\\\\n437: type PersistentVolumeList struct {\\\\n438: \\\\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\\\\\\\",inline\\\\\\\"`\\\\n439: \\\\t// Standard list metadata.\\\\n440: \\\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds\\\\n441: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n442: \\\\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\\\\\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\\\\\"`\\\\n443: \\\\t// items is a list of persistent volumes.\\\\n444: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes\\\\n445: \\\\tItems []PersistentVolume `json:\\\\\\\"items\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\\\\\\\"`\\\\n446: }\\\\n447: \\\\n448: // +genclient\\\\n449: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\\\n450: \\\\n451: // PersistentVolumeClaim is a user's request for and claim to a persistent volume\\\\n452: type PersistentVolumeClaim struct {\\\\n453: \\\\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\\\\\\\",inline\\\\\\\"`\\\\n454: \\\\t// Standard object's metadata.\\\\n455: \\\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\\\\n456: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n457: \\\\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\\\\\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\\\\\"`\\\\n458: \\\\n459: \\\\t// spec defines the desired characteristics of a volume requested by a pod author.\\\\n460: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims\\\\n461: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n462: \\\\tSpec PersistentVolumeClaimSpec `json:\\\\\\\"spec,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=spec\\\\\\\"`\\\\n463: \\\\n464: \\\\t// status represents the current information/status of a persistent volume claim.\\\\n465: \\\\t// Read-only.\\\\n466: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims\\\\n467: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n468: \\\\tStatus PersistentVolumeClaimStatus `json:\\\\\\\"status,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=status\\\\\\\"`\\\\n469: }\\\\n470: \\\\n471: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\\\n472: \\\\n473: // PersistentVolumeClaimList is a list of PersistentVolumeClaim items.\\\\n474: type PersistentVolumeClaimList struct {\\\\n475: \\\\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\\\\\\\",inline\\\\\\\"`\\\\n476: \\\\t// Standard list metadata.\\\\n477: \\\\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds\\\\n478: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n479: \\\\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\\\\\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\\\\\"`\\\\n480: \\\\t// items is a list of persistent volume claims.\\\\n481: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims\\\\n482: \\\\tItems []PersistentVolumeClaim `json:\\\\\\\"items\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=items\\\\\\\"`\\\\n483: }\\\\n484: \\\\n485: // PersistentVolumeClaimSpec describes the common attributes of storage devices\\\\n486: // and allows a Source for provider-specific attributes\\\\n487: type PersistentVolumeClaimSpec struct {\\\\n488: \\\\t// accessModes contains the desired access modes the volume should have.\\\\n489: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#access-modes-1\\\\n490: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n491: \\\\t// +listType=atomic\\\\n492: \\\\tAccessModes []PersistentVolumeAccessMode `json:\\\\\\\"accessModes,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=accessModes,casttype=PersistentVolumeAccessMode\\\\\\\"`\\\\n493: \\\\t// selector is a label query over volumes to consider for binding.\\\\n494: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n495: \\\\tSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\\\\\\\"selector,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=selector\\\\\\\"`\\\\n496: \\\\t// resources represents the minimum resources the volume should have.\\\\n497: \\\\t// If RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure feature is enabled users are allowed to specify resource requirements\\\\n498: \\\\t// that are lower than previous value but must still be higher than capacity recorded in the\\\\n499: \\\\t// status field of the claim.\\\\n500: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#resources\\\\n501: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL1-500\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package v1\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\\\"\\n21: \\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr\\\"\\n24: )\\n25: \\n26: const (\\n27: \\t// NamespaceDefault means the object is in the default namespace which is applied when not specified by clients\\n28: \\tNamespaceDefault string = \\\"default\\\"\\n29: \\t// NamespaceAll is the default argument to specify on a context when you want to list or filter resources across all namespaces\\n30: \\tNamespaceAll string = \\\"\\\"\\n31: \\t// NamespaceNodeLease is the namespace where we place node lease objects (used for node heartbeats)\\n32: \\tNamespaceNodeLease string = \\\"kube-node-lease\\\"\\n33: )\\n34: \\n35: // Volume represents a named volume in a pod that may be accessed by any container in the pod.\\n36: type Volume struct {\\n37: \\t// name of the volume.\\n38: \\t// Must be a DNS_LABEL and unique within the pod.\\n39: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names\\n40: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=name\\\"`\\n41: \\t// volumeSource represents the location and type of the mounted volume.\\n42: \\t// If not specified, the Volume is implied to be an EmptyDir.\\n43: \\t// This implied behavior is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.\\n44: \\tVolumeSource `json:\\\",inline\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=volumeSource\\\"`\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: // Represents the source of a volume to mount.\\n48: // Only one of its members may be specified.\\n49: type VolumeSource struct {\\n50: \\t// hostPath represents a pre-existing file or directory on the host\\n51: \\t// machine that is directly exposed to the container. This is generally\\n52: \\t// used for system agents or other privileged things that are allowed\\n53: \\t// to see the host machine. Most containers will NOT need this.\\n54: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath\\n55: \\t// ---\\n56: \\t// TODO(jonesdl) We need to restrict who can use host directory mounts and who can/can not\\n57: \\t// mount host directories as read/write.\\n58: \\t// +optional\\n59: \\tHostPath *HostPathVolumeSource `json:\\\"hostPath,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=hostPath\\\"`\\n60: \\t// emptyDir represents a temporary directory that shares a pod's lifetime.\\n61: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir\\n62: \\t// +optional\\n63: \\tEmptyDir *EmptyDirVolumeSource `json:\\\"emptyDir,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=emptyDir\\\"`\\n64: \\t// gcePersistentDisk represents a GCE Disk resource that is attached to a\\n65: \\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\n66: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk\\n67: \\t// +optional\\n68: \\tGCEPersistentDisk *GCEPersistentDiskVolumeSource `json:\\\"gcePersistentDisk,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=gcePersistentDisk\\\"`\\n69: \\t// awsElasticBlockStore represents an AWS Disk resource that is attached to a\\n70: \\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\n71: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore\\n72: \\t// +optional\\n73: \\tAWSElasticBlockStore *AWSElasticBlockStoreVolumeSource `json:\\\"awsElasticBlockStore,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=awsElasticBlockStore\\\"`\\n74: \\t// gitRepo represents a git repository at a particular revision.\\n75: \\t// DEPRECATED: GitRepo is deprecated. To provision a container with a git repo, mount an\\n76: \\t// EmptyDir into an InitContainer that clones the repo using git, then mount the EmptyDir\\n77: \\t// into the Pod's container.\\n78: \\t// +optional\\n79: \\tGitRepo *GitRepoVolumeSource `json:\\\"gitRepo,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=gitRepo\\\"`\\n80: \\t// secret represents a secret that should populate this volume.\\n81: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#secret\\n82: \\t// +optional\\n83: \\tSecret *SecretVolumeSource `json:\\\"secret,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=secret\\\"`\\n84: \\t// nfs represents an NFS mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime\\n85: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs\\n86: \\t// +optional\\n87: \\tNFS *NFSVolumeSource `json:\\\"nfs,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=nfs\\\"`\\n88: \\t// iscsi represents an ISCSI Disk resource that is attached to a\\n89: \\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\n90: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/iscsi/README.md\\n91: \\t// +optional\\n92: \\tISCSI *ISCSIVolumeSource `json:\\\"iscsi,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=iscsi\\\"`\\n93: \\t// glusterfs represents a Glusterfs mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime.\\n94: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md\\n95: \\t// +optional\\n96: \\tGlusterfs *GlusterfsVolumeSource `json:\\\"glusterfs,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=glusterfs\\\"`\\n97: \\t// persistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource represents a reference to a\\n98: \\t// PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace.\\n99: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims\\n100: \\t// +optional\\n101: \\tPersistentVolumeClaim *PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource `json:\\\"persistentVolumeClaim,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,10,opt,name=persistentVolumeClaim\\\"`\\n102: \\t// rbd represents a Rados Block Device mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime.\\n103: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md\\n104: \\t// +optional\\n105: \\tRBD *RBDVolumeSource `json:\\\"rbd,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,11,opt,name=rbd\\\"`\\n106: \\t// flexVolume represents a generic volume resource that is\\n107: \\t// provisioned/attached using an exec based plugin.\\n108: \\t// +optional\\n109: \\tFlexVolume *FlexVolumeSource `json:\\\"flexVolume,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,12,opt,name=flexVolume\\\"`\\n110: \\t// cinder represents a cinder volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.\\n111: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md\\n112: \\t// +optional\\n113: \\tCinder *CinderVolumeSource `json:\\\"cinder,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,13,opt,name=cinder\\\"`\\n114: \\t// cephFS represents a Ceph FS mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime\\n115: \\t// +optional\\n116: \\tCephFS *CephFSVolumeSource `json:\\\"cephfs,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,14,opt,name=cephfs\\\"`\\n117: \\t// flocker represents a Flocker volume attached to a kubelet's host machine. This depends on the Flocker control service being running\\n118: \\t// +optional\\n119: \\tFlocker *FlockerVolumeSource `json:\\\"flocker,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,15,opt,name=flocker\\\"`\\n120: \\t// downwardAPI represents downward API about the pod that should populate this volume\\n121: \\t// +optional\\n122: \\tDownwardAPI File Service mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.\\n127: \\t// +optional\\n128: \\tAzureFile *AzureFileVolumeSource `json:\\\"azureFile,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,18,opt,name=azureFile\\\"`\\n129: \\t// configMap represents a configMap that should populate this volume\\n130: \\t// +optional\\n131: \\tConfigMap *ConfigMapVolumeSource `json:\\\"configMap,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,19,opt,name=configMap\\\"`\\n132: \\t// vsphereVolume represents a vSphere volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine\\n133: \\t// protobuf:\\\"bytes,24,opt,name=portworxVolume\\\"`\\n148: \\t// scaleIO represents a ScaleIO persistent volume attached and mounted on Kubernetes nodes.\\n149: \\t// +optional\\n150: \\tScaleIO *ScaleIOVolumeSource `json:\\\"scaleIO,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,25,opt,name=scaleIO\\\"`\\n151: \\t// storageOS represents a StorageOS volume attached and mounted on Kubernetes nodes.\\n152: \\t// +optional\\n153: \\tStorageOS *StorageOSVolumeSource `json:\\\"storageos,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,27,opt,name=storageos\\\"`\\n154: \\t// csi (Container Storage Interface) represents ephemeral storage that is handled by certain external CSI drivers (Beta feature).\\n155: \\t// +optional\\n156: \\tCSI *CSIVolumeSource `json:\\\"csi,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,28,opt,name=csi\\\"`\\n157: \\t// ephemeral represents a volume that is handled by a cluster storage driver.\\n158: \\t// The volume's lifecycle is tied to the pod that defines it - it will be created before the pod starts,\\n159: \\t// and deleted when the pod is removed.\\n160: \\t//\\n161: \\t// Use this if:\\n162: \\t// a) the volume is only needed while the pod runs,\\n163: \\t// b) features of normal volumes like restoring from snapshot or capacity\\n164: \\t//    tracking are needed,\\n165: \\t// c) the storage driver is specified through a storage class, and\\n166: \\t// d) the storage driver supports dynamic volume provisioning through\\n167: \\t//    a PersistentVolumeClaim (see EphemeralVolumeSource for more\\n168: \\t//    information on the connection between this volume type\\n169: \\t//    and PersistentVolumeClaim).\\n170: \\t//\\n171: \\t// Use PersistentVolumeClaim or one of the vendor-specific\\n172: \\t// APIs for volumes that persist for longer than the lifecycle\\n173: \\t// of an individual pod.\\n174: \\t//\\n175: \\t// Use CSI for light-weight local ephemeral volumes if the CSI driver is meant to\\n176: \\t// be used that way - see the documentation of the driver for\\n177: \\t// more information.\\n178: \\t//\\n179: \\t// A pod can use both types of ephemeral volumes and\\n180: \\t// persistent volumes at the same time.\\n181: \\t//\\n182: \\t// +optional\\n183: \\tEphemeral *EphemeralVolumeSource `json:\\\"ephemeral,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,29,opt,name=ephemeral\\\"`\\n184: }\\n185: \\n186: // PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource references the user's PVC in the same namespace.\\n187: // This volume finds the bound PV and mounts that volume for the pod. A\\n188: // PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource is, essentially, a wrapper around another\\n189: // type of volume that is owned by someone else (the system).\\n190: type PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource struct {\\n191: \\t// claimName is the name of a PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace as the pod using this volume.\\n192: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims\\n193: \\tClaimName string `json:\\\"claimName\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=claimName\\\"`\\n194: \\t// readOnly Will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\\n195: \\t// Default false.\\n196: \\t// +optional\\n197: \\tReadOnly bool `json:\\\"readOnly,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,2,opt,name=readOnly\\\"`\\n198: }\\n199: \\n200: // PersistentVolumeSource is similar to VolumeSource but meant for the\\n201: // administrator who creates PVs. Exactly one of its members must be set.\\n202: type PersistentVolumeSource struct {\\n203: \\t// gcePersistentDisk represents a GCE Disk resource that is attached to a\\n204: \\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod. Provisioned by an admin.\\n205: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk\\n206: \\t// +optional\\n207: \\tGCEPersistentDisk *GCEPersistentDiskVolumeSource `json:\\\"gcePersistentDisk,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=gcePersistentDisk\\\"`\\n208: \\t// awsElasticBlockStore represents an AWS Disk resource that is attached to a\\n209: \\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\n210: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore\\n211: \\t// +optional\\n212: \\tAWSElasticBlockStore *AWSElasticBlockStoreVolumeSource `json:\\\"awsElasticBlockStore,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=awsElasticBlockStore\\\"`\\n213: \\t// hostPath represents a directory on the host.\\n214: \\t// Provisioned by a developer or tester.\\n215: \\t// This is useful for single-node development and testing only!\\n216: \\t// On-host storage is not supported in any way and WILL NOT WORK in a multi-node cluster.\\n217: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath\\n218: \\t// +optional\\n219: \\tHostPath *HostPathVolumeSource `json:\\\"hostPath,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=hostPath\\\"`\\n220: \\t// glusterfs represents a Glusterfs volume that is attached to a host and\\n221: \\t// exposed to the pod. Provisioned by an admin.\\n222: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md\\n223: \\t// +optional\\n224: \\tGlusterfs *GlusterfsPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\"glusterfs,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=glusterfs\\\"`\\n225: \\t// nfs represents an NFS mount on the host. Provisioned by an admin.\\n226: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs\\n227: \\t// +optional\\n228: \\tNFS *NFSVolumeSource `json:\\\"nfs,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=nfs\\\"`\\n229: \\t// rbd represents a Rados Block Device mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime.\\n230: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md\\n231: \\t// +optional\\n232: \\tRBD *RBDPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\"rbd,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=rbd\\\"`\\n233: \\t// iscsi represents an ISCSI Disk resource that is attached to a\\n234: \\t// kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod. Provisioned by an admin.\\n235: \\t// +optional\\n236: \\tISCSI *ISCSIPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\"iscsi,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=iscsi\\\"`\\n237: \\t// cinder represents a cinder volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.\\n238: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md\\n239: \\t// +optional\\n240: \\tCinder *CinderPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\"cinder,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=cinder\\\"`\\n241: \\t// cephFS represents a Ceph FS mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime\\n242: \\t// +optional\\n243: \\tCephFS *CephFSPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\"cephfs,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=cephfs\\\"`\\n244: \\t// fc represents a Fibre Channel resource that is attached to a kubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\\n245: \\t// +optional\\n246: \\tFC *FCVolumeSource `json:\\\"fc,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,10,opt,name=fc\\\"`\\n247: \\t// flocker represents a Flocker volume attached to a kubelet's host machine and exposed to the pod for its usage. protobuf:\\\"bytes,11,opt,name=flocker\\\"`\\n250: \\t// flexVolume represents a generic volume resource that is\\n251: \\t// provisioned/attached using an exec based plugin.\\n252: \\t// \\\"volume.beta.kubernetes.io/storage-class\\\"\\n290: \\n291: \\t// MountOptionAnnotation defines mount option annotation used in PVs\\n292: \\tMountOptionAnnotation = +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n298: \\n299: // PersistentVolume (PV) is a storage resource provisioned by an administrator.\\n300: // It is analogous to a node.\\n301: // More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes\\n302: type PersistentVolume struct {\\n303: \\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n304: \\t// Standard object's metadata.\\n305: \\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\\n306: \\t// +optional\\n307: \\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=metadata\\\"`\\n308: \\n309: \\t// spec defines a specification of a persistent volume owned by the cluster.\\n310: \\t// Provisioned by an administrator.\\n311: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistent-volumes\\n312: \\t// +optional\\n313: \\tSpec PersistentVolumeSpec `json:\\\"spec,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=spec\\\"`\\n314: \\n315: \\t// status represents the current information/status for the persistent volume.\\n316: \\t// Populated by the system.\\n317: \\t// Read-only.\\n318: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistent-volumes\\n319: \\t// +optional\\n320: \\tStatus PersistentVolumeStatus `json:\\\"status,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=status\\\"`\\n321: }\\n322: \\n323: // PersistentVolumeSpec is the specification of a persistent volume.\\n324: type PersistentVolumeSpec struct {\\n325: \\t// capacity is the description of the persistent volume's resources and capacity.\\n326: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#capacity\\n327: \\t// +optional\\n328: \\tCapacity ResourceList `json:\\\"capacity,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=capacity,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\\\"`\\n329: \\t// persistentVolumeSource is the actual volume backing the persistent volume.\\n330: \\tPersistentVolumeSource `json:\\\",inline\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=persistentVolumeSource\\\"`\\n331: \\t// accessModes contains all ways the volume can be mounted.\\n332: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#access-modes\\n333: \\t// +optional\\n334: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n335: \\tAccessModes []PersistentVolumeAccessMode `json:\\\"accessModes,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,rep,name=accessModes,casttype=PersistentVolumeAccessMode\\\"`\\n336: \\t// claimRef is part of a bi-directional binding between PersistentVolume and PersistentVolumeClaim.\\n337: \\t// Expected to be non-nil when bound.\\n338: \\t// claim.VolumeName is the authoritative bind between PV and PVC.\\n339: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#binding\\n340: \\t// +optional\\n341: \\t// +structType=granular\\n342: \\tClaimRef *ObjectReference `json:\\\"claimRef,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=claimRef\\\"`\\n343: \\t// persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy defines what happens to a persistent volume when released from its claim.\\n344: \\t// Valid options are Retain (default for manually created PersistentVolumes), Delete (default\\n345: \\t// for dynamically provisioned PersistentVolumes), and Recycle (deprecated).\\n346: \\t// Recycle must be supported by the volume plugin underlying this PersistentVolume.\\n347: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#reclaiming\\n348: \\t// +optional\\n349: \\tPersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy `json:\\\"persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy,casttype=PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy\\\"`\\n350: \\t// storageClassName is the name of StorageClass to which this persistent volume belongs. Empty value\\n351: \\t// means that this volume does not belong to any StorageClass.\\n352: \\t// +optional\\n353: \\tStorageClassName string `json:\\\"storageClassName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=storageClassName\\\"`\\n354: \\t// mountOptions is the list of mount options, e.g. [\\\"ro\\\", \\\"soft\\\"]. Not validated - mount will\\n355: \\t// simply fail if one is invalid.\\n356: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/#mount-options\\n357: \\t// +optional\\n358: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n359: \\tMountOptions []string `json:\\\"mountOptions,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=mountOptions\\\"`\\n360: \\t// volumeMode defines if a volume is intended to be used with a formatted filesystem\\n361: \\t// or to remain in raw block state. Value of Filesystem is implied when not included in spec.\\n362: \\t// +optional\\n363: \\tVolumeMode *PersistentVolumeMode `json:\\\"volumeMode,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=volumeMode,casttype=PersistentVolumeMode\\\"`\\n364: \\t// nodeAffinity defines constraints that limit what nodes this volume can be accessed from.\\n365: \\t// This field influences the scheduling of pods that use this volume.\\n366: \\t// +optional\\n367: \\tNodeAffinity *VolumeNodeAffinity `json:\\\"nodeAffinity,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=nodeAffinity\\\"`\\n368: \\t// Name of VolumeAttributesClass to which this persistent volume belongs. Empty value\\n369: \\t// is not allowed. When this field is not set, it indicates that this volume does not belong to any\\n370: \\t// VolumeAttributesClass. This field is mutable and can be changed by the CSI driver\\n371: \\t// after a volume has been updated successfully to a new class.\\n372: \\t// For an unbound PersistentVolume, the volumeAttributesClassName will be matched with unbound\\n373: \\t// PersistentVolumeClaims during the binding process.\\n374: \\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling VolumeAttributesClass feature.\\n375: \\t// +featureGate=VolumeAttributesClass\\n376: \\t// +optional\\n377: \\tVolumeAttributesClassName *string `json:\\\"volumeAttributesClassName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,10,opt,name=volumeAttributesClassName\\\"`\\n378: }\\n379: \\n380: // VolumeNodeAffinity defines constraints that limit what nodes this volume can be accessed from.\\n381: type VolumeNodeAffinity struct {\\n382: \\t// required specifies hard node constraints that must be met.\\n383: \\tRequired *NodeSelector `json:\\\"required,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=required\\\"`\\n384: }\\n385: \\n386: // PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy describes a policy for end-of-life maintenance of persistent volumes.\\n387: // +enum\\n388: type PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy string\\n389: \\n390: const (\\n391: \\t// PersistentVolumeReclaimRecycle means the volume will be recycled back into the pool of unbound persistent volumes on release from its claim.\\n392: \\t// The volume plugin must support Recycling.\\n393: \\tPersistentVolumeReclaimRecycle PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy = \\\"Recycle\\\"\\n394: \\t// PersistentVolumeReclaimDelete means the volume will be deleted from Kubernetes on release from its claim.\\n395: \\t// The volume plugin must support Deletion.\\n396: \\tPersistentVolumeReclaimDelete PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy = \\\"Delete\\\"\\n397: \\t// PersistentVolumeReclaimRetain means the volume will be left in its current phase (Released) for manual reclamation by the administrator.\\n398: \\t// The default policy is Retain.\\n399: \\tPersistentVolumeReclaimRetain PersistentVolumeReclaimPolicy = \\\"Retain\\\"\\n400: )\\n401: \\n402: // PersistentVolumeMode describes how a volume is intended to be consumed, either Block or Filesystem.\\n403: // +enum\\n404: type PersistentVolumeMode string\\n405: \\n406: const (\\n407: \\t// PersistentVolumeBlock means the volume will not be formatted with a filesystem and will remain a raw block device.\\n408: \\tPersistentVolumeBlock PersistentVolumeMode = \\\"Block\\\"\\n409: \\t// PersistentVolumeFilesystem means the volume will be or is formatted with a filesystem.\\n410: \\tPersistentVolumeFilesystem PersistentVolumeMode = \\\"Filesystem\\\"\\n411: )\\n412: \\n413: // PersistentVolumeStatus is the current status of a persistent volume.\\n414: type PersistentVolumeStatus struct {\\n415: \\t// phase indicates if a volume is available, bound to a claim, or released by a claim.\\n416: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#phase\\n417: \\t// +optional\\n418: \\tPhase PersistentVolumePhase `json:\\\"phase,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=phase,casttype=PersistentVolumePhase\\\"`\\n419: \\t// message is a human-readable message indicating details about why the volume is in this state.\\n420: \\t// +optional\\n421: \\tMessage string `json:\\\"message,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=message\\\"`\\n422: \\t// reason is a brief CamelCase string that describes any failure and is meant\\n423: \\t// for machine parsing and tidy display in the CLI.\\n424: \\t// +optional\\n425: \\tReason string `json:\\\"reason,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=reason\\\"`\\n426: \\t// lastPhaseTransitionTime is the time the phase transitioned from one to another\\n427: \\t// and automatically resets to current time everytime a volume phase transitions.\\n428: \\t// This is a beta field and requires the PersistentVolumeLastPhaseTransitionTime feature to be enabled (enabled by default).\\n429: \\t// +featureGate=PersistentVolumeLastPhaseTransitionTime\\n430: \\t// +optional\\n431: \\tLastPhaseTransitionTime *metav1.Time `json:\\\"lastPhaseTransitionTime,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=lastPhaseTransitionTime\\\"`\\n432: }\\n433: \\n434: // struct {\\n488: \\t// accessModes contains the desired access modes the volume should have.\\n489: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#access-modes-1\\n490: \\t// +optional\\n491: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n492: \\tAccessModes []PersistentVolumeAccessMode `json:\\\"accessModes,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=accessModes,casttype=PersistentVolumeAccessMode\\\"`\\n493: \\t// selector is a label query over volumes to consider for binding.\\n494: \\t// +optional\\n495: \\tSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\\\"selector,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=selector\\\"`\\n496: \\t// resources represents the minimum resources the volume should have.\\n497: \\t// If RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure feature is enabled users are allowed to specify resource requirements\\n498: \\t// that are lower than previous value but must still be higher than capacity recorded in the\\n499: \\t// status field of the claim.\\n500: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#resources\\n501: \", \"url\": \\\\t// +optional\\\\n505: \\\\tVolumeName string `json:\\\\\\\"volumeName,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=volumeName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n506: \\\\t// storageClassName is the name of the `json:\\\\\\\"storageClassName,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=storageClassName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n510: \\\\t// volumeMode defines what type of volume is required by the claim.\\\\n511: \\\\t// Value of Filesystem is implied when not included in claim spec.\\\\n512: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n513: \\\\tVolumeMode *PersistentVolumeMode `json:\\\\\\\"volumeMode,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=volumeMode,casttype=PersistentVolumeMode\\\\\\\"`\\\\n514: \\\\t// dataSource field can be used to specify either:\\\\n515: \\\\t// * An existing VolumeSnapshot object (snapshot.storage.k8s.io/VolumeSnapshot)\\\\n516: \\\\t// * An existing PVC (PersistentVolumeClaim)\\\\n517: \\\\t// If the provisioner or an external controller can support the specified data source,\\\\n518: \\\\t// it will create a new volume based on the contents of the specified data source.\\\\n519: \\\\t// When the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate is enabled, dataSource contents will be copied to dataSourceRef,\\\\n520: \\\\t// and dataSourceRef contents will be copied to dataSource when dataSourceRef.namespace is not specified.\\\\n521: \\\\t// If the namespace is specified, then dataSourceRef will not be copied to dataSource.\\\\n522: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n523: \\\\tDataSource *TypedLocalObjectReference `json:\\\\\\\"dataSource,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=dataSource\\\\\\\"`\\\\n524: \\\\t// dataSourceRef specifies the object from which to populate the volume with data, if a non-empty\\\\n525: \\\\t// volume is desired. This may be any object from a non-empty API group (non\\\\n526: \\\\t// core object) or a PersistentVolumeClaim object.\\\\n527: \\\\t// When this field is specified, volume binding will only succeed if the type of\\\\n528: \\\\t// the specified object matches some installed volume populator or dynamic\\\\n529: \\\\t// provisioner.\\\\n530: \\\\t// This field will replace the functionality of the dataSource field and as such\\\\n531: \\\\t// if both fields are non-empty, they must have the same value. For backwards\\\\n532: \\\\t// compatibility, when namespace isn't specified in dataSourceRef,\\\\n533: \\\\t// both fields (dataSource and dataSourceRef) will be set to the same\\\\n534: \\\\t// value automatically if one of them is empty and the other is non-empty.\\\\n535: \\\\t// When namespace is specified in dataSourceRef,\\\\n536: \\\\t// dataSource isn't set to the same value and must be empty.\\\\n537: \\\\t// There are three important differences between dataSource and dataSourceRef:\\\\n538: \\\\t// * While dataSource only allows two specific types of objects, dataSourceRef\\\\n539: \\\\t//   allows any non-core object, as well as PersistentVolumeClaim objects.\\\\n540: \\\\t// * While dataSource ignores disallowed values (dropping them), dataSourceRef\\\\n541: \\\\t//   preserves all values, and generates an error if a disallowed value is\\\\n542: \\\\t//   specified.\\\\n543: \\\\t// * While dataSource only allows local objects, dataSourceRef allows objects\\\\n544: \\\\t//   in any namespaces.\\\\n545: \\\\t// (Beta) Using this field requires the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\\\\n546: \\\\t// (Alpha) Using the namespace field of dataSourceRef requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\\\\n547: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n548: \\\\tDataSourceRef *TypedObjectReference `json:\\\\\\\"dataSourceRef,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=dataSourceRef\\\\\\\"`\\\\n549: \\\\t// volumeAttributesClassName may be used to set the VolumeAttributesClass used by this claim.\\\\n550: \\\\t// If specified, the CSI driver will create or update the volume with the attributes defined\\\\n551: \\\\t// in the corresponding VolumeAttributesClass. This has a different purpose than storageClassName,\\\\n552: \\\\t// it can be changed after the claim is created. An empty string value means that no VolumeAttributesClass\\\\n553: \\\\t// will be applied to the claim but it's not allowed to reset this field to empty string once it is set.\\\\n554: \\\\t// If unspecified and the PersistentVolumeClaim is unbound, the default VolumeAttributesClass\\\\n555: \\\\t// will be set by the persistentvolume controller if it exists.\\\\n556: \\\\t// If the resource referred to by volumeAttributesClass does not exist, this PersistentVolumeClaim will be\\\\n557: \\\\t// set to a Pending state, as reflected by the modifyVolumeStatus field, until such as a resource\\\\n558: \\\\t// exists.\\\\n559: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volume-attributes-classes/\\\\n560: \\\\t// (Alpha) Using this field requires the VolumeAttributesClass feature gate to be enabled.\\\\n561: \\\\t// +featureGate=VolumeAttributesClass\\\\n562: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n563: \\\\tVolumeAttributesClassName *string `json:\\\\\\\"volumeAttributesClassName,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=volumeAttributesClassName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n564: }\\\\n565: \\\\n566: type TypedObjectReference struct {\\\\n567: third-party types, APIGroup is required.\\\\n570: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n571: \\\\tAPIGroup *string `json:\\\\\\\"apiGroup\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=apiGroup\\\\\\\"`\\\\n572: \\\\t//
