PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType string\\\\n586: \\\\n587: const (\\\\n588: \\\\t// PersistentVolumeClaimResizing - a user trigger resize of pvc has been started\\\\n589: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimResizing PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType = \\\\\\\"Resizing\\\\\\\"\\\\n590: \\\\t// PersistentVolumeClaimFileSystemResizePending - controller resize is finished and a file system resize is pending on node\\\\n591: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimFileSystemResizePending PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType = \\\\\\\"FileSystemResizePending\\\\\\\"\\\\n592: \\\\n593: \\\\t// Applying the target VolumeAttributesClass encountered an error\\\\n594: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimVolumeModifyVolumeError PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType = \\\\\\\"ModifyVolumeError\\\\\\\"\\\\n595: \\\\t// Volume is being modified\\\\n596: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimVolumeModifyingVolume PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType = \\\\\\\"ModifyingVolume\\\\\\\"\\\\n597: )\\\\n598: \\\\n599: // +enum\\\\n600: // When a controller receives persistentvolume claim update with ClaimResourceStatus for a resource\\\\n601: // that it does not recognizes, then it should ignore that update and let other controllers\\\\n602: // handle it.\\\\n603: type ClaimResourceStatus string\\\\n604: \\\\n605: const (\\\\n606: \\\\t// State set when resize controller starts resizing the volume in control-plane.\\\\n607: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimControllerResizeInProgress ClaimResourceStatus = \\\\\\\"ControllerResizeInProgress\\\\\\\"\\\\n608: \\\\n609: \\\\t// State set when resize has failed in resize controller with a terminal error.\\\\n610: \\\\t// Transient errors such as timeout should not set this status and should leave allocatedResourceStatus\\\\n611: \\\\t// unmodified, so as resize controller can resume the volume expansion.\\\\n612: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimControllerResizeFailed ClaimResourceStatus = \\\\\\\"ControllerResizeFailed\\\\\\\"\\\\n613: \\\\n614: \\\\t// State set when resize controller has finished resizing the volume but further resizing of volume\\\\n615: \\\\t// is needed on the node.\\\\n616: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimNodeResizePending ClaimResourceStatus = \\\\\\\"NodeResizePending\\\\\\\"\\\\n617: \\\\t// State set when kubelet starts resizing the volume.\\\\n618: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimNodeResizeInProgress ClaimResourceStatus = \\\\\\\"NodeResizeInProgress\\\\\\\"\\\\n619: \\\\t// State set when resizing has failed in kubelet with a terminal error. Transient errors don't set NodeResizeFailed\\\\n620: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimNodeResizeFailed ClaimResourceStatus = \\\\\\\"NodeResizeFailed\\\\\\\"\\\\n621: )\\\\n622: \\\\n623: // +enum\\\\n624: // New statuses can be added in the future. Consumers should check for unknown statuses and fail appropriately\\\\n625: type PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus string\\\\n626: \\\\n627: const (\\\\n628: \\\\t// Pending indicates that the PersistentVolumeClaim cannot be modified due to unmet requirements, such as\\\\n629: \\\\t// the specified VolumeAttributesClass not existing\\\\n630: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumePending PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus = \\\\\\\"Pending\\\\\\\"\\\\n631: \\\\t// InProgress indicates that the volume is being modified\\\\n632: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeInProgress PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus = \\\\\\\"InProgress\\\\\\\"\\\\n633: \\\\t// Infeasible indicates that the request has been rejected as invalid by the CSI driver. To\\\\n634: \\\\t// resolve the error, a valid VolumeAttributesClass needs to be specified\\\\n635: \\\\tPersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeInfeasible PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus = \\\\\\\"Infeasible\\\\\\\"\\\\n636: )\\\\n637: \\\\n638: // ModifyVolumeStatus represents the status object of ControllerModifyVolume operation\\\\n639: type ModifyVolumeStatus struct {\\\\n640: \\\\t// targetVolumeAttributesClassName is the name of the VolumeAttributesClass the PVC currently being reconciled\\\\n641: \\\\tTargetVolumeAttributesClassName string `json:\\\\\\\"targetVolumeAttributesClassName,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=targetVolumeAttributesClassName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n642: \\\\t// status is the status of the ControllerModifyVolume operation. It can be in any of following states:\\\\n643: \\\\t//  - Pending\\\\n644: \\\\t//    Pending indicates that the PersistentVolumeClaim cannot be modified due to unmet requirements, such as\\\\n645: \\\\t//    the specified VolumeAttributesClass not existing.\\\\n646: \\\\t//  - InProgress\\\\n647: \\\\t//    InProgress indicates that the volume is being modified.\\\\n648: \\\\t//  - Infeasible\\\\n649: \\\\t//   Infeasible indicates that the request has been rejected as invalid by the CSI driver. To\\\\n650: \\\\t// \\\\t  resolve the error, a valid VolumeAttributesClass needs to be specified.\\\\n651: \\\\t// Note: New statuses can be added in the future. Consumers should check for unknown statuses and fail appropriately.\\\\n652: \\\\tStatus PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus `json:\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=status,casttype=PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus\\\\\\\"`\\\\n653: }\\\\n654: \\\\n655: // PersistentVolumeClaimCondition contains details about state of pvc\\\\n656: type PersistentVolumeClaimCondition struct {\\\\n657: \\\\tType   PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType `json:\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=type,casttype=PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType\\\\\\\"`\\\\n658: \\\\tStatus ConditionStatus                    `json:\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=status,casttype=ConditionStatus\\\\\\\"`\\\\n659: \\\\t// lastProbeTime is the time we probed the condition.\\\\n660: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n661: \\\\tLastProbeTime metav1.Time `json:\\\\\\\"lastProbeTime,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=lastProbeTime\\\\\\\"`\\\\n662: \\\\t// lastTransitionTime is the time the condition transitioned from one status to another.\\\\n663: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n664: \\\\tLastTransitionTime metav1.Time `json:\\\\\\\"lastTransitionTime,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=lastTransitionTime\\\\\\\"`\\\\n665: \\\\t// reason is a unique, this should be a short, machine understandable string that gives the reason\\\\n666: \\\\t// for condition's last transition. If it reports \\\\\\\"Resizing\\\\\\\" that means the underlying\\\\n667: \\\\t// persistent volume is being resized.\\\\n668: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n669: \\\\tReason string `json:\\\\\\\"reason,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=reason\\\\\\\"`\\\\n670: \\\\t// message is the human-readable message indicating details about last transition.\\\\n671: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n672: \\\\tMessage string `json:\\\\\\\"message,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=message\\\\\\\"`\\\\n673: }\\\\n674: \\\\n675: // PersistentVolumeClaimStatus is the current status of a persistent volume claim.\\\\n676: type PersistentVolumeClaimStatus struct {\\\\n677: \\\\t// phase represents the current phase of PersistentVolumeClaim.\\\\n678: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n679: \\\\tPhase PersistentVolumeClaimPhase `json:\\\\\\\"phase,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=phase,casttype=PersistentVolumeClaimPhase\\\\\\\"`\\\\n680: \\\\t// accessModes contains the actual access modes the volume backing the PVC has.\\\\n681: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#access-modes-1\\\\n682: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n683: \\\\t// +listType=atomic\\\\n684: \\\\tAccessModes []PersistentVolumeAccessMode `json:\\\\\\\"accessModes,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=accessModes,casttype=PersistentVolumeAccessMode\\\\\\\"`\\\\n685: \\\\t// capacity represents the actual resources of the underlying volume.\\\\n686: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n687: \\\\tCapacity ResourceList `json:\\\\\\\"capacity,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,rep,name=capacity,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n688: \\\\t// conditions is the current Condition of persistent volume claim. If underlying persistent volume is being\\\\n689: \\\\t// resized then the Condition will be set to 'Resizing'.\\\\n690: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n691: \\\\t// +patchMergeKey=type\\\\n692: \\\\t// +patchStrategy=merge\\\\n693: \\\\t// +listType=map\\\\n694: \\\\t// +listMapKey=type\\\\n695: \\\\tConditions []PersistentVolumeClaimCondition `json:\\\\\\\"conditions,omitempty\\\\\\\" patchStrategy:\\\\\\\"merge\\\\\\\" patchMergeKey:\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,rep,name=conditions\\\\\\\"`\\\\n696: \\\\t// allocatedResources tracks the resources allocated to a PVC including its capacity.\\\\n697: \\\\t// Key names follow standard Kubernetes label syntax. Valid values are either:\\\\n698: \\\\t// \\\\t* Un-prefixed keys:\\\\n699: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\t- storage - the capacity of the volume.\\\\n700: \\\\t//\\\\t* Custom resources must use implementation-defined prefixed names such as \\\\\\\"example.com/my-custom-resource\\\\\\\"\\\\n701: \\\\t// Apart from above values - keys that are unprefixed or have kubernetes.io prefix are considered\\\\n702: \\\\t// reserved and hence may not be used.\\\\n703: \\\\t//\\\\n704: \\\\t// Capacity reported here may be larger than the actual capacity when a volume expansion operation\\\\n705: \\\\t// is requested.\\\\n706: \\\\t// For storage quota, the larger value from allocatedResources and PVC.spec.resources is used.\\\\n707: \\\\t// If allocatedResources is not set, PVC.spec.resources alone is used for quota calculation.\\\\n708: \\\\t// If a volume expansion capacity request is lowered, allocatedResources is only\\\\n709: \\\\t// lowered if there are no expansion operations in progress and if the actual volume capacity\\\\n710: \\\\t// is equal or lower than the requested capacity.\\\\n711: \\\\t//\\\\n712: \\\\t// A controller that receives PVC update with previously unknown resourceName\\\\n713: \\\\t// should ignore the update for the purpose it was designed. For example - a controller that\\\\n714: \\\\t// only is responsible for resizing capacity of the volume, should ignore PVC updates that change other valid\\\\n715: \\\\t// resources associated with PVC.\\\\n716: \\\\t//\\\\n717: \\\\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure feature.\\\\n718: \\\\t// +featureGate=RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure\\\\n719: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n720: \\\\tAllocatedResources ResourceList `json:\\\\\\\"allocatedResources,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,5,rep,name=allocatedResources,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n721: \\\\n722: \\\\t// resizestatus is tombstoned since the field was replaced by allocatedResourceStatus\\\\n723: \\\\t// ResizeStatus *PersistentVolumeClaimResizeStatus `json:\\\\\\\"resizeStatus,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=resizeStatus,casttype=PersistentVolumeClaimResizeStatus\\\\\\\"`\\\\n724: \\\\n725: \\\\t// allocatedResourceStatuses stores status of resource being resized for the given PVC.\\\\n726: \\\\t// Key names follow standard Kubernetes label syntax. Valid values are either:\\\\n727: \\\\t// \\\\t* Un-prefixed keys:\\\\n728: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\t- storage - the capacity of the volume.\\\\n729: \\\\t//\\\\t* Custom resources must use implementation-defined prefixed names such as \\\\\\\"example.com/my-custom-resource\\\\\\\"\\\\n730: \\\\t// Apart from above values - keys that are unprefixed or have kubernetes.io prefix are considered\\\\n731: \\\\t// reserved and hence may not be used.\\\\n732: \\\\t//\\\\n733: \\\\t// ClaimResourceStatus can be in any of following states:\\\\n734: \\\\t//\\\\t- ControllerResizeInProgress:\\\\n735: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\tState set when resize controller starts resizing the volume in control-plane.\\\\n736: \\\\t// \\\\t- ControllerResizeFailed:\\\\n737: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\tState set when resize has failed in resize controller with a terminal error.\\\\n738: \\\\t//\\\\t- NodeResizePending:\\\\n739: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\tState set when resize controller has finished resizing the volume but further resizing of\\\\n740: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\tvolume is needed on the node.\\\\n741: \\\\t//\\\\t- NodeResizeInProgress:\\\\n742: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\tState set when kubelet starts resizing the volume.\\\\n743: \\\\t//\\\\t- NodeResizeFailed:\\\\n744: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\tState set when resizing has failed in kubelet with a terminal error. Transient errors don't set\\\\n745: \\\\t//\\\\t\\\\tNodeResizeFailed.\\\\n746: \\\\t// For example: if expanding a PVC for more capacity - this field can be one of the following states:\\\\n747: \\\\t// \\\\t- pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\\\\\"ControllerResizeInProgress\\\\\\\"\\\\n748: \\\\t//      - pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\\\\\"ControllerResizeFailed\\\\\\\"\\\\n749: \\\\t//      - pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\\\\\"NodeResizePending\\\\\\\"\\\\n750: \\\\t//      - pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\\\\\"NodeResizeInProgress\\\\\\\"\\\\n751: \\\\t//      - pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\\\\\"NodeResizeFailed\\\\\\\"\\\\n752: \\\\t// When this field is not set, it means that no resize operation is in progress for the given PVC.\\\\n753: \\\\t//\\\\n754: \\\\t// A controller that receives PVC update with previously unknown resourceName or ClaimResourceStatus\\\\n755: \\\\t// should ignore the update for the purpose it was designed. For example - a controller that\\\\n756: \\\\t// only is responsible for resizing capacity of the volume, should ignore PVC updates that change other valid\\\\n757: \\\\t// resources associated with PVC.\\\\n758: \\\\t//\\\\n759: \\\\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure feature.\\\\n760: \\\\t// +featureGate=RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure\\\\n761: \\\\t// +mapType=granular\\\\n762: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n763: \\\\tAllocatedResourceStatuses map[ResourceName]ClaimResourceStatus `json:\\\\\\\"allocatedResourceStatuses,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,7,rep,name=allocatedResourceStatuses\\\\\\\"`\\\\n764: \\\\t// currentVolumeAttributesClassName is the current name of the VolumeAttributesClass the PVC is using.\\\\n765: \\\\t// When unset, there is no VolumeAttributeClass applied to this PersistentVolumeClaim\\\\n766: \\\\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling VolumeAttributesClass feature.\\\\n767: \\\\t// +featureGate=VolumeAttributesClass\\\\n768: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n769: \\\\tCurrentVolumeAttributesClassName *string `json:\\\\\\\"currentVolumeAttributesClassName,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=currentVolumeAttributesClassName\\\\\\\"`\\\\n770: \\\\t// ModifyVolumeStatus represents the status object of ControllerModifyVolume operation.\\\\n771: \\\\t// When this is unset, there is no ModifyVolume operation being attempted.\\\\n772: \\\\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling VolumeAttributesClass feature.\\\\n773: \\\\t// +featureGate=VolumeAttributesClass\\\\n774: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n775: \\\\tModifyVolumeStatus *ModifyVolumeStatus `json:\\\\\\\"modifyVolumeStatus,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=modifyVolumeStatus\\\\\\\"`\\\\n776: }\\\\n777: \\\\n778: // +enum\\\\n779: type PersistentVolumeAccessMode string\\\\n780: \\\\n781: const (\\\\n782: \\\\t// can be mounted in read/write mode to exactly 1 host\\\\n783: \\\\tReadWriteOnce PersistentVolumeAccessMode = \\\\\\\"ReadWriteOnce\\\\\\\"\\\\n784: \\\\t// can be mounted in read-only mode to many hosts\\\\n785: \\\\tReadOnlyMany PersistentVolumeAccessMode = \\\\\\\"ReadOnlyMany\\\\\\\"\\\\n786: \\\\t// can be mounted in read/write mode to many hosts\\\\n787: \\\\tReadWriteMany PersistentVolumeAccessMode = \\\\\\\"ReadWriteMany\\\\\\\"\\\\n788: \\\\t// can be mounted in read/write mode to exactly 1 pod\\\\n789: \\\\t// cannot be used in combination with other access modes\\\\n790: \\\\tReadWriteOncePod PersistentVolumeAccessMode = \\\\\\\"ReadWriteOncePod\\\\\\\"\\\\n791: )\\\\n792: \\\\n793: // +enum\\\\n794: type PersistentVolumePhase string\\\\n795: \\\\n796: const (\\\\n797: \\\\t// used for PersistentVolumes that are not available\\\\n798: \\\\tVolumePending PersistentVolumePhase = \\\\\\\"Pending\\\\\\\"\\\\n799: \\\\t// used for PersistentVolumes that are not yet bound\\\\n800: \\\\t// Available volumes are held by the binder and matched to PersistentVolumeClaims\\\\n801: \\\\tVolumeAvailable PersistentVolumePhase = \\\\\\\"Available\\\\\\\"\\\\n802: \\\\t// used for PersistentVolumes that are bound\\\\n803: \\\\tVolumeBound PersistentVolumePhase = \\\\\\\"Bound\\\\\\\"\\\\n804: \\\\t// used for PersistentVolumes where the bound PersistentVolumeClaim was deleted\\\\n805: \\\\t// released volumes must be recycled before becoming available again\\\\n806: \\\\t// this phase is used by the persistent volume claim binder to signal to another process to reclaim the resource\\\\n807: \\\\tVolumeReleased PersistentVolumePhase = \\\\\\\"Released\\\\\\\"\\\\n808: \\\\t// used for PersistentVolumes that failed to be correctly recycled or deleted after being released from a claim\\\\n809: \\\\tVolumeFailed PersistentVolumePhase = \\\\\\\"Failed\\\\\\\"\\\\n810: )\\\\n811: \\\\n812: // +enum\\\\n813: type PersistentVolumeClaimPhase string\\\\n814: \\\\n815: const (\\\\n816: \\\\t// used for PersistentVolumeClaims that are not yet bound\\\\n817: \\\\tClaimPending PersistentVolumeClaimPhase = \\\\\\\"Pending\\\\\\\"\\\\n818: \\\\t// used for PersistentVolumeClaims that are bound\\\\n819: \\\\tClaimBound PersistentVolumeClaimPhase = \\\\\\\"Bound\\\\\\\"\\\\n820: \\\\t// used for PersistentVolumeClaims that lost their underlying\\\\n821: \\\\t// PersistentVolume. The claim was bound to a PersistentVolume and this\\\\n822: \\\\t// volume does not exist any longer and all data on it was lost.\\\\n823: \\\\tClaimLost PersistentVolumeClaimPhase = \\\\\\\"Lost\\\\\\\"\\\\n824: )\\\\n825: \\\\n826: // +enum\\\\n827: type HostPathType string\\\\n828: \\\\n829: const (\\\\n830: \\\\t// For backwards compatible, leave it empty if unset\\\\n831: \\\\tHostPathUnset HostPathType = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n832: \\\\t// If nothing exists at the given path, an empty directory will be created there\\\\n833: \\\\t// as needed with file mode 0755, having the same group and ownership with Kubelet.\\\\n834: \\\\tHostPathDirectoryOrCreate HostPathType = \\\\\\\"DirectoryOrCreate\\\\\\\"\\\\n835: \\\\t// A directory must exist at the given path\\\\n836: \\\\tHostPathDirectory HostPathType = \\\\\\\"Directory\\\\\\\"\\\\n837: \\\\t// If nothing exists at the given path, an empty file will be created there\\\\n838: \\\\t// as needed with file mode 0644, having the same group and ownership with Kubelet.\\\\n839: \\\\tHostPathFileOrCreate HostPathType = \\\\\\\"FileOrCreate\\\\\\\"\\\\n840: \\\\t// A file must exist at the given path\\\\n841: \\\\tHostPathFile HostPathType = \\\\\\\"File\\\\\\\"\\\\n842: \\\\t// A UNIX socket must exist at the given path\\\\n843: \\\\tHostPathSocket HostPathType = \\\\\\\"Socket\\\\\\\"\\\\n844: \\\\t// A character device must exist at the given path\\\\n845: \\\\tHostPathCharDev HostPathType = \\\\\\\"CharDevice\\\\\\\"\\\\n846: \\\\t// A block device must exist at the given path\\\\n847: \\\\tHostPathBlockDev HostPathType = \\\\\\\"BlockDevice\\\\\\\"\\\\n848: )\\\\n849: \\\\n850: // Represents a host path mapped into a pod.\\\\n851: // Host path volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.\\\\n852: type HostPathVolumeSource struct {\\\\n853: \\\\t// path of the directory on the host.\\\\n854: \\\\t// If the path is a symlink, it will follow the link to the real path.\\\\n855: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath\\\\n856: \\\\tPath string `json:\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=path\\\\\\\"`\\\\n857: \\\\t// type for HostPath Volume\\\\n858: \\\\t// Defaults to \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n859: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath\\\\n860: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n861: \\\\tType *HostPathType `json:\\\\\\\"type,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=type\\\\\\\"`\\\\n862: }\\\\n863: \\\\n864: // Represents an empty directory for a pod.\\\\n865: // Empty directory volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.\\\\n866: type EmptyDirVolumeSource struct {\\\\n867: \\\\t// medium represents what type of storage medium should back this directory.\\\\n868: \\\\t// The default is \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\" which means to use the node's default medium.\\\\n869: \\\\t// Must be an empty string (default) or Memory.\\\\n870: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir\\\\n871: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n872: \\\\tMedium StorageMedium `json:\\\\\\\"medium,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=medium,casttype=StorageMedium\\\\\\\"`\\\\n873: \\\\t// sizeLimit is the total amount of local storage required for this EmptyDir volume.\\\\n874: \\\\t// The size limit is also applicable for memory medium.\\\\n875: \\\\t// The maximum usage on memory medium EmptyDir would be the minimum value between\\\\n876: \\\\t// the SizeLimit specified here and the sum of memory limits of all containers in a pod.\\\\n877: \\\\t// The default is nil which means that the limit is undefined.\\\\n878: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir\\\\n879: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n880: \\\\tSizeLimit *resource.Quantity `json:\\\\\\\"sizeLimit,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=sizeLimit\\\\\\\"`\\\\n881: }\\\\n882: \\\\n883: // Represents a Glusterfs mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod.\\\\n884: // Glusterfs volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.\\\\n885: type GlusterfsVolumeSource struct {\\\\n886: \\\\t// endpoints is the endpoint name that details Glusterfs topology.\\\\n887: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\\\n888: \\\\tEndpointsName string `json:\\\\\\\"endpoints\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=endpoints\\\\\\\"`\\\\n889: \\\\n890: \\\\t// path is the Glusterfs volume path.\\\\n891: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\\\n892: \\\\tPath string `json:\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=path\\\\\\\"`\\\\n893: \\\\n894: \\\\t// readOnly here will force the Glusterfs volume to be mounted with read-only permissions.\\\\n895: \\\\t// Defaults to false.\\\\n896: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\\\n897: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n898: \\\\tReadOnly bool `json:\\\\\\\"readOnly,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"varint,3,opt,name=readOnly\\\\\\\"`\\\\n899: }\\\\n900: \\\\n901: // Represents a Glusterfs mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod.\\\\n902: // Glusterfs volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.\\\\n903: type GlusterfsPersistentVolumeSource struct {\\\\n904: \\\\t// endpoints is the endpoint name that details Glusterfs topology.\\\\n905: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\\\n906: \\\\tEndpointsName string `json:\\\\\\\"endpoints\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=endpoints\\\\\\\"`\\\\n907: \\\\n908: \\\\t// path is the Glusterfs volume path.\\\\n909: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\\\n910: \\\\tPath string `json:\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=path\\\\\\\"`\\\\n911: \\\\n912: \\\\t// readOnly here will force the Glusterfs volume to be mounted with read-only permissions.\\\\n913: \\\\t// Defaults to false.\\\\n914: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\\\n915: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n916: \\\\tReadOnly bool `json:\\\\\\\"readOnly,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"varint,3,opt,name=readOnly\\\\\\\"`\\\\n917: \\\\n918: \\\\t// endpointsNamespace is the namespace that contains Glusterfs endpoint.\\\\n919: \\\\t// If this field is empty, the EndpointNamespace defaults to the same namespace as the bound PVC.\\\\n920: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\\\n921: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n922: \\\\tEndpointsNamespace *string `json:\\\\\\\"endpointsNamespace,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=endpointsNamespace\\\\\\\"`\\\\n923: }\\\\n924: \\\\n925: // Represents a Rados Block Device mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod.\\\\n926: // RBD volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.\\\\n927: type RBDVolumeSource struct {\\\\n928: \\\\t// monitors is a collection of Ceph monitors.\\\\n929: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n930: \\\\t// +listType=atomic\\\\n931: \\\\tCephMonitors []string `json:\\\\\\\"monitors\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=monitors\\\\\\\"`\\\\n932: \\\\t// image is the rados image name.\\\\n933: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n934: \\\\tRBDImage string `json:\\\\\\\"image\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=image\\\\\\\"`\\\\n935: \\\\t// fsType is the filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.\\\\n936: \\\\t// Tip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.\\\\n937: \\\\t// Examples: \\\\\\\"ext4\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"xfs\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"ntfs\\\\\\\". Implicitly inferred to be \\\\\\\"ext4\\\\\\\" if unspecified.\\\\n938: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#rbd\\\\n939: \\\\t// TODO: how do we prevent errors in the filesystem from compromising the machine\\\\n940: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n941: \\\\tFSType string `json:\\\\\\\"fsType,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=fsType\\\\\\\"`\\\\n942: \\\\t// pool is the rados pool name.\\\\n943: \\\\t// Default is rbd.\\\\n944: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n945: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n946: \\\\tRBDPool string `json:\\\\\\\"pool,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=pool\\\\\\\"`\\\\n947: \\\\t// user is the rados user name.\\\\n948: \\\\t// Default is admin.\\\\n949: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n950: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n951: \\\\tRadosUser string `json:\\\\\\\"user,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=user\\\\\\\"`\\\\n952: \\\\t// keyring is the path to key ring for RBDUser.\\\\n953: \\\\t// Default is /etc/ceph/keyring.\\\\n954: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n955: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n956: \\\\tKeyring string `json:\\\\\\\"keyring,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=keyring\\\\\\\"`\\\\n957: \\\\t// secretRef is name of the authentication secret for RBDUser. If provided\\\\n958: \\\\t// overrides keyring.\\\\n959: \\\\t// Default is nil.\\\\n960: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n961: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n962: \\\\tSecretRef *LocalObjectReference `json:\\\\\\\"secretRef,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=secretRef\\\\\\\"`\\\\n963: \\\\t// readOnly here will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\\\\n964: \\\\t// Defaults to false.\\\\n965: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n966: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n967: \\\\tReadOnly bool `json:\\\\\\\"readOnly,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"varint,8,opt,name=readOnly\\\\\\\"`\\\\n968: }\\\\n969: \\\\n970: // Represents a Rados Block Device mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod.\\\\n971: // RBD volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.\\\\n972: type RBDPersistentVolumeSource struct {\\\\n973: \\\\t// monitors is a collection of Ceph monitors.\\\\n974: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n975: \\\\t// +listType=atomic\\\\n976: \\\\tCephMonitors []string `json:\\\\\\\"monitors\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=monitors\\\\\\\"`\\\\n977: \\\\t// image is the rados image name.\\\\n978: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n979: \\\\tRBDImage string `json:\\\\\\\"image\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=image\\\\\\\"`\\\\n980: \\\\t// fsType is the filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.\\\\n981: \\\\t// Tip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.\\\\n982: \\\\t// Examples: \\\\\\\"ext4\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"xfs\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"ntfs\\\\\\\". Implicitly inferred to be \\\\\\\"ext4\\\\\\\" if unspecified.\\\\n983: \\\\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#rbd\\\\n984: \\\\t// TODO: how do we prevent errors in the filesystem from compromising the machine\\\\n985: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n986: \\\\tFSType string `json:\\\\\\\"fsType,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=fsType\\\\\\\"`\\\\n987: \\\\t// pool is the rados pool name.\\\\n988: \\\\t// Default is rbd.\\\\n989: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n990: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n991: \\\\tRBDPool string `json:\\\\\\\"pool,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=pool\\\\\\\"`\\\\n992: \\\\t// user is the rados user name.\\\\n993: \\\\t// Default is admin.\\\\n994: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n995: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n996: \\\\tRadosUser string `json:\\\\\\\"user,omitempty\\\\\\\" protobuf:\\\\\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=user\\\\\\\"`\\\\n997: \\\\t// keyring is the path to key ring for RBDUser.\\\\n998: \\\\t// Default is /etc/ceph/keyring.\\\\n999: \\\\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\\\n1000: \\\\t// +optional\\\\n1001: \\\",\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL501-1000\\\"}\", \"annotations\": null, \"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"501: \\t// +optional\\n502: \\tResources VolumeResourceRequirements `json:\\\"resources,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=resources\\\"`\\n503: \\t// volumeName is the binding reference to the PersistentVolume backing this claim.\\n504: \\t// +optional\\n505: \\tVolumeName string `json:\\\"volumeName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=volumeName\\\"`\\n506: \\t// storageClassName is the name of the StorageClass required by the claim.\\n507: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#class-1\\n508: \\t// +optional\\n509: \\tStorageClassName *string `json:\\\"storageClassName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=storageClassName\\\"`\\n510: \\t// volumeMode defines what type of volume is required by the claim.\\n511: \\t// Value of Filesystem is implied when not included in claim spec.\\n512: \\t// +optional\\n513: \\tVolumeMode *PersistentVolumeMode `json:\\\"volumeMode,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=volumeMode,casttype=PersistentVolumeMode\\\"`\\n514: \\t// dataSource field can be used to specify either:\\n515: \\t// * An existing VolumeSnapshot object (snapshot.storage.k8s.io/VolumeSnapshot)\\n516: \\t// * An existing PVC (PersistentVolumeClaim)\\n517: \\t// If the provisioner or an external controller can support the specified data source,\\n518: \\t// it will create a new volume based on the contents of the specified data source.\\n519: \\t// When the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate is enabled, dataSource contents will be copied to dataSourceRef,\\n520: \\t// and dataSourceRef contents will be copied to dataSource when dataSourceRef.namespace is not specified.\\n521: \\t// If the namespace is specified, then dataSourceRef will not be copied to dataSource.\\n522: \\t// +optional\\n523: \\tDataSource *TypedLocalObjectReference `json:\\\"dataSource,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,7,opt,name=dataSource\\\"`\\n524: \\t// dataSourceRef specifies the object from which to populate the volume with data, if a non-empty\\n525: \\t// volume is desired. This may be any object from a non-empty API group (non\\n526: \\t// core object) or a PersistentVolumeClaim object.\\n527: \\t// When this field is specified, volume binding will only succeed if the type of\\n528: \\t// the specified object matches some installed volume populator or dynamic\\n529: \\t// provisioner.\\n530: \\t// This field will replace the functionality of the dataSource field and as such\\n531: \\t// if both fields are non-empty, they must have the same value. For backwards\\n532: \\t// compatibility, when namespace isn't specified in dataSourceRef,\\n533: \\t// both fields (dataSource and dataSourceRef) will be set to the same\\n534: \\t// value automatically if one of them is empty and the other is non-empty.\\n535: \\t// When namespace is specified in dataSourceRef,\\n536: \\t// dataSource isn't set to the same value and must be empty.\\n537: \\t// There are three important differences between dataSource and dataSourceRef:\\n538: \\t// * While dataSource only allows two specific types of objects, dataSourceRef\\n539: \\t//   allows any non-core object, as well as PersistentVolumeClaim objects.\\n540: \\t// * While dataSource ignores disallowed values (dropping them), dataSourceRef\\n541: \\t//   preserves all values, and generates an error if a disallowed value is\\n542: \\t//   specified.\\n543: \\t// * While dataSource only allows local objects, dataSourceRef allows objects\\n544: \\t//   in any namespaces.\\n545: \\t// (Beta) Using this field requires the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\\n546: \\t// (Alpha) Using the namespace field of dataSourceRef requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\\n547: \\t// +optional\\n548: \\tDataSourceRef *TypedObjectReference `json:\\\"dataSourceRef,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=dataSourceRef\\\"`\\n549: \\t// volumeAttributesClassName may be used to set the VolumeAttributesClass used by this claim.\\n550: \\t// If specified, the CSI driver will create or update the volume with the attributes defined\\n551: \\t// in the corresponding VolumeAttributesClass. This has a different purpose than storageClassName,\\n552: \\t// it can be changed after the claim is created. An empty string value means that no VolumeAttributesClass\\n553: \\t// will be applied to the claim but it's not allowed to reset this field to empty string once it is set.\\n554: \\t// If unspecified and the PersistentVolumeClaim is unbound, the default VolumeAttributesClass\\n555: \\t// will be set by the persistentvolume controller if it exists.\\n556: \\t// If the resource referred to by volumeAttributesClass does not exist, this PersistentVolumeClaim will be\\n557: \\t// set to a Pending state, as reflected by the modifyVolumeStatus field, until such as a resource\\n558: \\t// exists.\\n559: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volume-attributes-classes/\\n560: \\t// (Alpha) Using this field requires the VolumeAttributesClass feature gate to be enabled.\\n561: \\t// +featureGate=VolumeAttributesClass\\n562: \\t// +optional\\n563: \\tVolumeAttributesClassName *string `json:\\\"volumeAttributesClassName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=volumeAttributesClassName\\\"`\\n564: }\\n565: \\n566: type TypedObjectReference struct {\\n567: \\t// APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced.\\n568: \\t// If APIGroup is not specified, the specified Kind must be in the core API group.\\n569: \\t// For any other third-party types, APIGroup is required.\\n570: \\t// +optional\\n571: \\tAPIGroup *string `json:\\\"apiGroup\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=apiGroup\\\"`\\n572: \\t// Kind is the type of resource being referenced\\n573: \\tKind string `json:\\\"kind\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=kind\\\"`\\n574: \\t// Name is the name of resource being referenced\\n575: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=name\\\"`\\n576: \\t// Namespace is the namespace of resource being referenced\\n577: \\t// Note that when a namespace is specified, a gateway.networking.k8s.io/ReferenceGrant object is required in the referent namespace to allow that namespace's owner to accept the reference. See the ReferenceGrant documentation for details.\\n578: \\t// (Alpha) This field requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\\n579: \\t// +featureGate=CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource\\n580: \\t// +optional\\n581: \\tNamespace *string `json:\\\"namespace,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=namespace\\\"`\\n582: }\\n583: \\n584: // PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType is a valid value of PersistentVolumeClaimCondition.Type\\n585: type PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType string\\n586: \\n587: const (\\n588: \\t// PersistentVolumeClaimResizing - a user trigger resize of pvc has been started\\n589: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimResizing PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType = \\\"Resizing\\\"\\n590: \\t// PersistentVolumeClaimFileSystemResizePending - controller resize is finished and a file system resize is pending on node\\n591: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimFileSystemResizePending PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType = \\\"FileSystemResizePending\\\"\\n592: \\n593: \\t// Applying the target VolumeAttributesClass encountered an error\\n594: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimVolumeModifyVolumeError PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType = \\\"ModifyVolumeError\\\"\\n595: \\t// Volume is being modified\\n596: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimVolumeModifyingVolume PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType = \\\"ModifyingVolume\\\"\\n597: )\\n598: \\n599: // +enum\\n600: // When a controller receives persistentvolume claim update with ClaimResourceStatus for a resource\\n601: // that it does not recognizes, then it should ignore that update and let other controllers\\n602: // handle it.\\n603: type ClaimResourceStatus string\\n604: \\n605: const (\\n606: \\t// State set when resize controller starts resizing the volume in control-plane.\\n607: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimControllerResizeInProgress ClaimResourceStatus = \\\"ControllerResizeInProgress\\\"\\n608: \\n609: \\t// State set when resize has failed in resize controller with a terminal error.\\n610: \\t// Transient errors such as timeout should not set this status and should leave allocatedResourceStatus\\n611: \\t// unmodified, so as resize controller can resume the volume expansion.\\n612: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimControllerResizeFailed ClaimResourceStatus = \\\"ControllerResizeFailed\\\"\\n613: \\n614: \\t// State set when resize controller has finished resizing the volume but further resizing of volume\\n615: \\t// is needed on the node.\\n616: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimNodeResizePending ClaimResourceStatus = \\\"NodeResizePending\\\"\\n617: \\t// State set when kubelet starts resizing the volume.\\n618: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimNodeResizeInProgress ClaimResourceStatus = \\\"NodeResizeInProgress\\\"\\n619: \\t// State set when resizing has failed in kubelet with a terminal error. Transient errors don't set NodeResizeFailed\\n620: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimNodeResizeFailed ClaimResourceStatus = \\\"NodeResizeFailed\\\"\\n621: )\\n622: \\n623: // +enum\\n624: // New statuses can be added in the future. Consumers should check for unknown statuses and fail appropriately\\n625: type PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus string\\n626: \\n627: const (\\n628: \\t// Pending indicates that the PersistentVolumeClaim cannot be modified due to unmet requirements, such as\\n629: \\t// the specified VolumeAttributesClass not existing\\n630: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumePending PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus = \\\"Pending\\\"\\n631: \\t// InProgress indicates that the volume is being modified\\n632: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeInProgress PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus = \\\"InProgress\\\"\\n633: \\t// Infeasible indicates that the request has been rejected as invalid by the CSI driver. To\\n634: \\t// resolve the error, a valid VolumeAttributesClass needs to be specified\\n635: \\tPersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeInfeasible PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus = \\\"Infeasible\\\"\\n636: )\\n637: \\n638: // ModifyVolumeStatus represents the status object of ControllerModifyVolume operation\\n639: type ModifyVolumeStatus struct {\\n640: \\t// targetVolumeAttributesClassName is the name of the VolumeAttributesClass the PVC currently being reconciled\\n641: \\tTargetVolumeAttributesClassName string `json:\\\"targetVolumeAttributesClassName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=targetVolumeAttributesClassName\\\"`\\n642: \\t// status is the status of the ControllerModifyVolume operation. It can be in any of following states:\\n643: \\t//  - Pending\\n644: \\t//    Pending indicates that the PersistentVolumeClaim cannot be modified due to unmet requirements, such as\\n645: \\t//    the specified VolumeAttributesClass not existing.\\n646: \\t//  - InProgress\\n647: \\t//    InProgress indicates that the volume is being modified.\\n648: \\t//  - Infeasible\\n649: \\t//   Infeasible indicates that the request has been rejected as invalid by the CSI driver. To\\n650: \\t// \\t  resolve the error, a valid VolumeAttributesClass needs to be specified.\\n651: \\t// Note: New statuses can be added in the future. Consumers should check for unknown statuses and fail appropriately.\\n652: \\tStatus PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus `json:\\\"status\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=status,casttype=PersistentVolumeClaimModifyVolumeStatus\\\"`\\n653: }\\n654: \\n655: // PersistentVolumeClaimCondition contains details about state of pvc\\n656: type PersistentVolumeClaimCondition struct {\\n657: \\tType   PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType `json:\\\"type\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=type,casttype=PersistentVolumeClaimConditionType\\\"`\\n658: \\tStatus ConditionStatus                    `json:\\\"status\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=status,casttype=ConditionStatus\\\"`\\n659: \\t// lastProbeTime is the time we probed the condition.\\n660: \\t// +optional\\n661: \\tLastProbeTime metav1.Time `json:\\\"lastProbeTime,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=lastProbeTime\\\"`\\n662: \\t// lastTransitionTime is the time the condition transitioned from one status to another.\\n663: \\t// +optional\\n664: \\tLastTransitionTime metav1.Time `json:\\\"lastTransitionTime,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=lastTransitionTime\\\"`\\n665: \\t// reason is a unique, this should be a short, machine understandable string that gives the reason\\n666: \\t// for condition's last transition. If it reports \\\"Resizing\\\" that means the underlying\\n667: \\t// persistent volume is being resized.\\n668: \\t// +optional\\n669: \\tReason string `json:\\\"reason,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,opt,name=reason\\\"`\\n670: \\t// message is the human-readable message indicating details about last transition.\\n671: \\t// +optional\\n672: \\tMessage string `json:\\\"message,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=message\\\"`\\n673: }\\n674: \\n675: // PersistentVolumeClaimStatus is the current status of a persistent volume claim.\\n676: type PersistentVolumeClaimStatus struct {\\n677: \\t// phase represents the current phase of PersistentVolumeClaim.\\n678: \\t// +optional\\n679: \\tPhase PersistentVolumeClaimPhase `json:\\\"phase,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=phase,casttype=PersistentVolumeClaimPhase\\\"`\\n680: \\t// accessModes contains the actual access modes the volume backing the PVC has.\\n681: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#access-modes-1\\n682: \\t// +optional\\n683: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n684: \\tAccessModes []PersistentVolumeAccessMode `json:\\\"accessModes,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,rep,name=accessModes,casttype=PersistentVolumeAccessMode\\\"`\\n685: \\t// capacity represents the actual resources of the underlying volume.\\n686: \\t// +optional\\n687: \\tCapacity ResourceList `json:\\\"capacity,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,rep,name=capacity,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\\\"`\\n688: \\t// conditions is the current Condition of persistent volume claim. If underlying persistent volume is being\\n689: \\t// resized then the Condition will be set to 'Resizing'.\\n690: \\t// +optional\\n691: \\t// +patchMergeKey=type\\n692: \\t// +patchStrategy=merge\\n693: \\t// +listType=map\\n694: \\t// +listMapKey=type\\n695: \\tConditions []PersistentVolumeClaimCondition `json:\\\"conditions,omitempty\\\" patchStrategy:\\\"merge\\\" patchMergeKey:\\\"type\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,rep,name=conditions\\\"`\\n696: \\t// allocatedResources tracks the resources allocated to a PVC including its capacity.\\n697: \\t// Key names follow standard Kubernetes label syntax. Valid values are either:\\n698: \\t// \\t* Un-prefixed keys:\\n699: \\t//\\t\\t- storage - the capacity of the volume.\\n700: \\t//\\t* Custom resources must use implementation-defined prefixed names such as \\\"example.com/my-custom-resource\\\"\\n701: \\t// Apart from above values - keys that are unprefixed or have kubernetes.io prefix are considered\\n702: \\t// reserved and hence may not be used.\\n703: \\t//\\n704: \\t// Capacity reported here may be larger than the actual capacity when a volume expansion operation\\n705: \\t// is requested.\\n706: \\t// For storage quota, the larger value from allocatedResources and PVC.spec.resources is used.\\n707: \\t// If allocatedResources is not set, PVC.spec.resources alone is used for quota calculation.\\n708: \\t// If a volume expansion capacity request is lowered, allocatedResources is only\\n709: \\t// lowered if there are no expansion operations in progress and if the actual volume capacity\\n710: \\t// is equal or lower than the requested capacity.\\n711: \\t//\\n712: \\t// A controller that receives PVC update with previously unknown resourceName\\n713: \\t// should ignore the update for the purpose it was designed. For example - a controller that\\n714: \\t// only is responsible for resizing capacity of the volume, should ignore PVC updates that change other valid\\n715: \\t// resources associated with PVC.\\n716: \\t//\\n717: \\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure feature.\\n718: \\t// +featureGate=RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure\\n719: \\t// +optional\\n720: \\tAllocatedResources ResourceList `json:\\\"allocatedResources,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,5,rep,name=allocatedResources,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\\\"`\\n721: \\n722: \\t// resizestatus is tombstoned since the field was replaced by allocatedResourceStatus\\n723: \\t// ResizeStatus *PersistentVolumeClaimResizeStatus `json:\\\"resizeStatus,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,6,opt,name=resizeStatus,casttype=PersistentVolumeClaimResizeStatus\\\"`\\n724: \\n725: \\t// allocatedResourceStatuses stores status of resource being resized for the given PVC.\\n726: \\t// Key names follow standard Kubernetes label syntax. Valid values are either:\\n727: \\t// \\t* Un-prefixed keys:\\n728: \\t//\\t\\t- storage - the capacity of the volume.\\n729: \\t//\\t* Custom resources must use implementation-defined prefixed names such as \\\"example.com/my-custom-resource\\\"\\n730: \\t// Apart from above values - keys that are unprefixed or have kubernetes.io prefix are considered\\n731: \\t// reserved and hence may not be used.\\n732: \\t//\\n733: \\t// ClaimResourceStatus can be in any of following states:\\n734: \\t//\\t- ControllerResizeInProgress:\\n735: \\t//\\t\\tState set when resize controller starts resizing the volume in control-plane.\\n736: \\t// \\t- ControllerResizeFailed:\\n737: \\t//\\t\\tState set when resize has failed in resize controller with a terminal error.\\n738: \\t//\\t- NodeResizePending:\\n739: \\t//\\t\\tState set when resize controller has finished resizing the volume but further resizing of\\n740: \\t//\\t\\tvolume is needed on the node.\\n741: \\t//\\t- NodeResizeInProgress:\\n742: \\t//\\t\\tState set when kubelet starts resizing the volume.\\n743: \\t//\\t- NodeResizeFailed:\\n744: \\t//\\t\\tState set when resizing has failed in kubelet with a terminal error. Transient errors don't set\\n745: \\t//\\t\\tNodeResizeFailed.\\n746: \\t// For example: if expanding a PVC for more capacity - this field can be one of the following states:\\n747: \\t// \\t- pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\"ControllerResizeInProgress\\\"\\n748: \\t//      - pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\"ControllerResizeFailed\\\"\\n749: \\t//      - pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\"NodeResizePending\\\"\\n750: \\t//      - pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\"NodeResizeInProgress\\\"\\n751: \\t//      - pvc.status.allocatedResourceStatus['storage'] = \\\"NodeResizeFailed\\\"\\n752: \\t// When this field is not set, it means that no resize operation is in progress for the given PVC.\\n753: \\t//\\n754: \\t// A controller that receives PVC update with previously unknown resourceName or ClaimResourceStatus\\n755: \\t// should ignore the update for the purpose it was designed. For example - a controller that\\n756: \\t// only is responsible for resizing capacity of the volume, should ignore PVC updates that change other valid\\n757: \\t// resources associated with PVC.\\n758: \\t//\\n759: \\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure feature.\\n760: \\t// +featureGate=RecoverVolumeExpansionFailure\\n761: \\t// +mapType=granular\\n762: \\t// +optional\\n763: \\tAllocatedResourceStatuses map[ResourceName]ClaimResourceStatus `json:\\\"allocatedResourceStatuses,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,7,rep,name=allocatedResourceStatuses\\\"`\\n764: \\t// currentVolumeAttributesClassName is the current name of the VolumeAttributesClass the PVC is using.\\n765: \\t// When unset, there is no VolumeAttributeClass applied to this PersistentVolumeClaim\\n766: \\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling VolumeAttributesClass feature.\\n767: \\t// +featureGate=VolumeAttributesClass\\n768: \\t// +optional\\n769: \\tCurrentVolumeAttributesClassName *string `json:\\\"currentVolumeAttributesClassName,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,8,opt,name=currentVolumeAttributesClassName\\\"`\\n770: \\t// ModifyVolumeStatus represents the status object of ControllerModifyVolume operation.\\n771: \\t// When this is unset, there is no ModifyVolume operation being attempted.\\n772: \\t// This is an alpha field and requires enabling VolumeAttributesClass feature.\\n773: \\t// +featureGate=VolumeAttributesClass\\n774: \\t// +optional\\n775: \\tModifyVolumeStatus *ModifyVolumeStatus `json:\\\"modifyVolumeStatus,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,9,opt,name=modifyVolumeStatus\\\"`\\n776: }\\n777: \\n778: // +enum\\n779: type PersistentVolumeAccessMode string\\n780: \\n781: const (\\n782: \\t// can be mounted in read/write mode to exactly 1 host\\n783: \\tReadWriteOnce PersistentVolumeAccessMode = \\\"ReadWriteOnce\\\"\\n784: \\t// can be mounted in read-only mode to many hosts\\n785: \\tReadOnlyMany PersistentVolumeAccessMode = \\\"ReadOnlyMany\\\"\\n786: \\t// can be mounted in read/write mode to many hosts\\n787: \\tReadWriteMany PersistentVolumeAccessMode = \\\"ReadWriteMany\\\"\\n788: \\t// can be mounted in read/write mode to exactly 1 pod\\n789: \\t// cannot be used in combination with other access modes\\n790: \\tReadWriteOncePod PersistentVolumeAccessMode = \\\"ReadWriteOncePod\\\"\\n791: )\\n792: \\n793: // +enum\\n794: type PersistentVolumePhase string\\n795: \\n796: const (\\n797: \\t// used for PersistentVolumes that are not available\\n798: \\tVolumePending PersistentVolumePhase = \\\"Pending\\\"\\n799: \\t// used for PersistentVolumes that are not yet bound\\n800: \\t// Available volumes are held by the binder and matched to PersistentVolumeClaims\\n801: \\tVolumeAvailable PersistentVolumePhase = \\\"Available\\\"\\n802: \\t// used for PersistentVolumes that are bound\\n803: \\tVolumeBound PersistentVolumePhase = \\\"Bound\\\"\\n804: \\t// used for PersistentVolumes where the bound PersistentVolumeClaim was deleted\\n805: \\t// released volumes must be recycled before becoming available again\\n806: \\t// this phase is used by the persistent volume claim binder to signal to another process to reclaim the resource\\n807: \\tVolumeReleased PersistentVolumePhase = \\\"Released\\\"\\n808: \\t// used for PersistentVolumes that failed to be correctly recycled or deleted after being released from a claim\\n809: \\tVolumeFailed PersistentVolumePhase = \\\"Failed\\\"\\n810: )\\n811: \\n812: // +enum\\n813: type PersistentVolumeClaimPhase string\\n814: \\n815: const (\\n816: \\t// used for PersistentVolumeClaims that are not yet bound\\n817: \\tClaimPending PersistentVolumeClaimPhase = \\\"Pending\\\"\\n818: \\t// used for PersistentVolumeClaims that are bound\\n819: \\tClaimBound PersistentVolumeClaimPhase = \\\"Bound\\\"\\n820: \\t// used for PersistentVolumeClaims that lost their underlying\\n821: \\t// PersistentVolume. The claim was bound to a PersistentVolume and this\\n822: \\t// volume does not exist any longer and all data on it was lost.\\n823: \\tClaimLost PersistentVolumeClaimPhase = \\\"Lost\\\"\\n824: )\\n825: \\n826: // +enum\\n827: type HostPathType string\\n828: \\n829: const (\\n830: \\t// For backwards compatible, leave it empty if unset\\n831: \\tHostPathUnset HostPathType = \\\"\\\"\\n832: \\t// If nothing exists at the given path, an empty directory will be created there\\n833: \\t// as needed with file mode 0755, having the same group and ownership with Kubelet.\\n834: \\tHostPathDirectoryOrCreate HostPathType = \\\"DirectoryOrCreate\\\"\\n835: \\t// A directory must exist at the given path\\n836: \\tHostPathDirectory HostPathType = \\\"Directory\\\"\\n837: \\t// If nothing exists at the given path, an empty file will be created there\\n838: \\t// as needed with file mode 0644, having the same group and ownership with Kubelet.\\n839: \\tHostPathFileOrCreate HostPathType = \\\"FileOrCreate\\\"\\n840: \\t// A file must exist at the given path\\n841: \\tHostPathFile HostPathType = \\\"File\\\"\\n842: \\t// A UNIX socket must exist at the given path\\n843: \\tHostPathSocket HostPathType = \\\"Socket\\\"\\n844: \\t// A character device must exist at the given path\\n845: \\tHostPathCharDev HostPathType = \\\"CharDevice\\\"\\n846: \\t// A block device must exist at the given path\\n847: \\tHostPathBlockDev HostPathType = \\\"BlockDevice\\\"\\n848: )\\n849: \\n850: // Represents a host path mapped into a pod.\\n851: // Host path volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.\\n852: type HostPathVolumeSource struct {\\n853: \\t// path of the directory on the host.\\n854: \\t// If the path is a symlink, it will follow the link to the real path.\\n855: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath\\n856: \\tPath string `json:\\\"path\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=path\\\"`\\n857: \\t// type for HostPath Volume\\n858: \\t// Defaults to \\\"\\\"\\n859: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath\\n860: \\t// +optional\\n861: \\tType *HostPathType `json:\\\"type,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=type\\\"`\\n862: }\\n863: \\n864: // Represents an empty directory for a pod.\\n865: // Empty directory volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.\\n866: type EmptyDirVolumeSource struct {\\n867: \\t// medium represents what type of storage medium should back this directory.\\n868: \\t// The default is \\\"\\\" which means to use the node's default medium.\\n869: \\t// Must be an empty string (default) or Memory.\\n870: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir\\n871: \\t// +optional\\n872: \\tMedium StorageMedium `json:\\\"medium,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=medium,casttype=StorageMedium\\\"`\\n873: \\t// sizeLimit is the total amount of local storage required for this EmptyDir volume.\\n874: \\t// The size limit is also applicable for memory medium.\\n875: \\t// The maximum usage on memory medium EmptyDir would be the minimum value between\\n876: \\t// the SizeLimit specified here and the sum of memory limits of all containers in a pod.\\n877: \\t// The default is nil which means that the limit is undefined.\\n878: \\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir\\n879: \\t// +optional\\n880: \\tSizeLimit *resource.Quantity `json:\\\"sizeLimit,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=sizeLimit\\\"`\\n881: }\\n882: \\n883: // Represents a Glusterfs mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod.\\n884: // Glusterfs volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.\\n885: type GlusterfsVolumeSource struct {\\n886: \\t// endpoints is the endpoint name that details Glusterfs topology.\\n887: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\n888: \\tEndpointsName string `json:\\\"endpoints\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=endpoints\\\"`\\n889: \\n890: \\t// path is the Glusterfs volume path.\\n891: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\n892: \\tPath string `json:\\\"path\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=path\\\"`\\n893: \\n894: \\t// readOnly here will force the Glusterfs volume to be mounted with read-only permissions.\\n895: \\t// Defaults to false.\\n896: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\n897: \\t// +optional\\n898: \\tReadOnly bool `json:\\\"readOnly,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,3,opt,name=readOnly\\\"`\\n899: }\\n900: \\n901: // Represents a Glusterfs mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod.\\n902: // Glusterfs volumes do not support ownership management or SELinux relabeling.\\n903: type GlusterfsPersistentVolumeSource struct {\\n904: \\t// endpoints is the endpoint name that details Glusterfs topology.\\n905: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\n906: \\tEndpointsName string `json:\\\"endpoints\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=endpoints\\\"`\\n907: \\n908: \\t// path is the Glusterfs volume path.\\n909: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\n910: \\tPath string `json:\\\"path\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=path\\\"`\\n911: \\n912: \\t// readOnly here will force the Glusterfs volume to be mounted with read-only permissions.\\n913: \\t// Defaults to false.\\n914: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\n915: \\t// +optional\\n916: \\tReadOnly bool `json:\\\"readOnly,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"varint,3,opt,name=readOnly\\\"`\\n917: \\n918: \\t// endpointsNamespace is the namespace that contains Glusterfs endpoint.\\n919: \\t// If this field is empty, the EndpointNamespace defaults to the same namespace as the bound PVC.\\n920: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod\\n921: \\t// +optional\\n922: \\tEndpointsNamespace *string `json:\\\"endpointsNamespace,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=endpointsNamespace\\\"`\\n923: }\\n924: \\n925: // Represents a Rados Block Device mount that lasts the lifetime of a pod.\\n926: // RBD volumes support ownership management and SELinux relabeling.\\n927: type RBDVolumeSource struct {\\n928: \\t// monitors is a collection of Ceph monitors.\\n929: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\n930: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n931: \\tCephMonitors []string `json:\\\"monitors\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,rep,name=monitors\\\"`\\n932: \\t// image is the rados image name.\\n933: \\t// More info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/rbd/README.md#how-to-use-it\\n934: \\tRBDImage string `json:\\\"image\\\"
