A commit search tool.\\nSearch for commits in the repository. Find who made changes, when features were implemented, or track code history.\\n\\nKey features:\\n- Search commit messages for relevant terms\\n- Find commits by specific authors\\n- Search for changes containing specific code\\n- Filter by file paths or repository\\n- Filter by date ranges with before/after\\n- Regex pattern support\\n\\nLogic: All parameter types are combined with AND, but within each parameter type, multiple values use OR.\\nExample: messageTerms=[\\\"bug\\\",\\\"fix\\\"] + authors=[\\\"jane\\\"] finds commits by jane with \\\"bug\\\" OR \\\"fix\\\" in message.\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n    \\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eSearch for commits mentioning 'implement feature' or 'create feature' in the message\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the commit search tool with messageTerms=[\\\"implement feature\\\", \\\"create feature\\\"] repos=[\\\"github.com/myorg/repo\\\"]\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eSearch for commits that added pandas imports\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the commit search tool with contentTerms=[\\\"import pandas\\\"] repos=[\\\"github.com/myorg/repo\\\"]\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n    \\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eFind commits by jane.doe (email jane@doe.com) that changed files in the ui/components directory\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the commit search tool with authors=[\\\"jane.doe\\\", \\\"jane@doe.com\\\", \\\"jane\\\", \\\"doe\\\"] files=[\\\"ui/components/**\\\"] repos=[\\\"github.com/myorg/frontend\\\"]\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n    \\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eFind changes to authentication code in May 2025\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the commit search tool with contentTerms=[\\\"auth\\\"] repos=[\\\"github.com/myorg/auth-service\\\", \\\"github.com/myorg/user-service\\\"] after=\\\"2025-05-01\\\" before=\\\"2025-05-31\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n    \\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eWhat has John been working on for the past month?\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the commit search tool with authors=[\\\"John\\\"] repos=[\\\"github.com/myorg/repo1\\\", \\\"github.com/myorg/repo2\\\"] after=\\\"1 month ago\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003c/examples\\u003e\",\"inputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"after\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"Search for commits after this date. Supports various formats including structured dates (e.g. \\\"YYYY-MM-DD\\\" or \\\"MM/DD/YYYY\\\", or \\\"november 2023\\\") and natural language (e.g. \\\"1 month ago\\\", \\\"last week\\\", or \\\"yesterday\\\")\"},\"authors\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"description\":\"Authors to filter by. Multiple authors will be combined with OR logic.\"},\"before\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"Search for commits before this date. Supports various formats including structured dates (e.g. \\\"YYYY-MM-DD\\\", \\\"MM/DD/YYYY\\\", or \\\"november 2023\\\") and natural language (e.g. \\\"1 month ago\\\", \\\"last week\\\", or \\\"yesterday\\\")\"},\"contentTerms\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"description\":\"Code content terms to search for in the actual changes. Multiple terms will be combined with OR logic.\"},\"count\":{\"type\":\"integer\",\"description\":\"Number of results to return (max 100). Defaults to 50, or 100 with date filters.\"},\"files\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"description\":\"File paths to filter by. Multiple files will be combined with OR logic.\"},\"messageTerms\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"description\":\"Terms to search for in commit messages. Multiple terms will be combined with OR logic by default.\"},\"repos\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"description\":\"REQUIRED: Repositories to search in (e.g., [\\\"github.com/gohugoio/hugo\\\"] or [\\\"repo1\\\", \\\"repo2\\\"]). Multiple repositories will be combined with OR logic.\"},\"useRegex\":{\"type\":\"boolean\",\"description\":\"Use regular expressions for searching (default: false)\"}},\"required\":[\"repos\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"name\":\"commit_search\",\"outputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"commits\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"author\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"commit\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"date\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"message\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"repository\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"url\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"repository\",\"commit\",\"author\",\"date\",\"title\",\"message\",\"url\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},\"limitHit\":{\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"query\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"totalCount\":{\"type\":\"integer\"}},\"required\":[\"query\",\"totalCount\",\"commits\",\"limitHit\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},{\"description\":\"This is a Sourcegraph search tool and is best used with other sourcegraph search tools. A tool that compares changes between two specific revisions in a repository.\\nUse this tool when you need to:\\n- Compare changes between two versions (commits, branches, tags) in a single repository\\n- View specific file changes between revisions in detail\\n- Examine code differences in a pull request, branch or commit\\n- See all files modified between two specific points in a repository\\n\\nTips for effective revision comparison:\\n- Use specific revisions for precise comparisons\\n- For very large diffs, start with a smaller \\\"first\\\" value (e.g., 5) to preview changes\\n- For comparing across branches, use the branch names directly\\n- To see changes from a specific commit, use commitHash~1 as base and commitHash as head\\n- Use \\\"after\\\" with the provided endCursor value to paginate through large diffs\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n    \\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eWhat changed in commit abc123 in the github.com/django/django repository?\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the compare revisions tool with repo=\\\"github.com/django/django\\\" base=\\\"abc123~1\\\" head=\\\"abc123\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n    \\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eCompare the feature-auth branch with main in the github.com/microsoft/vscode repository\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the compare revisions tool with repo=\\\"github.com/microsoft/vscode\\\" base=\\\"main\\\" head=\\\"feature-auth\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n    \\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eShow me all file changes between commits abc123 and def456 in kubernetes\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the compare revisions tool with repo=\\\"github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes\\\" base=\\\"abc123\\\" head=\\\"def456\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n    \\u003cexample\\u003e\\n        \\u003cuser\\u003eWhat files were modified in the last 3 commits on main branch of the rails repo?\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n        \\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the compare revisions tool with repo=\\\"github.com/rails/rails\\\" base=\\\"main~3\\\" head=\\\"main\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n    \\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003c/examples\\u003e\",\"inputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"after\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"Pagination cursor for fetching more results\"},\"base\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The base revision (older version, e.g., \\\"main~5\\\", a commit hash, or a tag)\"},\"first\":{\"type\":\"integer\",\"description\":\"Maximum number of file diffs to return (default: 50, max: 100)\"},\"head\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The head revision (newer version, e.g., \\\"main\\\", a commit hash, or a tag)\"},\"repo\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The repository name to compare revisions in (e.g., \\\"github.com/grafana/loki\\\")\"}},\"required\":[\"repo\",\"base\",\"head\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"name\":\"compare_revisions\",\"outputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"base\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"endCursor\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"files\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"added\":{\"type\":\"integer\"},\"deleted\":{\"type\":\"integer\"},\"hunks\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"body\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"newRange\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"lines\":{\"type\":\"integer\"},\"startLine\":{\"type\":\"integer\"}},\"required\":[\"startLine\",\"lines\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"oldRange\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"lines\":{\"type\":\"integer\"},\"startLine\":{\"type\":\"integer\"}},\"required\":[\"startLine\",\"lines\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"section\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"oldRange\",\"newRange\",\"body\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},\"path\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"path\",\"added\",\"deleted\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},\"hasNextPage\":{\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"head\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"repository\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"totalAdded\":{\"type\":\"integer\"},\"totalDeleted\":{\"type\":\"integer\"},\"totalModified\":{\"type\":\"integer\"}},\"required\":[\"repository\",\"base\",\"head\",\"totalAdded\",\"totalDeleted\",\"totalModified\",\"files\",\"hasNextPage\",\"endCursor\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},{\"description\":\"\\nCreate a new deep search conversation to answer complex questions about your codebase.\\n\\nPowered by an agentic LLM, this deep research tool performs an in-depth investigation of your codebase\\n- Performs comprehensive analysis of your question\\n- Uses multiple search and analysis tools automatically\\n- Provides detailed, well-researched answers with evidence\\n- Generates related follow-up suggestions\\n\\nThe response is a curated summary document optimized for readability, synthesizing findings\\ninto concise, actionable insights rather than raw search results.\\n\\nUse this tool when you need:\\n- Comprehensive analysis of complex technical questions\\n- Multi-step research across one or many remote codebases\\n- Detailed explanations with supporting evidence\\n- Questions that require combining information from multiple sources\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eHow does authentication work in this codebase?\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the deep search tool with question: \\\"How does authentication work in this codebase?\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind all the security vulnerabilities related to user input validation\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the deep search tool with question: \\\"Find all the security vulnerabilities related to user input validation\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eExplain the architecture and data flow of the payment processing system\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the deep search tool with question: \\\"Explain the architecture and data flow of the payment processing system\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003c/examples\\u003e\",\"inputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"question\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The question to research using deep search. Should be detailed and specific about what you want to understand.\"}},\"required\":[\"question\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"name\":\"deepsearch\",\"outputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"answer\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The answer generated by the deep search tool.\"},\"error\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"details\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"kind\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"message\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"description\":\"Any error that occurred during the deep search process.\",\"required\":[\"title\",\"kind\",\"message\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"link\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"A link to the deep search conversation for further exploration.\"},\"note\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"Additional information about the tool execution.\"},\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The title or summary of the deep search result.\"}},\"additionalProperties\":false}},{\"description\":\"This is a Sourcegraph search tool and is best used with other sourcegraph search tools. \\nReads a Deep Search conversation and returns the markdown content of the questions and answers.\\n\\nThe response is formatted as a markdown document containing the full conversation history,\\na curated summary optimized for comprehension.\\n\\nThis tool accepts either:\\n- A full Sourcegraph Deep Search URL (e.g., https://sourcegraph.com/deepsearch/abc123 or https://sourcegraph.com/deepsearch/shared/abc123)\\n- A read token (e.g., abc123-def456-...)\\n\\nUse this tool when you need to:\\n- Read or re-open the results of a Deep Search conversation\\n- Summarize or analyze an existing Deep Search answer\\n- Use a past Deep Search as context for a new question\\n- Share or cite Deep Search findings\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eCan you read this Deep Search result and summarize the main findings about our flaky tests? https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/deepsearch/b875dd16-a58f-4c27-84b8-4b4306e49df7\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003eCalls the deepsearch_read tool with identifier: \\\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/deepsearch/b875dd16-a58f-4c27-84b8-4b4306e49df7\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eLook at the Deep Search I ran about our logging performance issues: https://example.sourcegraph.com/deepsearch/shared/e3c0150a-b7ef-4955-bed4-d5820ca7a70d. What were the key repositories and files it highlighted?\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003eCalls the deepsearch_read tool with identifier: \\\"https://example.sourcegraph.com/deepsearch/shared/e3c0150a-b7ef-4955-bed4-d5820ca7a70d\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eUse this Deep Search e782c115-2dfa-44a5-8a88-2fad218a2fe1 and list the top 5 functions it flagged as risky.\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003eCalls the deepsearch_read tool with identifier: \\\"e782c115-2dfa-44a5-8a88-2fad218a2fe\n[... Observation truncated due to length ...]\nharacters) from each matching file (up to 15 files).\\nIndividual lines longer than 256 characters are truncated.\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind all code in the secret-fellowship organization on Github using log4j\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the keyword search tool with query: \\\"repo:^github.com/secret-fellowship log4j\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind all code in the github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler repo that uses the http.NewRequest function\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the keyword search tool with query: \\\"repo:^github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler$ http.NewRequest\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind Go code in ollama where memory mapping is used on darwin or linux\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the keyword search tool with query: \\\"repo:ollama file:.*.go mmap AND (darwin OR linux)\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind FAQs in all readme files across all repositories\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the keyword search tool with query: \\\"file:README.md FAQ\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind code in bar or foo repos that uses lodash\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the keyword search tool with query: \\\"(repo:foo OR repo:bar) lodash\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind code containing either foo or bar in the dev/docs directory\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the keyword search tool with query: \\\"file:^dev/docs foo OR bar\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind code containing foo in dev/docs directory of the repo bar/bas on branch quz/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the keyword search tool with query: \\\"repo:^bar/bas$ rev:quz file:^dev/docs foo\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/examples\\u003e\\n\",\"inputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"query\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The search query. Can include keywords, regex patterns, and filters.\"}},\"required\":[\"query\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"name\":\"keyword_search\",\"outputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"blocks\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":true}},\"required\":[\"blocks\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},{\"description\":\"This is a Sourcegraph search tool and is best used with other sourcegraph search tools. Lists the files and subdirectories in the workspace in a given directory.\\nDirect subdirectories are included and the path is returned with a trailing slash.\\nThe input path is relative to the root of the repository.\\n\\nLists up to 1000 files or directories. If there are more, the response will be truncated.\\nDirectories are listed first.\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eList all files in the src directory of the repository.\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003eCalls the list files tool with path: \\\"src\\\"\\n\\t\\t[{\\\"path\\\": \\\"app/\\\", \\\"isDirectory\\\": true}, {\\\"path\\\": \\\"DEV.md\\\", \\\"isDirectory\\\": false}]\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003c/examples\\u003e\\n\",\"inputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"path\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The directory path within the workspace to list files from. Defaults to workspace root if not specified.\"},\"repo\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The name of the repository containing the files. For example, \\\"github.com/torvalds/linux\\\".\"},\"revision\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The revision or branch to list files from. If not specified, defaults to the HEAD of the default branch.\"}},\"required\":[\"repo\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"name\":\"list_files\",\"outputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"files\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"isDirectory\":{\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"path\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"path\",\"isDirectory\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}}},\"required\":[\"files\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},{\"description\":\"This is a Sourcegraph search tool and is best used with other sourcegraph search tools. \\nLists repositories that match a search query.\\nYou can use this tool to figure out the repositories the user wants to talk about.\\nReturns repository names, descriptions, and other metadata.\\nBy default, returns up to 50 repositories per page.\\nSupports cursor-based pagination - the response will include cursor information that can be used to fetch the next or previous page of results.\\nUse the \\\"after\\\" parameter with the \\\"endCursor\\\" from the previous response to get the next page, or \\\"before\\\" with \\\"startCursor\\\" to get the previous page.\\n\\nWhen to use this tool:\\n- When you do not know the full name of a repository (e.g. the user asks to search the xyz repo, you can use this tool to get github.com/my-org/xyz)\\n- When you have to find repositories by substring matching (e.g. you want to find all repositories that contain the word \\\"foobar\\\")\\nThe pattern is not a regular expression, but uses substring matching on the name of the repo.\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind repositories in the cool-kids-club org on Github\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003eCalls the list repos tool with query: \\\"github.com/cool-kids-club\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eExplain the foo repository\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003eCalls the list repos tool with query: \\\"foo\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eDo we have repositories named \\\"baz\\\"  and \\\"qux\\\"?\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003eCalls the list repos tool with query \\\"baz\\\", calls the list repos tool with query \\\"qux\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003c/examples\\u003e\",\"inputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"after\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"Cursor to start fetching results after. Use the \\\"endCursor\\\" from the previous response.\"},\"before\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"Cursor to start fetching results before. Use the \\\"startCursor\\\" from the previous response.\"},\"limit\":{\"type\":\"integer\",\"description\":\"Maximum number of repositories to return per page. Defaults to 50.\"},\"query\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"A search query to filter repositories. For example, \\\"django\\\" to find repositories containing that term.\"}},\"required\":[\"query\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"name\":\"list_repos\",\"outputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"pagination\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"endCursor\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"hasNextPage\":{\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"hasPreviousPage\":{\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"startCursor\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"totalCount\":{\"type\":\"integer\"}},\"required\":[\"totalCount\",\"hasNextPage\",\"hasPreviousPage\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"repositories\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"archived\":{\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"description\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"fork\":{\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"name\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"private\":{\"type\":\"boolean\"},\"stars\":{\"type\":\"integer\"}},\"required\":[\"name\",\"description\",\"private\",\"archived\",\"fork\",\"stars\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}}},\"required\":[\"repositories\",\"pagination\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},{\"description\":\"This is a Sourcegraph search tool and is best used with other sourcegraph search tools. \\nA semantic code search tool that helps find relevant code snippets using flexible linguistic matching.\\n\\nUse this tool over keyword_search when you need to:\\n- Find code related to concepts rather than exact matches\\n- Get broader results with more potential matches\\n- Find code when you don't know the exact keyword to search for like symbol (function or variable) names\\n- When the keyword_search tool returns too few results\\n\\nSearch capabilities:\\n- The core matching is also keyword-based, but it's more flexible and can match more terms\\n- Uses stemming and OR binding between terms for broader matching (e.g. \\\"auth impl\\\" matches on either term)\\n- Filters:\\n\\t* repo: to search specific repositories (supports regex) Example: repo:github.com/google will select all repositories with the matching prefix (github.com/google-gemini/cookbook and github.com/google/neuroglancer both match). Use ^ to match the start of the string and $ to match the end of the string to avoid unwanted matches. You can chain multiple repo filters with OR while putting them in parentheses.\\n\\t* file: to search specific file patterns (supports regex) Example: file:README.md will only search files that have README.md in their name. file:.*.ts will search all files with a .ts extension.\\n\\t* rev: to search specific revisions (branches, tags, commits/sha). MUST always be used together with a repo: filter. Example: repo:^foo/bar$ rev:feat/xyz will only search the branch feat/xyz of the repo foo/bar. If not specified, defaults to the HEAD of the default branch.\\n\\nWhen not to use this tool:\\n- When you need exact string matches (use the keyword_search tool instead)\\n- When you're looking for specific function names or variables\\n\\nBest practices:\\n- IMPORTANT: Extract relevant keywords from a natural language query and search with only the keywords - if a user asks \\\"How are database connections created or closed in the codebase?\\\", search with \\\"database connection create close\\\", not \\\"how are database connections created closed\\\"\\n- For complex topics with multiple aspects, do separate searches for each main concept\\n- Use repo: and file: filters to improve result relevance\\n- If results seem off-target, try refining your query to be more specific or use filters to avoid unwanted results\\n- The results are case insensitive, so you should not repeat the same search term with different capitalization\\n\\nReturns the top 3 most relevant code chunks (truncated to 1024 characters) from each matching file (up to 15 files).\\nIndividual lines longer than 256 characters are truncated.\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eFind where event listener handlers are set up in the codebase\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the nls search tool with query: \\\"event listen handler\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eExplain the code that controls the chat view in barfoo repo\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the nls search tool with query: \\\"repo:barfoo$ view controller chat\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eHow are database connections managed in the github.com/django/django repo?\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003ecalls the nls search tool with query: \\\"repo:^github.com/django/django$ database connection manage\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003c/examples\\u003e\\n\",\"inputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"query\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The search query. Can include keywords, regex patterns, and filters.\"}},\"required\":[\"query\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"name\":\"nls_search\",\"outputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"blocks\":{\"type\":\"array\",\"items\":true}},\"required\":[\"blocks\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}},{\"description\":\"This is a Sourcegraph search tool and is best used with other sourcegraph search tools. \\nReads the content of a file in the workspace.\\nReturns the file content as a string.\\nEach line is prefixed with its actual line number from the file. The line numbers are 1-indexed. If a file contains \\\"abc\\\\\\\\ndef\\\", you will receive \\\"1: abc\\\\\\\\n2: def\\\".\\nYou can optionally specify a line range to read only a portion of the file.\\nIf you attempt to read a file that is too large (limit is 128 KB), you will receive an error. In that case, retry with a smaller line range.\\n\\nIM* Closing connection 0\nPORTANT: Use this tool ONLY when you have already located the specific file.\\nBefore using this tool:\\n- ALWAYS verify both the repository and file exist by using list_repos, list_files, keyword_search, or nls_search first\\n- NEVER try to read a file without first confirming it exists at the exact path\\n- NEVER guess file paths or assume common structures like \\\"lib/shared/src\\\" exist - always verify\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\u003cexamples\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eSummarize the readme in the pytorch/torcheval repository\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003e[First checks that the repository exists by calling list_repos]\\n\\t[Then verifies README.md exists using list_files with repo: \\\"pytorch/torcheval\\\"]\\n\\tCalls the read file tool with repo: \\\"pytorch/torcheval\\\", path: \\\"README.md\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eRead the first 100 lines of the code in cli/build.rs in the tokio-rs/tokio repository\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003e[First confirms the file exists using list_files or keyword_search]\\n\\tCalls the read file tool with repo: \\\"tokio-rs/tokio\\\", path: \\\"cli/build.rs\\\", startLine: 1, endLine: 100\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eRead the first 100 lines of the code in cli/build.rs in the tokio-rs/tokio repository at commit 1234567890\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003e[First confirms the file exists using list_files or keyword_search]\\n\\tCalls the read file tool with repo: \\\"tokio-rs/tokio\\\", path: \\\"cli/build.rs\\\", startLine: 1, endLine: 100, revision: \\\"1234567890\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003cexample\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cuser\\u003eRead the first 100 lines of the code in cli/build.rs in the tokio-rs/tokio repository at branch feat/xzy\\u003c/user\\u003e\\n\\t\\u003cresponse\\u003e[First confirms the file exists using list_files or keyword_search]\\n\\tCalls the read file tool with repo: \\\"tokio-rs/tokio\\\", path: \\\"cli/build.rs\\\", startLine: 1, endLine: 100, revision: \\\"feat/xzy\\\"\\u003c/response\\u003e\\n\\u003c/example\\u003e\\n\\u003c/examples\\u003e\",\"inputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"endLine\":{\"type\":\"integer\",\"description\":\"The 1-based line number to end reading at. If not specified, reads to the end of the file.\"},\"path\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The path to the file within the repository.\"},\"repo\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The name of the repository containing the file. For example, \\\"github.com/sveltejs/svelte\\\".\"},\"revision\":{\"type\":\"string\",\"description\":\"The revision to read the file from. If not specified, reads from the default branch. This can be a commit hash or a branch name\"},\"startLine\":{\"type\":\"integer\",\"description\":\"The 1-based line number to start reading from. If not specified, starts from the beginning of the file.\"}},\"required\":[\"repo\",\"path\"],\"additionalProperties\":false},\"name\":\"read_file\",\"outputSchema\":{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"content\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"url\":{\"type\":\"string\"}},\"required\":[\"content\"],\"additionalProperties\":false}}]}}"

Input

{
  "thought": "I can access the Sourcegraph MCP API via HTTP! The endpoint is `http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp`. I can use this to read files from the Sourcegraph API directly from bash.\n\nLet me write a Python script that:\n1. Calls the read_file tool via the MCP API\n2. Reads the large files in chunks\n3. Creates the modified versions\n\nThis will be much more efficient than using the read_file tool through the MCP interface.\n\nLet me write the Python script.\n"
}

"Your thought has been logged."
87. execute_bash @ 2026-03-11T08:13:44.604082

Input

{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/fetch_and_modify.py << 'PYEOF'\nimport json\nimport urllib.request\nimport urllib.error\nimport os\nimport sys\n\nMCP_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp\"\nREPO = \"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\"\n\ndef call_read_file(path, start_line=None, end_line=None):\n    \"\"\"Call the read_file MCP tool.\"\"\"\n    params = {\"repo\": REPO, \"path\": path}\n    if start_line is not None:\n        params[\"startLine\"] = start_line\n    if end_line is not None:\n        params[\"endLine\"] = end_line\n    \n    payload = {\n        \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",\n        \"method\": \"tools/call\",\n        \"id\": 1,\n        \"params\": {\n            \"name\": \"read_file\",\n            \"arguments\": params\n        }\n    }\n    \n    data = json.dumps(payload).encode('utf-8')\n    req = urllib.request.Request(\n        MCP_URL,\n        data=data,\n        headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n        method=\"POST\"\n    )\n    \n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as response:\n            # Read SSE response\n            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')\n            # Parse SSE format: \"event: message\\ndata: {...}\\n\\n\"\n            for line in content.split('\\n'):\n                if line.startswith('data: '):\n                    json_str = line[6:]\n                    result = json.loads(json_str)\n                    if 'result' in result:\n                        # Extract content from the result\n                        tool_result = result['result']\n                        if isinstance(tool_result, dict) and 'content' in tool_result:\n                            for item in tool_result['content']:\n                                if item.get('type') == 'text':\n                                    # Parse the inner JSON\n                                    inner = json.loads(item['text'])\n                                    return inner.get('content', '')\n                    return None\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\n\ndef parse_content(raw_content):\n    \"\"\"Parse the line-prefixed content into a list of lines.\"\"\"\n    lines = []\n    for line in raw_content.split('\\n'):\n        # Each line is prefixed with \"N: \" where N is the line number\n        if ': ' in line:\n            idx = line.index(': ')\n            lines.append(line[idx+2:])\n        else:\n            lines.append(line)\n    return lines\n\ndef read_full_file(path, chunk_size=800):\n    \"\"\"Read a full file in chunks.\"\"\"\n    all_lines = []\n    start = 1\n    \n    while True:\n        end = start + chunk_size - 1\n        print(f\"  Reading lines {start}-{end}...\", file=sys.stderr)\n        content = call_read_file(path, start, end)\n        if not content:\n            break\n        \n        lines = parse_content(content)\n        # Remove the last empty line if present\n        if lines and lines[-1] == '':\n            lines = lines[:-1]\n        \n        all_lines.extend(lines)\n        \n        # Check if we've reached the end\n        if len(lines) < chunk_size:\n            break\n        \n        start = end + 1\n    \n    return all_lines\n\ndef write_file(path, lines):\n    \"\"\"Write lines to a file.\"\"\"\n    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)\n    with open(path, 'w') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines))\n        if lines and not lines[-1].endswith('\\n'):\n            f.write('\\n')\n\n# Test the API\nprint(\"Testing MCP API...\", file=sys.stderr)\ncontent = call_read_file(\"staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\", 3525, 3530)\nif content:\n    print(\"API works!\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(content[:200], file=sys.stderr)\nelse:\n    print(\"API failed!\", file=sys.stderr)\n    sys.exit(1)\n\nPYEOF\npython3 /tmp/fetch_and_modify.py",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"cat > /tmp/fetch_and_modify.py << 'PYEOF'\nimport json\nimport urllib.request\nimport urllib.error\nimport os\nimport sys\nMCP_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp\"\nREPO = \"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\"\ndef call_read_file(path, start_line=None, end_line=None):\n    \"\"\"Call the read_file MCP tool.\"\"\"\n    params = {\"repo\": REPO, \"path\": path}\n    if start_line is not None:\n        params[\"startLine\"] = start_line\n    if end_line is not None:\n        params[\"endLine\"] = end_line\n\n    payload = {\n        \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",\n        \"method\": \"tools/call\",\n        \"id\": 1,\n        \"params\": {\n            \"name\": \"read_file\",\n            \"arguments\": params\n        }\n    }\n\n    data = json.dumps(payload).encode('utf-8')\n    req = urllib.request.Request(\n        MCP_URL,\n        data=data,\n        headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n        method=\"POST\"\n    )\n\n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as response:\n            # Read SSE response\n            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')\n            # Parse SSE format: \"event: message\\ndata: {...}\\n\\n\"\n            for line in content.split('\\n'):\n                if line.startswith('data: '):\n                    json_str = line[6:]\n                    result = json.loads(json_str)\n                    if 'result' in result:\n                        # Extract content from the result\n                        tool_result = result['result']\n                        if isinstance(tool_result, dict) and 'content' in tool_result:\n                            for item in tool_result['content']:\n                                if item.get('type') == 'text':\n                                    # Parse the inner JSON\n                                    inner = json.loads(item['text'])\n                                    return inner.get('content', '')\n                    return None\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"Error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\ndef parse_content(raw_content):\n    \"\"\"Parse the line-prefixed content into a list of lines.\"\"\"\n    lines = []\n    for line in raw_content.split('\\n'):\n        # Each line is prefixed with \"N: \" where N is the line number\n        if ': ' in line:\n            idx = line.index(': ')\n            lines.append(line[idx+2:])\n        else:\n            lines.append(line)\n    return lines\ndef read_full_file(path, chunk_size=800):\n    \"\"\"Read a full file in chunks.\"\"\"\n    all_lines = []\n    start = 1\n\n    while True:\n        end = start + chunk_size - 1\n        print(f\"  Reading lines {start}-{end}...\", file=sys.stderr)\n        content = call_read_file(path, start, end)\n        if not content:\n            break\n\n        lines = parse_content(content)\n        # Remove the last empty line if present\n        if lines and lines[-1] == '':\n            lines = lines[:-1]\n\n        all_lines.extend(lines)\n\n        # Check if we've reached the end\n        if len(lines) < chunk_size:\n            break\n\n        start = end + 1\n\n    return all_lines\ndef write_file(path, lines):\n    \"\"\"Write lines to a file.\"\"\"\n    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)\n    with open(path, 'w') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines))\n        if lines and not lines[-1].endswith('\\n'):\n            f.write('\\n')\n# Test the API\nprint(\"Testing MCP API...\", file=sys.stderr)\ncontent = call_read_file(\"staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\", 3525, 3530)\nif content:\n    print(\"API works!\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(content[:200], file=sys.stderr)\nelse:\n    print(\"API failed!\", file=sys.stderr)\n    sys.exit(1)\nPYEOF\n\n\npython3 /tmp/fetch_and_modify.py\nTesting MCP API...\nError: HTTP Error 403: Forbidden\nAPI failed!"

{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/build_modified_files.py << 'PYEOF'\nimport json\nimport urllib.request\nimport os\nimport sys\n\nMCP_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp\"\nREPO = \"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\"\nWORKSPACE = \"/workspace\"\n\ndef call_read_file(path, start_line=None, end_line=None):\n    params = {\"repo\": REPO, \"path\": path}\n    if start_line is not None:\n        params[\"startLine\"] = start_line\n    if end_line is not None:\n        params[\"endLine\"] = end_line\n    \n    payload = json.dumps({\n        \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",\n        \"method\": \"tools/call\",\n        \"id\": 1,\n        \"params\": {\"name\": \"read_file\", \"arguments\": params}\n    }).encode('utf-8')\n    \n    req = urllib.request.Request(\n        MCP_URL, data=payload,\n        headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"Accept\": \"application/json, text/event-stream\"},\n        method=\"POST\"\n    )\n    \n    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=120) as response:\n        content = response.read().decode('utf-8')\n        for line in content.split('\\n'):\n            if line.startswith('data: '):\n                result = json.loads(line[6:])\n                if 'result' in result:\n                    sc = result['result'].get('structuredContent', {})\n                    return sc.get('content', '')\n    return ''\n\ndef read_file_lines(path, start=1, end=None, chunk=800):\n    \"\"\"Read file and return list of (line_num, line_content) tuples.\"\"\"\n    all_lines = []\n    cur = start\n    while True:\n        e = cur + chunk - 1 if end is None else min(cur + chunk - 1, end)\n        print(f\"  Fetching {path} lines {cur}-{e}...\", file=sys.stderr)\n        raw = call_read_file(path, cur, e)\n        if not raw:\n            break\n        \n        for line in raw.split('\\n'):\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            # Format: \"N: content\"\n            colon_idx = line.index(': ') if ': ' in line else -1\n            if colon_idx > 0:\n                try:\n                    lnum = int(line[:colon_idx])\n                    lcontent = line[colon_idx+2:]\n                    all_lines.append((lnum, lcontent))\n                except ValueError:\n                    pass\n        \n        if not all_lines:\n            break\n        last_num = all_lines[-1][0]\n        if end is not None and last_num >= end:\n            break\n        if last_num < e:\n            break  # reached end of file\n        cur = last_num + 1\n    \n    return all_lines\n\ndef lines_to_text(line_tuples):\n    return '\\n'.join(content for _, content in line_tuples) + '\\n'\n\ndef write_workspace_file(rel_path, text):\n    full_path = os.path.join(WORKSPACE, rel_path)\n    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(full_path), exist_ok=True)\n    with open(full_path, 'w') as f:\n        f.write(text)\n    print(f\"Written: {full_path}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n# ============================================================\n# 1. staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\n#    Insert TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic after line 3527\n# ============================================================\nprint(\"\\n=== Processing staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go ===\", file=sys.stderr)\npath_v1 = \"staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\"\n\n# Read the whole file\nlines_v1 = read_file_lines(path_v1)\n\n# Build modified content\nnew_lines_v1 = []\nfor lnum, lcontent in lines_v1:\n    new_lines_v1.append(lcontent)\n    if lnum == 3527:  # After TaintEffectNoExecute line\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally removes the node from')\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\t// Service EndpointSlices to stop traffic routing to the node.')\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\t// Does not evict existing pods. Enforced by the scheduler and')\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\t// EndpointSlice controller.')\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"')\n\nwrite_workspace_file(path_v1, '\\n'.join(new_lines_v1) + '\\n')\n\n# ============================================================\n# 2. pkg/apis/core/types.go\n#    Insert TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic after line 3185\n# ============================================================\nprint(\"\\n=== Processing pkg/apis/core/types.go ===\", file=sys.stderr)\npath_core = \"pkg/apis/core/types.go\"\n\nlines_core = read_file_lines(path_core)\n\nnew_lines_core = []\nfor lnum, lcontent in lines_core:\n    new_lines_core.append(lcontent)\n    if lnum == 3185:  # After TaintEffectNoExecute line\n        new_lines_core.append('\\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally removes the node from')\n        new_lines_core.append('\\t// Service EndpointSlices to stop traffic routing to the node.')\n        new_lines_core.append('\\t// Does not evict existing pods. Enforced by the scheduler and')\n        new_lines_core.append('\\t// EndpointSlice controller.')\n        new_lines_core.append('\\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"')\n\nwrite_workspace_file(path_core, '\\n'.join(new_lines_core) + '\\n')\n\n# ============================================================\n# 3. pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go\n#    Update validateTaintEffect at lines 3864-3876\n# ============================================================\nprint(\"\\n=== Processing pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go ===\", file=sys.stderr)\npath_val = \"pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go\"\n\nlines_val = read_file_lines(path_val)\n\nnew_lines_val = []\nfor lnum, lcontent in lines_val:\n    if lnum == 3866:\n        # Replace: case core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoExecute:\n        # With: case core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoExecute, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic:\n        new_lines_val.append('\\tcase core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoExecute, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic:')\n    elif lnum == 3869:\n        # Replace the validValues slice to include NoScheduleNoTraffic\n        new_lines_val.append('\\t\\tvalidValues := []core.TaintEffect{')\n    elif lnum == 3872:\n        new_lines_val.append('\\t\\t\\tcore.TaintEffectNoExecute,')\n        new_lines_val.append('\\t\\t\\tcore.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,')\n    else:\n        new_lines_val.append(lcontent)\n\nwrite_workspace_file(path_val, '\\n'.join(new_lines_val) + '\\n')\n\nprint(\"\\nDone!\", file=sys.stderr)\nPYEOF\npython3 /tmp/build_modified_files.py 2>&1",
  "security_risk": "MEDIUM",
  "timeout": 300
}

  "command": "# Check what headers are needed - the curl worked but Python didn't\n# Let me check the curl headers more carefully\ncurl -sv -X POST \"http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp\" \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -H \"Accept: application/json, text/event-stream\" \\\n  -d '{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"method\":\"tools/call\",\"id\":1,\"params\":{\"name\":\"read_file\",\"arguments\":{\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"path\":\"staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\",\"startLine\":3525,\"endLine\":3530}}}' 2>&1 | grep -E \"^[<>]\" | head -20",

  "command": "cat > /tmp/build_modified_files2.py << 'PYEOF'\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport os\nimport sys\n\nMCP_URL = \"http://127.0.0.1:18973/mcp\"\nREPO = \"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\"\nWORKSPACE = \"/workspace\"\n\ndef call_read_file(path, start_line=None, end_line=None):\n    params = {\"repo\": REPO, \"path\": path}\n    if start_line is not None:\n        params[\"startLine\"] = start_line\n    if end_line is not None:\n        params[\"endLine\"] = end_line\n    \n    payload = json.dumps({\n        \"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\",\n        \"method\": \"tools/call\",\n        \"id\": 1,\n        \"params\": {\"name\": \"read_file\", \"arguments\": params}\n    })\n    \n    result = subprocess.run(\n        [\"curl\", \"-s\", \"-X\", \"POST\", MCP_URL,\n         \"-H\", \"Content-Type: application/json\",\n         \"-H\", \"Accept: application/json, text/event-stream\",\n         \"-d\", payload],\n        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120\n    )\n    \n    for line in result.stdout.split('\\n'):\n        if line.startswith('data: '):\n            data = json.loads(line[6:])\n            if 'result' in data:\n                sc = data['result'].get('structuredContent', {})\n                return sc.get('content', '')\n    return ''\n\ndef read_file_lines(path, chunk=800):\n    \"\"\"Read file and return list of (line_num, line_content) tuples.\"\"\"\n    all_lines = []\n    cur = 1\n    while True:\n        e = cur + chunk - 1\n        print(f\"  Fetching {path} lines {cur}-{e}...\", file=sys.stderr)\n        raw = call_read_file(path, cur, e)\n        if not raw:\n            break\n        \n        chunk_lines = []\n        for line in raw.split('\\n'):\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            if ': ' in line:\n                colon_idx = line.index(': ')\n                try:\n                    lnum = int(line[:colon_idx])\n                    lcontent = line[colon_idx+2:]\n                    chunk_lines.append((lnum, lcontent))\n                except ValueError:\n                    pass\n        \n        if not chunk_lines:\n            break\n        \n        all_lines.extend(chunk_lines)\n        last_num = chunk_lines[-1][0]\n        \n        if last_num < e:\n            break  # reached end of file\n        cur = last_num + 1\n    \n    return all_lines\n\ndef write_workspace_file(rel_path, lines):\n    full_path = os.path.join(WORKSPACE, rel_path)\n    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(full_path), exist_ok=True)\n    with open(full_path, 'w') as f:\n        f.write('\\n'.join(lines) + '\\n')\n    print(f\"Written: {full_path} ({len(lines)} lines)\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n# ============================================================\n# 1. staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\n# ============================================================\nprint(\"\\n=== Processing staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go ===\", file=sys.stderr)\npath_v1 = \"staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go\"\nlines_v1 = read_file_lines(path_v1)\n\nnew_lines_v1 = []\nfor lnum, lcontent in lines_v1:\n    new_lines_v1.append(lcontent)\n    if lnum == 3527:  # After TaintEffectNoExecute\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally removes the node from')\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\t// Service EndpointSlices to stop traffic routing to the node.')\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\t// Does not evict existing pods. Enforced by the scheduler and')\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\t// EndpointSlice controller.')\n        new_lines_v1.append('\\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"')\n\nwrite_workspace_file(path_v1, new_lines_v1)\n\n# ============================================================\n# 2. pkg/apis/core/types.go\n# ============================================================\nprint(\"\\n=== Processing pkg/apis/core/types.go ===\", file=sys.stderr)\npath_core = \"pkg/apis/core/types.go\"\nlines_core = read_file_lines(path_core)\n\nnew_lines_core = []\nfor lnum, lcontent in lines_core:\n    new_lines_core.append(lcontent)\n    if lnum == 3185:  # After TaintEffectNoExecute\n        new_lines_core.append('\\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally removes the node from')\n        new_lines_core.append('\\t// Service EndpointSlices to stop traffic routing to the node.')\n        new_lines_core.append('\\t// Does not evict existing pods. Enforced by the scheduler and')\n        new_lines_core.append('\\t// EndpointSlice controller.')\n        new_lines_core.append('\\tTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"')\n\nwrite_workspace_file(path_core, new_lines_core)\n\n# ============================================================\n# 3. pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go\n# ============================================================\nprint(\"\\n=== Processing pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go ===\", file=sys.stderr)\npath_val = \"pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go\"\nlines_val = read_file_lines(path_val)\n\nnew_lines_val = []\nfor lnum, lcontent in lines_val:\n    if lnum == 3866:\n        new_lines_val.append('\\tcase core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoExecute, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic:')\n    elif lnum == 3872:\n        new_lines_val.append('\\t\\t\\tcore.TaintEffectNoExecute,')\n        new_lines_val.append('\\t\\t\\tcore.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,')\n    else:\n        new_lines_val.append(lcontent)\n\nwrite_workspace_file(path_val, new_lines_val)\n\nprint(\"\\nAll done!\", file=sys.stderr)\nPYEOF\npython3 /tmp/build_modified_files2.py 2>&1",
  "security_risk": "MEDIUM",
  "timeout": 300
}

"// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n        // Currently enforced by NodeController.\n        TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n        // Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally removes the node from\n        // Service EndpointSlices to stop traffic routing to the node.\n        // Does not evict existing pods. Enforced by the scheduler and\n        // EndpointSlice controller.\n        TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n)\n\n// The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n// the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\ntype Toleration struct {\n        // Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n        // If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n        // +optional"

"// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n        // Currently enforced by NodeController.\n        TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n        // Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally removes the node from\n        // Service EndpointSlices to stop traffic routing to the node.\n        // Does not evict existing pods. Enforced by the scheduler and\n        // EndpointSlice controller.\n        TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n)\n\n// Toleration represents the toleration object that can be attached to a pod.\n// The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n// the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\ntype Toleration struct {\n        // Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n        // If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n---\n        allErrors := field.ErrorList{}\n        switch *effect {\n        // TODO: Replace next line with subsequent commented-out line when implement TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit.\n        case core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoExecute, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic:\n                // case core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit, core.TaintEffectNoExecute:\n        default:\n                validValues := []core.TaintEffect{\n                        core.TaintEffectNoSchedule,\n                        core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule,\n                        core.TaintEffectNoExecute,\n                        core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n                        // TODO: Uncomment this block when implement TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit.\n                        // core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit,\n                }\n                allErrors = append(allErrors, field.NotSupported(fldPath, *effect, validValues))\n        }\n        return allErrors\n}"

{
  "command": "sed -n '205,220p' /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go && echo \"---\" && sed -n '655,670p' /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"continue\n                        }\n                }\n\n                // Skip pods on nodes tainted with NoScheduleNoTraffic: the node is being\n                // drained from traffic routing while keeping existing pods running.\n                if node != nil && nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(node) {\n                        logger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node has NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n                        continue\n                }\n\n                endpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n                if len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n                        desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n                }\n        }\n---\n}\n\n// nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint returns true if the node has a taint with\n// effect NoScheduleNoTraffic, indicating that the node should be removed from\n// service EndpointSlices while keeping existing pods running.\nfunc nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(node *corev1.Node) bool {\n        for _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n                if taint.Effect == corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n                        return true\n                }\n        }\n        return false\n}"

{
  "command": "cat /workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"/*\nCopyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage helper\n\nimport v1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\n// DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n// filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\nfunc DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n        return func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n                // PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule, NoScheduleNoTraffic and NoExecute taints.\n                return t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic\n        }\n}"

{
  "command": "cat /workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint_test.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"/*\nCopyright 2022 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage helper\n\nimport (\n        \"testing\"\n\n        v1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n)\n\nfunc TestDoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc(t *testing.T) {\n        tests := []struct {\n                name     string\n                taint    *v1.Taint\n                expected bool\n        }{\n                {\n                        name: \"should include the taints with NoSchedule effect\",\n                        taint: &v1.Taint{\n                                Effect: v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule,\n                        },\n                        expected: true,\n                },\n                {\n                        name: \"should include the taints with NoExecute effect\",\n                        taint: &v1.Taint{\n                                Effect: v1.TaintEffectNoExecute,\n                        },\n                        expected: true,\n                },\n                {\n                        name: \"should not include the taints with PreferNoSchedule effect\",\n                        taint: &v1.Taint{\n                                Effect: v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule,\n                        },\n                        expected: false,\n                },\n                {\n                        name: \"should include the taints with NoScheduleNoTraffic effect\",\n                        taint: &v1.Taint{\n                                Effect: v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n                        },\n                        expected: true,\n                },\n        }\n\n        filterPredicate := DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc()\n\n        for i := range tests {\n                test := tests[i]\n                t.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n                        if got := filterPredicate(test.taint); got != test.expected {\n                                t.Errorf(\"unexpected result, expected %v but got %v\", test.expected, got)\n                        }\n                })\n        }\n}"
