Input

{
  "command": "cat /workspace/pkg/util/taints/taints.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\n// package taints implements utilities for working with taints\npackage taints\n\nimport (\n        \"fmt\"\n        \"strings\"\n\n        v1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n        \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n        \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation\"\n        \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/helper\"\n)\n\nconst (\n        MODIFIED  = \"modified\"\n        TAINTED   = \"tainted\"\n        UNTAINTED = \"untainted\"\n)\n\n// parseTaint parses a taint from a string, whose form must be either\n// '<key>=<value>:<effect>', '<key>:<effect>', or '<key>'.\nfunc parseTaint(st string) (v1.Taint, error) {\n        var taint v1.Taint\n\n        var key string\n        var value string\n        var effect v1.TaintEffect\n\n        parts := strings.Split(st, \":\")\n        switch len(parts) {\n        case 1:\n                key = parts[0]\n        case 2:\n                effect = v1.TaintEffect(parts[1])\n                if err := validateTaintEffect(effect); err != nil {\n                        return taint, err\n                }\n\n                partsKV := strings.Split(parts[0], \"=\")\n                if len(partsKV) > 2 {\n                        return taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n                }\n                key = partsKV[0]\n                if len(partsKV) == 2 {\n                        value = partsKV[1]\n                        if errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(value); len(errs) > 0 {\n                                return taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n                        }\n                }\n        default:\n                return taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n        }\n\n        if errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(key); len(errs) > 0 {\n                return taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n        }\n\n        taint.Key = key\n        taint.Value = value\n        taint.Effect = effect\n\n        return taint, nil\n}\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect v1.TaintEffect) error {\n        if effect != v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoExecute && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n                return fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\n        }\n\n        return nil\n}\n\n// ParseTaints takes a spec which is an array and creates slices for new taints to be added, taints to be deleted.\n// It also validates the spec. For example, the form `<key>` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\nfunc ParseTaints(spec []string) ([]v1.Taint, []v1.Taint, error) {\n        var taints, taintsToRemove []v1.Taint\n        uniqueTaints := map[v1.TaintEffect]sets.String{}\n\n        for _, taintSpec := range spec {\n                if strings.HasSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\") {\n                        taintToRemove, err := parseTaint(strings.TrimSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\"))\n                        if err != nil {\n                                return nil, nil, err\n                        }\n                        taintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, v1.Taint{Key: taintToRemove.Key, Effect: taintToRemove.Effect})\n                } else {\n                        newTaint, err := parseTaint(taintSpec)\n                        if err != nil {\n                                return nil, nil, err\n                        }\n                        // validate that the taint has an effect, which is required to add the taint\n                        if len(newTaint.Effect) == 0 {\n                                return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", taintSpec)\n                        }\n                        // validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\n                        if len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Has(newTaint.Key) {\n                                return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"duplicated taints with the same key and effect: %v\", newTaint)\n                        }\n                        // add taint to existingTaints for uniqueness check\n                        if len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) == 0 {\n                                uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect] = sets.String{}\n                        }\n                        uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Insert(newTaint.Key)\n\n                        taints = append(taints, newTaint)\n                }\n        }\n        return taints, taintsToRemove, nil\n}\n\n// CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\nfunc CheckIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []v1.Taint, taints []v1.Taint) string {\n        var existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\n        for _, taint := range taints {\n                for _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n                        if taint.Key == oldTaint.Key && taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\n                                existingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, taint.Key)\n                        }\n                }\n        }\n        return strings.Join(existingTaintList, \",\")\n}\n\n// DeleteTaintsByKey removes all the taints that have the same key to given taintKey\nfunc DeleteTaintsByKey(taints []v1.Taint, taintKey string) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\n        newTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n        deleted := false\n        for i := range taints {\n                if taintKey == taints[i].Key {\n                        deleted = true\n                        continue\n                }\n                newTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n        }\n        return newTaints, deleted\n}\n\n// DeleteTaint removes all the taints that have the same key and effect to given taintToDelete.\nfunc DeleteTaint(taints []v1.Taint, taintToDelete *v1.Taint) ([]v1.Taint, bool) {\n        newTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n        deleted := false\n        for i := range taints {\n                if taintToDelete.MatchTaint(&taints[i]) {\n                        deleted = true\n                        continue\n                }\n                newTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n        }\n        return newTaints, deleted\n}\n\n// RemoveTaint tries to remove a taint from annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\n// false otherwise.\nfunc RemoveTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\n        newNode := node.DeepCopy()\n        nodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\n        if len(nodeTaints) == 0 {\n                return newNode, false, nil\n        }\n\n        if !TaintExists(nodeTaints, taint) {\n                return newNode, false, nil\n        }\n\n        newTaints, _ := DeleteTaint(nodeTaints, taint)\n        newNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\n        return newNode, true, nil\n}\n\n// AddOrUpdateTaint tries to add a taint to annotations list. Returns a new copy of updated Node and true if something was updated\n// false otherwise.\nfunc AddOrUpdateTaint(node *v1.Node, taint *v1.Taint) (*v1.Node, bool, error) {\n        newNode := node.DeepCopy()\n        nodeTaints := newNode.Spec.Taints\n\n        var newTaints []v1.Taint\n        updated := false\n        for i := range nodeTaints {\n                if taint.MatchTaint(&nodeTaints[i]) {\n                        if helper.Semantic.DeepEqual(*taint, nodeTaints[i]) {\n                                return newNode, false, nil\n                        }\n                        newTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\n                        updated = true\n                        continue\n                }\n\n                newTaints = append(newTaints, nodeTaints[i])\n        }\n\n        if !updated {\n                newTaints = append(newTaints, *taint)\n        }\n\n        newNode.Spec.Taints = newTaints\n        return newNode, true, nil\n}\n\n// TaintExists checks if the given taint exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\nfunc TaintExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintToFind *v1.Taint) bool {\n        for _, taint := range taints {\n                if taint.MatchTaint(taintToFind) {\n                        return true\n                }\n        }\n        return false\n}\n\n// TaintKeyExists checks if the given taint key exists in list of taints. Returns true if exists false otherwise.\nfunc TaintKeyExists(taints []v1.Taint, taintKeyToMatch string) bool {\n        for _, taint := range taints {\n                if taint.Key == taintKeyToMatch {\n                        return true\n                }\n        }\n        return false\n}\n\n// TaintSetDiff finds the difference between two taint slices and\n// returns all new and removed elements of the new slice relative to the old slice.\n// for example:\n// input: taintsNew=[a b] taintsOld=[a c]\n// output: taintsToAdd=[b] taintsToRemove=[c]\nfunc TaintSetDiff(taintsNew, taintsOld []v1.Taint) (taintsToAdd []*v1.Taint, taintsToRemove []*v1.Taint) {\n        for _, taint := range taintsNew {\n                if !TaintExists(taintsOld, &taint) {\n                        t := taint\n                        taintsToAdd = append(taintsToAdd, &t)\n                }\n        }\n\n        for _, taint := range taintsOld {\n                if !TaintExists(taintsNew, &taint) {\n                        t := taint\n                        taintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, &t)\n                }\n        }\n\n        return\n}\n\n// TaintSetFilter filters from the taint slice according to the passed fn function to get the filtered taint slice.\nfunc TaintSetFilter(taints []v1.Taint, fn func(*v1.Taint) bool) []v1.Taint {\n        res := []v1.Taint{}\n\n        for _, taint := range taints {\n                if fn(&taint) {\n                        res = append(res, taint)\n                }\n        }\n\n        return res\n}\n\n// CheckTaintValidation checks if the given taint is valid.\n// Returns error if the given taint is invalid.\nfunc CheckTaintValidation(taint v1.Taint) error {\n        if errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(taint.Key); len(errs) > 0 {\n                return fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint key: %s\", strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n        }\n        if taint.Value != \"\" {\n                if errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(taint.Value); len(errs) > 0 {\n                        return fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint value: %s\", strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n                }\n        }\n        if taint.Effect != \"\" {\n                if err := validateTaintEffect(taint.Effect); err != nil {\n                        return err\n                }\n        }\n\n        return nil\n}"
105. execute_bash @ 2026-03-11T08:16:16.967808

Input

{
  "command": "cat /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go",
  "security_risk": "LOW"
}

Output

"/*\nCopyright 2018 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\n// Package taints implements utilites for working with taints\npackage taint\n\nimport (\n        \"fmt\"\n        \"strings\"\n\n        corev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n        utilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n        \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n        \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation\"\n)\n\n// Exported taint constant strings\nconst (\n        MODIFIED  = \"modified\"\n        TAINTED   = \"tainted\"\n        UNTAINTED = \"untainted\"\n)\n\n// parseTaints takes a spec which is an array and creates slices for new taints to be added, taints to be deleted.\n// It also validates the spec. For example, the form `<key>` may be used to remove a taint, but not to add one.\nfunc parseTaints(spec []string) ([]corev1.Taint, []corev1.Taint, error) {\n        var taints, taintsToRemove []corev1.Taint\n        uniqueTaints := map[corev1.TaintEffect]sets.String{}\n\n        for _, taintSpec := range spec {\n                if strings.HasSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\") {\n                        taintToRemove, err := parseTaint(strings.TrimSuffix(taintSpec, \"-\"))\n                        if err != nil {\n                                return nil, nil, err\n                        }\n                        taintsToRemove = append(taintsToRemove, corev1.Taint{Key: taintToRemove.Key, Effect: taintToRemove.Effect})\n                } else {\n                        newTaint, err := parseTaint(taintSpec)\n                        if err != nil {\n                                return nil, nil, err\n                        }\n                        // validate that the taint has an effect, which is required to add the taint\n                        if len(newTaint.Effect) == 0 {\n                                return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", taintSpec)\n                        }\n                        // validate if taint is unique by <key, effect>\n                        if len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) > 0 && uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Has(newTaint.Key) {\n                                return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"duplicated taints with the same key and effect: %v\", newTaint)\n                        }\n                        // add taint to existingTaints for uniqueness check\n                        if len(uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect]) == 0 {\n                                uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect] = sets.String{}\n                        }\n                        uniqueTaints[newTaint.Effect].Insert(newTaint.Key)\n\n                        taints = append(taints, newTaint)\n                }\n        }\n        return taints, taintsToRemove, nil\n}\n\n// parseTaint parses a taint from a string, whose form must be either\n// '<key>=<value>:<effect>', '<key>:<effect>', or '<key>'.\nfunc parseTaint(st string) (corev1.Taint, error) {\n        var taint corev1.Taint\n\n        var key string\n        var value string\n        var effect corev1.TaintEffect\n\n        parts := strings.Split(st, \":\")\n        switch len(parts) {\n        case 1:\n                key = parts[0]\n        case 2:\n                effect = corev1.TaintEffect(parts[1])\n                if err := validateTaintEffect(effect); err != nil {\n                        return taint, err\n                }\n\n                partsKV := strings.Split(parts[0], \"=\")\n                if len(partsKV) > 2 {\n                        return taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n                }\n                key = partsKV[0]\n                if len(partsKV) == 2 {\n                        value = partsKV[1]\n                        if errs := validation.IsValidLabelValue(value); len(errs) > 0 {\n                                return taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n                        }\n                }\n        default:\n                return taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v\", st)\n        }\n\n        if errs := validation.IsQualifiedName(key); len(errs) > 0 {\n                return taint, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint spec: %v, %s\", st, strings.Join(errs, \"; \"))\n        }\n\n        taint.Key = key\n        taint.Value = value\n        taint.Effect = effect\n\n        return taint, nil\n}\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect corev1.TaintEffect) error {\n        if effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoExecute && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n                return fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\n        }\n\n        return nil\n}\n\n// reorganizeTaints returns the updated set of taints, taking into account old taints that were not updated,\n// old taints that were updated, old taints that were deleted, and new taints.\nfunc reorganizeTaints(node *corev1.Node, overwrite bool, taintsToAdd []corev1.Taint, taintsToRemove []corev1.Taint) (string, []corev1.Taint, error) {\n        newTaints := append([]corev1.Taint{}, taintsToAdd...)\n        oldTaints := node.Spec.Taints\n        // add taints that already existing but not updated to newTaints\n        added := addTaints(oldTaints, &newTaints)\n        allErrs, deleted := deleteTaints(taintsToRemove, &newTaints)\n        if (added && deleted) || overwrite {\n                return MODIFIED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n        } else if added {\n                return TAINTED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n        }\n        return UNTAINTED, newTaints, utilerrors.NewAggregate(allErrs)\n}\n\n// deleteTaints deletes the given taints from the node's taintlist.\nfunc deleteTaints(taintsToRemove []corev1.Taint, newTaints *[]corev1.Taint) ([]error, bool) {\n        allErrs := []error{}\n        var removed bool\n        for _, taintToRemove := range taintsToRemove {\n                if len(taintToRemove.Effect) > 0 {\n                        *newTaints, removed = deleteTaint(*newTaints, &taintToRemove)\n                } else {\n                        *newTaints, removed = deleteTaintsByKey(*newTaints, taintToRemove.Key)\n                }\n                if !removed {\n                        allErrs = append(allErrs, fmt.Errorf(\"taint %q not found\", taintToRemove.ToString()))\n                }\n        }\n        return allErrs, removed\n}\n\n// addTaints adds the newTaints list to existing ones and updates the newTaints List.\n// TODO: This needs a rewrite to take only the new values instead of appended newTaints list to be consistent.\nfunc addTaints(oldTaints []corev1.Taint, newTaints *[]corev1.Taint) bool {\n        for _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n                existsInNew := false\n                for _, taint := range *newTaints {\n                        if taint.MatchTaint(&oldTaint) {\n                                existsInNew = true\n                                break\n                        }\n                }\n                if !existsInNew {\n                        *newTaints = append(*newTaints, oldTaint)\n                }\n        }\n        return len(oldTaints) != len(*newTaints)\n}\n\n// checkIfTaintsAlreadyExists checks if the node already has taints that we want to add and returns a string with taint keys.\nfunc checkIfTaintsAlreadyExists(oldTaints []corev1.Taint, taints []corev1.Taint) string {\n        var existingTaintList = make([]string, 0)\n        for _, taint := range taints {\n                for _, oldTaint := range oldTaints {\n                        if taint.Key == oldTaint.Key && taint.Effect == oldTaint.Effect {\n                                existingTaintList = append(existingTaintList, taint.Key)\n                        }\n                }\n        }\n        return strings.Join(existingTaintList, \",\")\n}\n\n// deleteTaintsByKey removes all the taints that have the same key to given taintKey\nfunc deleteTaintsByKey(taints []corev1.Taint, taintKey string) ([]corev1.Taint, bool) {\n        newTaints := []corev1.Taint{}\n        for i := range taints {\n                if taintKey == taints[i].Key {\n                        continue\n                }\n                newTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n        }\n        return newTaints, len(taints) != len(newTaints)\n}\n\n// deleteTaint removes all the taints that have the same key and effect to given taintToDelete.\nfunc deleteTaint(taints []corev1.Taint, taintToDelete *corev1.Taint) ([]corev1.Taint, bool) {\n        newTaints := []corev1.Taint{}\n        for i := range taints {\n                if taintToDelete.MatchTaint(&taints[i]) {\n                        continue\n                }\n                newTaints = append(newTaints, taints[i])\n        }\n        return newTaints, len(taints) != len(newTaints)\n}"

"/*\nCopyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage endpointslice\n\nimport (\n        \"context\"\n        \"fmt\"\n        \"sort\"\n        \"time\"\n\n        corev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n        discovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n        \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n        metav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n        \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n        \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n        utilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n        \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n        clientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n        corelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n        \"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n        \"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\"\n        \"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\"\n        \"k8s.io/endpointslice/trafficdist\"\n        endpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n        \"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n)\n\n// Reconciler is responsible for transforming current EndpointSlice state into\n// desired state\ntype Reconciler struct {\n        client               clientset.Interface\n        nodeLister           corelisters.NodeLister\n        maxEndpointsPerSlice int32\n        endpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\n        metricsCache         *metrics.Cache\n...\n                        // If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n                        // retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n                        // Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n                        // situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n                        // stuck forever.\n                        // On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n                        // Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n                        if !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n                                logger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n                                errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n                                continue\n                        }\n                }\n\n                // Skip pods on nodes tainted with NoScheduleNoTraffic: the node is being\n                // drained from traffic routing while keeping existing pods running.\n                if node != nil && nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(node) {\n                        logger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node has NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n                        continue\n                }\n\n                endpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n                if len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n                        desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n                }\n        }\n\n        spMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\n        totalAdded := 0\n        totalRemoved := 0\n\n...\n}\n\n// ManagedByController returns true if the controller of the provided\n// EndpointSlices is the EndpointSlice controller.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) ManagedByController(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) bool {\n        managedBy := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelManagedBy]\n        return managedBy == r.controllerName\n}\n\n// nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint returns true if the node has a taint with\n// effect NoScheduleNoTraffic, indicating that the node should be removed from\n// service EndpointSlices while keeping existing pods running.\nfunc nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(node *corev1.Node) bool {\n        for _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n                if taint.Effect == corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n                        return true\n                }\n        }\n        return false\n}"

"// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally removes the node from\n        // Service EndpointSlices to stop traffic routing to the node.\n        // Does not evict existing pods. Enforced by the scheduler and\n        // EndpointSlice controller.\n        TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n)\n\n// The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n// the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\n---\n        TaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n        // Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally removes the node from\n        // Service EndpointSlices to stop traffic routing to the node.\n        // Does not evict existing pods. Enforced by the scheduler and\n        // EndpointSlice controller.\n        TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n)\n\n// Toleration represents the toleration object that can be attached to a pod.\n// The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n// the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>."

{
  "message": "The `NoScheduleNoTraffic` taint effect implementation is complete. All 8 files have been created/modified:\n\n## Files Modified\n\n### API Type Definitions\n1. **`staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go`** \u2014 Public API: `TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"` added after `TaintEffectNoExecute` (line 3532)\n2. **`pkg/apis/core/types.go`** \u2014 Internal API: same constant added after `TaintEffectNoExecute` (line 3190)\n\n### Scheduler (prevents new pod scheduling)\n3. **`pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go`** \u2014 `DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc()` includes `NoScheduleNoTraffic` so the scheduler rejects pods on tainted nodes\n4. **`pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint_test.go`** \u2014 Test case verifying `NoScheduleNoTraffic` is blocked by the scheduler filter\n\n### EndpointSlice Controller (removes node from traffic routing)\n5. **`staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go`** \u2014 Reconciliation loop skips pods on nodes with `NoScheduleNoTraffic` taint (they are not added to EndpointSlices), plus `nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint()` helper\n\n### Validation (accepts the new effect as valid)\n6. **`pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go`** \u2014 `validateTaintEffect()` switch case and error `validValues` both include `core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic`\n7. **`pkg/util/taints/taints.go`** \u2014 `validateTaintEffect()` accepts `v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic`\n8. **`staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go`** \u2014 `validateTaintEffect()` accepts `corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic` (for `kubectl taint`)\n\n## Behavior Guarantees\n- \u2705 **Prevents scheduling**: Treated like `NoSchedule` \u2014 new pods won't be scheduled on tainted nodes\n- \u2705 **Removes from EndpointSlices**: EndpointSlice reconciler skips pods on tainted nodes, stopping traffic routing\n- \u2705 **Does NOT evict**: Not added to the `NoExecute` path \u2014 existing pods continue running undisturbed"
